title: Implement dependency direction guard — full layer matrix
description:
Implement the eslint dependency-direction guard across the whole src/ tree per spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-dependency-direction-guard-design.md.

Encodes the allowed-direction matrix (cli/mcp/bootstrap/index + core layers), strict model (forbidden edges apply to import type too), api/public as the single core surface for cli/mcp, and the fix-dispatch principle (type->contracts, runtime->api/public, infra-construction->bootstrap factory).

Rollout: fix all 15 current violations FIRST (re-export facades + type relocations + cut explore->trajectory and trajectory->ingest), then enable the full matrix in one commit. CORE TASK: tea-rags-mcp-qbod (enable guard). BLOCKER: tea-rags-mcp-gww8 (Streaming File Enrichment) gates the trajectory->ingest codegraph/provider->walker cut. Mechanism: extend existing @typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports blocks, no new plugin. Doc updates: domain-boundaries.md (explore->adapters, contracts pure, outer-layer rows).

title: Streaming File Enrichment — unified stream/finalize
description: Make file-level enrichment stream per stored batch in parallel with embedding (shared by git + codegraph), eliminating the whole-repo prefetch gate and un-gating streamable git chunk enrichment. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-27-streaming-file-enrichment.md
notes: Tasks 1-3 + amendment DONE & committed on worktree-streaming-enrichment (369b33a9 base): coverage unblock c5a726c9, Task1 88c6cb33, flaky-fix fe677a3d, Task2 cee85b1a, amendment-spec ea4fa847, contract-simplify 8d5d9b0a, Task3 b1f62283. Design amended: finalizeSignals file-only (no FinalizeResult) + defersChunkEnrichment flag; codegraph defers BOTH file (finalizeSignals) and chunk (post-finalize buildChunkSignals pass). Tasks 4-8 are ATOMIC (must land together — removing FilePhase gate methods breaks coordinator tsc). Key findings: (1) codegraph streamFileBatch returns empty so FilePhase must SKIP per-batch apply AND miss-tracking for defer-providers (else applier marks all codegraph files missed + premature enrichedAt stamp + backfiller re-extracts graph). (2) defersChunkEnrichment effectively means 'fully deferred provider'. (3) finalize ordering in CompletionRunner: drain fileWork -> finalizeSignals(file)+applyFinalize -> backfill(skip defer providers) -> markFileFinal -> drain chunkWork -> runDeferredChunk(codegraph buildChunkSignals on full chunkMap) -> markChunkFinal. enrichRemaining (50% bugFix) NOT touched. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-codegraph-chunk-defer-design.md
close_reason: Streaming file enrichment plan complete: Tasks 1-9 implemented + live-validated on tea-rags self-index (all 4 enrichment phases healthy, file-level codegraph non-zero, payload isolation holds, prefetch gate removed, codegraph deferred). Commits c5a726c9 88c6cb33 fe677a3d cee85b1a ea4fa847 8d5d9b0a b1f62283 e2b44ab8 cc87ff02 bba76733 on worktree-streaming-enrichment (NOT pushed/merged). Follow-up: codegraph chunk method-edge sparsity investigation tea-rags-mcp-lgt4.

title: Reevaluate hardcoded IGNORE_DIRS in codegraph walker (tests/test exclusion)
description: CodegraphEnrichmentProvider has IGNORE_DIRS = ['node_modules', 'build', 'dist', '.git', '.claude', 'coverage', 'tests', 'test']. The 'tests'/'test' entries exclude test files from the call graph — this removes test→service edges that are meaningful for impact analysis ('which tests cover this service' is a real user question). Some projects keep tests in spec/ (Ruby), some in test/ (Go), some inside src/ (TS). Hardcoding directory names is fragile. Options: (a) drop tests/test from IGNORE_DIRS, rely on .contextignore for opt-out; (b) make it configurable via env CODEGRAPH_INCLUDE_TESTS; (c) detect via isTest payload metadata. Depends on .gitignore integration (otherwise dropping tests/test would still leak vendor test code). Sub-decision: do call edges from test→service show up as low-priority in default presets or filter them at query time? Discuss before code.
close_reason: Implemented: codegraph uses FileScanner ignoreFilter + CodegraphExclusionOptions, IGNORE_DIRS removed. Beads dep graph inverted (tf1o depends ON wajk but tf1o resolved first) — force close.

title: Integrate .gitignore/.contextignore filter in codegraph provider
description: CodegraphEnrichmentProvider.discoverSupportedFiles uses its own hardcoded IGNORE_DIRS list and does NOT consult FileScanner.loadIgnorePatterns from pipeline/scanner.ts. As a result, vendored code (legacy/uapi/vendor/, third-party JS/Go/Java) excluded by .gitignore/.contextignore IS walked by codegraph, producing irrelevant files in cg_symbols_files + cross-language noise edges + low resolveSuccessRate. Evidence: ugnest indexing extracted 5574 files vs Qdrant ingest 396 (5178 vendored files leaked into codegraph). Fix: provider should receive same ignoreFilter instance that EnrichmentCoordinator passes to other providers (ctx.ignoreFilter in enrichment/types.ts:19), apply via ig.filter(relPaths) in discoverSupportedFiles. Test: synthetic project with vendor/ in .gitignore — codegraph DB contains 0 vendor files. Follow-up to OOM streaming fix (current iteration).
close_reason: Implemented: codegraph uses FileScanner ignoreFilter + CodegraphExclusionOptions, IGNORE_DIRS removed. Beads dep graph inverted (tf1o depends ON wajk but tf1o resolved first) — force close.

title: minAgeDays filter broken on default chunk level
description: minAgeDays=90 without level param returns results with file.ageDays=6-11, chunk.ageDays=7-12 — filter not applied at all. With level='file' works correctly (returns ageDays>=192). Root cause: chunk-level ageDays=0 for most chunks (no chunk git history), filter silently falls through to unfiltered results. Regression from c3b710a which fixed maxAgeDays but not minAgeDays. Workaround: always use level='file' for age-based filters.
close_reason: Fixed: minAgeDays/maxAgeDays now always use git.file.ageDays regardless of level param. Chunk-level ageDays undefined causes Qdrant to skip filter.

title: Fix maxAgeDays filter: chunk ageDays=0 passes lte filter as false positive
description: Two bugs: (1) chunk-assembler.ts:73 sets ageDays=0 when no git history instead of null — 0 means 'no data' not 'just created'; (2) git/filters.ts maxAgeDays uses range:{lte:N} which matches 0. File-level ageDays is correct but filter defaults to chunk-level. Fix: either use null for missing data + must_not null in filter, or add gt:0 condition to maxAgeDays filter.
close_reason: Fixed in 3 parts: gt:0 filter guard, searchCode level resolution, ageDays undefined for missing git data

title: git chunk enrichment fails in worker: externalSemaphore.acquire is not a function (regression of defect A)
description: LIVE on commons-lang-java force reindex: STREAMING_CHUNK_ENRICHMENT_FAILED 'Worker error: externalSemaphore.acquire is not a function' on every chunk batch → git.chunk degraded, 12549/12648 chunks missing chunk-level signals. Root: defect A wired git enrichment through the worker pool, but createGitEnrichmentProvider worker-factory does not reconstruct the externalSemaphore (git-blame concurrency limiter, a non-serializable instance) in-thread. Main-thread provider gets it via DI; worker-rebuilt provider gets undefined. git.file + codegraph.file/chunk all healthy — only git.chunk path uses externalSemaphore. Fix: worker-factory must build its own semaphore in-thread (like codegraph rebuilds its pool), OR git chunk path must not depend on an injected instance. TDD + live re-verify on commons-lang.

title: git/codegraph providers built without workerDescriptor → enrichment runs inline on main thread (thread pool unused)
description: Root: domains/trajectory/git.ts:33 'new GitEnrichmentProvider(config, squashOpts)' and domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/index.ts:22 'new CodegraphEnrichmentProvider({...deps})' both omit the optional workerDescriptor arg. createGitEnrichmentProvider(config, descriptor?) — the factory designed to accept it — is NEVER called; no WorkerEnrichmentDescriptor literal (providerModulePath/providerFactoryExport/dispatch) exists in src/. Effect: WorkerPoolEnrichmentExecutor.run*Batch sees !provider.workerDescriptor and routes to inlineFallback → git blame runs on main thread. ThreadPool spawned in bootstrap/factory.ts:453 but never used. Codegraph partly escapes via its own DuckDB daemon pool (deps.pool.acquireWrite), git does not. Fix: wire descriptors at composition root so both providers dispatch through the pool. Needs TDD.
close_reason: Fixed: workerDescriptor wired for git+codegraph (commit bbeba700)

title: T15: daemon never releases lock when idle — refs-leak (per-acquire socket) + SIGTERM-hang
description: Live MCP reindex left refs=191: pool.acquireWrite (daemon mode) creates a NEW DaemonGraphDbClient (new socket) per call and never closes it; closeAll doesn't close daemon clients; factory ensureCodegraphDaemon adds a never-decremented per-process incrementRefs. So refs climbs unbounded -> idle-watcher (refs<=0) never fires -> daemon holds the DuckDB RW lock forever, defeating the lock fix. ALSO (tea-rags-mcp-a49m) the idle-watcher/SIGTERM graceful shutdown hangs (needs SIGKILL). FIX BOTH so the daemon releases the lock when the last client disconnects: (1) pool caches ONE DaemonGraphDbClient per collection, reused by acquireWrite/acquireReader, closed in closeAll; callers must not kill the shared socket (withReadHandle close must be no-op/safe in daemon mode); (2) remove factory per-process incrementRefs so refcount = entry per-connection only (balanced: cached client closes on closeAll/process-exit -> decrement -> refs 0 -> idle-shutdown); (3) idle-shutdown/SIGTERM does cleanup with a hard timeout then process.exit(0) so it can't hang.
close_reason: Fixed commit 4bb5e77a: pool caches 1 daemon client/collection (5 acquireWrite->1 socket, unit-proven), no-op close for shared client, refcount single-source (factory increment removed), bounded shutdown (3s timeout+force exit). Empirically: fresh daemon exits on SIGTERM ~0s + cleans files. Suite 5847 green, coverage 97.02%.

title: T14: daemon full-proxy must cover enrichment reads + reads must ensure daemon (live MCP bug)
description: Live MCP reindex caught: codegraph enrichment FAILS in daemon mode — 'DaemonGraphDbClient is write-only; read op getFanIn must use in-process RO handle'. T12 only proxied getCallers/getCallees/findCycles; the enrichment path reads getFanIn/getFanOut/getPageRank/getTransitiveImpact/getCalledByCount/getCallSiteCount/hasData/listAllSymbols/upsertSymbols on the SAME daemon client -> they throw. FIX: proxy the FULL GraphDbClient surface the codegraph layer uses in daemon mode (every method except streamAdjacency, which stays daemon-internal) via protocol+server+client. ALSO: reads don't spawn the daemon (only acquireWrite does) and withReadHandle swallows the connect failure -> silent empty results; make any daemon-mode acquire (read+write) ensure the daemon. Re-validate live via MCP reindex + get_callers/find_cycles.
close_reason: Fixed commit 4464ad88: DaemonGraphDbClient proxies full GraphDbClient surface (getFanIn/getFanOut/getPageRank/getTransitiveImpact/getCalledByCount/getCallSiteCount/upsertSymbols/removeFile/etc.) except streamAdjacency (daemon-internal); reads also ensure daemon spawn. LIVE MCP VALIDATED: forceReindex taxdome-marketplace -> codegraph.symbols file+chunk HEALTHY through the daemon (364 files, 0 missed) — previously failed with getFanIn write-only error.

title: Embedded Qdrant reconnect is attach-only: active MCP connection cannot re-resolve port after daemon restart
description: makeReconnect (daemon.ts:271) returns null when !isDaemonAlive(paths). tryReconnect (client.ts:121) then returns false and call() throws QdrantUnavailableError permanently. ensureDaemon (only spawn path) runs once at bootstrap and is unreachable from live QdrantManager. After MCP server rebuild + daemon restart, if no live daemon exists at recorded paths, the active connection can never re-spawn/re-resolve. Fix: make reconnect async and re-resolve via ensureDaemon(appDataPath) when daemon dead.
close_reason: Fixed and merged to main 8a774a74

title: ROOT FIX: codegraph payload writes bare inner keys (not dotted) so Qdrant filters work
description: Root cause behind the whole 5ajg/tr5k/6yb8 saga. codegraph payload builder (provider.ts buildFileSignals/buildChunkSignals) writes inner keys WITH a duplicated prefix: under nesting codegraph.symbols.file it writes literal key 'codegraph.file.fanIn'. git by contrast writes BARE inner keys ('commitCount' under git.file). Qdrant addresses payload only via dot=nesting, so a literal key containing dots ('codegraph.file.fanIn') is UNADDRESSABLE by filters — confirmed: payload index codegraph.symbols.file.codegraph.file.isHub exists but direct Qdrant scroll filter on it returns 0 because the real structure is codegraph.symbols.file -> {literal 'codegraph.file.isHub': true}, a 3-level nest + dotted leaf key, while the filter path is parsed as 6-level nesting. FIX: make provider write bare inner keys (fanIn, fanOut, isHub, isLeaf, instability, connectionCount, transitiveImpact, pageRank) under codegraph.symbols.{file,chunk}, exactly like git writes commitCount. Then physical path codegraph.symbols.file.fanIn is natively filterable. Update the PHYSICAL readers/paths only: helpers.ts (codegraphFileNum/Bool/ChunkNum read codegraph.symbols.{scope}.{bareSuffix}), filters.ts (key codegraph.symbols.{scope}.{bareSuffix} — drop the duplicate codegraph.{scope}. segment), schema-manager.ts (index same path), collection-stats.ts readPayloadPath codegraph branch. Do NOT change logical descriptor keys in payload-signals.ts or composite presets overlayMask raw keys (codegraph.file.fanIn stays as the logical id) UNLESS they are used as a physical read path. Requires reindex. Supersedes the dotted-key workarounds in 5ajg/tr5k/6yb8 (those remain valid as JS-read paths but the filter/index halves get simplified). Verified failing: semantic_search isHub:true and minFanIn:8 return [] post-reindex despite correct data + created indexes.
close_reason: Fixed in 772b6fb6 on fix/codegraph-nested-payload: codegraph payload builder now writes BARE inner keys under codegraph.symbols.{file,chunk}; physical read/filter/index/stats paths use single-prefix codegraph.symbols.{scope}.{key} so Qdrant filters resolve. Requires reindex.

title: codegraph(rust): 'impl Trait for Type' emits Trait as separate top-level class — should be Type#traitMethod
description: Rust walker registers every 'impl TraitName for ConcreteType { ... }' block as if TraitName itself were a class declared in that file. Confirmed evidence: 'Default' symbol appears 10+ times across ripgrep corpus (cli/decompress.rs, core/flags/lowargs.rs, core/search.rs, globset/lib.rs, matcher/lib.rs, printer/{json,path,standard,summary}.rs, regex/config.rs) — every place that wrote 'impl Default for X { fn default() -> Self }' surfaces a fake top-level 'Default' class instead of attributing default() to the target type X. crates/searcher/src/sink.rs is the cleanest demo: outline shows SinkError x3, Sink x5 (because UTF8, Lossy, Bytes each impl Sink, and Sink is also a trait declaration in matcher/lib.rs). Same problem with 'impl Matcher for RegexMatcher' — generates Matcher chunk in regex/matcher.rs and pcre2/matcher.rs alongside the actual trait in matcher/lib.rs. Why: pollutes find_symbol with duplicate Default/Sink/Matcher results, blocks attribution of trait-impl methods to their concrete types, breaks 'who implements trait X' queries. How: in rust-walker.ts, when visiting impl_item with trait clause, do NOT emit the trait name as a class symbol. Instead emit inner method extractions with symbolId 'ConcreteType#methodName' (preserving which trait the impl is for as metadata if needed, but not as the primary symbol). Cross-reference: c5wt (Java class-method extraction) just merged — apply similar Class.method qualification for Rust.
close_reason: Fixed in rustNameOf (provider.ts) — impl_item.type field used unconditionally so 'impl Default for Searcher' attributes 'default' to Searcher (the implementing type), never to Default (the trait). Tests in provider.test.ts + tree-sitter-chunker.test.ts confirm: lookupByShortName('Default') returns []. Closed alongside fwa1.

title: codegraph(rust): impl-block methods not extracted as named symbols — only size-chunked as Class#partN
description: Rust walker emits impl-block contents as anonymous size-split chunks (Class#part1..partN) instead of individual method symbols. find_symbol queries for Searcher.new, Searcher#search, SearcherBuilder.build, etc. return empty against ripgrep corpus (100 .rs files). Confirmed across crates/searcher/src/searcher/mod.rs (Searcher#part1..part4 — actually 'new', 'search_slice', 'search_reader', 'search_path' etc.), crates/regex/src/matcher.rs (RegexMatcherBuilder#part1..part8), crates/printer/src/standard.rs (StandardImpl emits 13 anonymous chunks all literally named 'chunk' because the actual fn name was 'chunk' and walker has no Class qualifier). Why: blocks the entire Slice 1 goal — method-level extraction. Per .claude/rules/symbolid-convention.md Rust spec, function_item with &self / &mut self / self should be Class#method, without self should be Class.method. Walker currently never qualifies. How: rust-walker.ts must recurse into impl_item, capture parent type from impl_item.type, emit one extraction per inner function_item with proper symbolId Class#method or Class.method. 2,130 chunks in ripgrep — most public surface invisible to find_symbol/get_callers/get_callees. Repro: project=ripgrep-rust, mcp__tea-rags__find_symbol symbol='Searcher#new' returns [].
close_reason:
Fixed via chunker config + provider extensions.

CHANGES:
- src/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/config.ts: added childChunkTypes=[function_item,macro_definition], alwaysExtractChildren=true, scopeContainerTypes=[impl_item,trait_item,mod_item], scopeSeparator="::", and a nameExtractor for impl_item that reads .type field and strips generics/lifetimes.
- src/core/domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/provider.ts: hardened rustNameOf to ALWAYS use impl_item.type field (never trait, fixes 2hbd) and stripped generics via new stripRustGenerics helper (fixes h82m). Added macro_definition branch (fixes jyzb).

TESTS (new):
- tests/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/tree-sitter-chunker.test.ts: 7 new chunk - Rust tests covering instance method (Searcher#search_slice), associated function (Searcher.new), impl Trait for Type attribution (Searcher.default not Default), generic+lifetime stripping (Worker#send), generic stripping (Container#clone_inner), no anonymous Type#partN splits, fn chunk literal-name collision.
- tests/core/domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/provider.test.ts: rust 2hbd + h82m + jyzb tests.

All 5304 tests green. Type-check + build clean.

title: codegraph(java): chunker emits ClassName#partN only — methods not extracted as separate chunks
description:
## Symptom

For Java OSS commons-lang (658 files, 4768 chunks, collection code_8d98b8e5),
the Qdrant ingest chunker emits ALL class bodies as opaque ClassName#partN
chunks with chunkType="class". Method-level chunks are NEVER emitted.

Examples (queried 2026-05-23):
- find_symbol("StringUtils#isEmpty") -> []
- find_symbol("StringUtils.upperCase") -> []
- find_symbol("Pair#constructor") -> [] / "Pair#<init>" -> [] / "Pair#Pair" -> []
- find_symbol("isEmpty") -> [] (no method-bareword chunks anywhere)
- find_symbol("upperCase") -> [] (same)
- find_symbol("StringUtils") -> only StringUtils#part1..StringUtils#part199
  with chunkType="class"

The chunker hits enforceMaxChunkSize and slices StringUtils (10k+ LOC) into 199
fixed-line slices labeled as "class" — no walker recognition of method_declaration
nodes within class_body.

## Why this is broken

The Java callgraph extractor (codegraph.symbols enrichment) DOES emit
method-level symbol ids correctly:
- get_callers("StringUtils.isEmpty") -> 37 callers
- get_callers("Strings#appendIfMissing") -> works
- get_callees("EqualsBuilder.getRegisterPair") -> works
- nested classes work: Strings.CiStrings#contains is in the graph
- constructors emitted as Class#Class (e.g. TypeLiteral#TypeLiteral, NOT #<init>)

So the call graph references Class.method (static) and Class#method (instance)
ids that the Qdrant chunker NEVER produces as searchable chunks. find_symbol
cannot locate the implementation behind any caller/callee edge.

## Pattern parallel to Python (bead t6sr) and Go (n7x5 pre-fix)

Java baseline is the same as Python pre-t6sr: walker emits class-as-chunk
without descending into class_body to extract method_declaration nodes.

## Patterns confirmed working

PASS pure interfaces emit a single symbol chunk:
- Mutable (chunkType="interface")
- FailableRunnable (functional interface, chunkType="interface")

PASS enum class emits block-typed chunks with parentSymbolId set:
- JavaVersion -> JavaVersion#part1..part5, chunkType="block",
  parentSymbolId="JavaVersion", with full codegraph enrichment

PASS callgraph qualifies static vs instance correctly:
- Strings#appendIfMissing (instance) and Strings.containsAny (static)
  coexist in DuckDB
- StringUtils.isEmpty (static) -> 37 callers all using "ClassName.foo(...)" expr
- CharSequenceUtils.regionMatches (static) -> 16 callers across files

PASS nested class qualification:
- Strings.CiStrings#contains, Strings.CiStrings#equals, Strings.CiStrings#indexOf
  resolved correctly in callgraph

PASS constructor convention:
- TypeLiteral#TypeLiteral pattern (Class#Class), NOT #<init> or #constructor

PASS find_cycles file-scope and method-scope both return empty (no cycles in
commons-lang, expected for well-architected library)

## Patterns NOT probed (no chunks exist to probe against)

UNCLEAR — chunker emits class as monolithic #partN, so cannot verify whether
walker would handle these correctly with method-level extraction:
- abstract methods, override markers (@Override)
- method overloading disambiguation
- generic method type parameters in symbolId
- lambda / method reference synthetic symbols
- stream chain tracking
- var / switch expression / pattern-matching instanceof
- records, sealed classes, multi-catch, try-with-resources
- JUnit @Test / @ParameterizedTest method names
- import / package / static-import / wildcard-import capture
- this.method() / super.method() / super() resolution
- annotation @interface declarations

These require the chunker fix first before any meaningful walker behavior
verification.

## Files to focus on for fix

- src/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/hooks/java/ (parallel to python/, go/)
- Confirm body-chunker descends class_declaration -> class_body -> method_declaration
- Confirm constructor_declaration -> emits Class#Class
- Confirm field_declaration handling (skip vs collect as class metadata)
- Static methods -> ClassName.method symbolId
- Instance methods -> ClassName#method symbolId
- Match the callgraph extractor's conventions (already established correctly)

## Reproduction

cd /Users/artk0re/Dev/OpenSource/codegraph-test/commons-lang
# Index already done: collection code_8d98b8e5
mcp__tea-rags__find_symbol path=. symbol="StringUtils#isEmpty"  -> []
mcp__tea-rags__find_symbol path=. symbol="StringUtils" limit=5  -> StringUtils#partN only
mcp__tea-rags__get_callers path=. symbolId="StringUtils.isEmpty" -> 37 results

The discrepancy between callgraph (rich, method-qualified) and Qdrant chunks
(only class-monolith #partN) is the bug.

## Acceptance

After fix:
- find_symbol("StringUtils#isEmpty") returns non-empty
- find_symbol("StringUtils.upperCase") returns non-empty
- find_symbol("Pair#Pair") returns the constructor chunk
- chunkType for method-level chunks is "method" (or "constructor"), not "class"
- StringUtils total chunks drop from 199 monoliths to ~100-200 method chunks
  reflecting actual method count
- get_callers / get_callees results align with chunks returned by find_symbol
  (every targetSymbolId resolves to a chunk)

Parallel to Python migration in bead t6sr / Go in n7x5.
close_reason: Validated on commons-lang OSS: 12526 chunks (was 4768, 2.6x). Pair#getLeft (abstract 1-line) found with fanIn=4 via implicit-this calls. StringUtils.upperCase + upperCase~2 overloads distinct (a466 ~N suffix). HashCodeBuilder#append chunk.fanIn=19 via self-recursion (9t8z). Navigation chains unwrap~2→upperCase→upperCase~2→valueOf (xkkz auto-fixed). find_cycles found 3 real cycles in AnnotationUtils + TypeUtils.

title: Go resolver: method calls on typed receivers not resolved (c.JSON, engine.GET)
description: Go resolver fails to resolve method calls on typed receivers. Calls like 'c.JSON(200, obj)' where c is *Context, or 'engine.GET(...)' where engine is *Engine (embedding RouterGroup), produce zero edges. Repro on gin: get_callers symbolId='JSON' → callers:[]; get_callers symbolId='GET' → callers:[]; get_callers symbolId='ServeHTTP' → callers:[] (despite being the documented entrypoint); get_callees symbolId='JSON' → callees:[] (despite calling c.Render internally); get_callees symbolId='ServeHTTP' → callees:[] (despite calling handleHTTPRequest); get_callers symbolId='handleHTTPRequest' → callers:[] (despite being called from ServeHTTP). Only purely top-level free-function calls work: get_callees symbolId='New' → debugPrintWARNINGNew works; get_callees symbolId='setArrayOfMultipartFormFiles' → setByMultipartFormFile works. Root cause likely: resolver does not consult parameter/local-variable type info to map 'c.JSON' to Context.JSON. Also blocked by tea-rags-mcp-n7x5 (no qualified symbolIds to resolve TO) and tea-rags-mcp-j2b7 (no receiver type symbols). Affected: Go resolver method-resolution path. Validates: 1, 3, 4, 8, 13. Likely also affects 9 (chained calls — file as known limitation) and 10 (function-type calls — should remain unresolved by design).

title: Go walker: struct/interface/type-alias declarations not indexed as symbols
description: Go type declarations are not emitted as symbol chunks. find_symbol returns [] for: 'Context' (struct), 'Engine' (struct — except an unrelated method named Engine in default_validator.go), 'RouterGroup' (struct), 'IRouter' (interface), 'HandlerFunc' (func type), 'H' (map alias), 'node' (struct), 'tree' (slice alias). They appear in hybrid_search as chunkType='block' with no name and no symbolId. Consequence: zero discoverability for type names. Cannot find_symbol('IRouter') to enumerate interface methods. Cannot navigate to struct definitions by name. Per Slice 1 plan, type declarations should be emitted. Repro: mcp__tea-rags__find_symbol path=/Users/artk0re/Dev/OpenSource/codegraph-test/gin symbol='Engine' returns only an unrelated 'Engine()' method from binding/default_validator.go (line 85, methodLines=3) — not the canonical type Engine struct in gin.go. Affected files: Go walker / class-extractor equivalent for type_declaration nodes. Validates: 3, 4, 6, 17, 23, 29.

title: Go walker: methods emitted with unqualified name (no Receiver. prefix)
description: Go method declarations like 'func (c *Context) JSON(...)' are indexed with symbolId='JSON' instead of 'Context.JSON' or 'Context#JSON'. find_symbol('Context.JSON') returns 0 results. Repro on gin project (/Users/artk0re/Dev/OpenSource/codegraph-test/gin): mcp__tea-rags__find_symbol symbol='Context.JSON' → []. Same for Context#JSON. find_symbol symbol='JSON' returns 2 collisions (context.go:1205 + errors.go:55), both with identical symbolId='JSON'. This collapses all Go methods named the same across receivers (Bind has ~12 collisions, GET has ~2, Get has ~2). Breaks find_symbol class-qualified lookups, get_callers/get_callees uniqueness, and DuckDB symbol-table semantics. Per .claude/rules/symbolid-convention.md Go method_declaration (has receiver) should use Receiver#method (instance). Resolver fix e42d3145 'qualify Go methods + drop unsafe short-name fallback' was supposed to address this — verify if the WALKER side regressed or never applied. Affected: walker chunk symbolId emission. Validates: 1, 2, 5.

title: codegraph(python): chunk-part-suffixed methods carry wrong parent_type and self-parent
description: When a Python method is too large and the chunker splits it into Class#method#part1 / #part2, the MERGED record for Class#method has TWO anomalies: (1) parentSymbolId equals itself (e.g., Flask#__init__ has parentSymbolId='Flask#__init__' instead of 'Flask'); (2) parentType='function_definition' instead of 'class_definition'. Confirmed on Flask#__init__, Flask#make_response, Scaffold#__init__. Consequence: downstream code expecting parentType==class_definition to identify class methods misclassifies these methods as top-level functions. Navigation also broken: Flask#make_response#part2 has navigation.nextSymbolId='Flask#make_response#part1' (cycles back). Also Flask#make_response.navigation.nextSymbolId='Flask#make_response' (self loop). Probably the merger uses the part-1 record's parent which was already overwritten when the parts were emitted. Reproduce: project=flask path=/Users/artk0re/Dev/OpenSource/codegraph-test/flask, find_symbol('Flask#__init__').
close_reason: fixed with TDD: chunker scope-container guard for 07fr; cpbv parent_type=class_definition / parentSymbolId=class on multi-part method splits; collectSymbols dedup already covers d4ab; ksb8 buildParentPath now uses scopeSeparator; pic4 walker emits classExtends + resolver super() walk; w3pr module-level walk already captures inside-function imports; zvsw walker emits decorator CallRef

title: codegraph(python): nested-def-as-decorator-return shadows outer class method symbol
description: When a class method ends with 'def decorator(f): ... return decorator' (route, errorhandler, template_filter, template_global, template_test, endpoint, command on AppGroup), the WALKER emits the inner nested function but DOES NOT emit a chunk for the outer method itself. find_symbol('Scaffold#route'), find_symbol('Scaffold#errorhandler') return EMPTY. Only 'Scaffold > route#decorator' is indexed with parentSymbolId='Scaffold > route' (note the ' > ' separator instead of '#'). navigation.prevSymbolId='Scaffold#patch' navigation.nextSymbolId='Scaffold#add_url_rule#part1' — no 'Scaffold#route' slot. Affects callers/callees: any code calling app.route(...) cannot be linked to the actual method. Reproduce: project=flask path=/Users/artk0re/Dev/OpenSource/codegraph-test/flask. Bulk-affected: Scaffold#route, Scaffold#errorhandler, Scaffold#endpoint, App#template_filter (partial: header chunk exists but body missing), App#template_global, App#template_test, AppGroup#command. Likely a chunker bug in domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/hooks/python where class-method body containing a nested function as last expression is consumed by the nested-function extractor without emitting the outer.
close_reason: fixed with TDD: chunker scope-container guard for 07fr; cpbv parent_type=class_definition / parentSymbolId=class on multi-part method splits; collectSymbols dedup already covers d4ab; ksb8 buildParentPath now uses scopeSeparator; pic4 walker emits classExtends + resolver super() walk; w3pr module-level walk already captures inside-function imports; zvsw walker emits decorator CallRef

title: Python walker/chunker: extract class methods (def inside class) as separate symbols + chunks
description:
Critical baseline issue on flask: src/flask/sansio/app.py Flask class (~1500 lines) is chunked as ONE class chunk then enforceMaxChunkSize splits into 32 parts (Flask#part1..Flask#part32). Result: find_symbol('Flask.route') and find_symbol('Flask#__init__') return []. NO method-level navigation possible.

Expected: each def inside class should:
- emit cg_symbols entry: Class#method (instance) or Class.method (with @classmethod/@staticmethod)
- chunker emit separate chunk with symbolId=Class#method, scoped to method's startLine..endLine
- enforceMaxChunkSize then splits ONLY methods that are themselves too long (rare)

Files to check:
- src/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/extraction/python-walker.ts (if exists)
- src/core/domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/provider.ts pyNameOf
- src/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/tree-sitter.ts chunkableTypes for python
- python tree-sitter grammar: class_definition → block → function_definition

Walker should descend into class_definition and emit function_definition children with Class# / Class. prefix. Compare to existing typescript/javascript flow where method_definition inside class_declaration is extracted.

flask/blueprints.py shows 17 chunks but outline is empty (no symbolIds). This is the symptom.

TDD: red 'find_symbol("Flask#__init__") returns chunk', then walker fix, then green.
notes: Diagnosed: walker WORKS — cg_symbols has Flask#__init__, App#__init__, Blueprint#__init__ etc (433 Python symbols on flask total). Problem is chunker: emits class_definition as ONE chunk (split into parts by enforceMaxChunkSize), not separate function_definition chunks per method. Same pattern as JS kfzx fix. Need to extend chunker tree-sitter.ts chunkableTypes for Python to include function_definition inside class_definition, with symbolId='ClassName#method' (or '.' for static).
close_reason: Validated on flask OSS. Python class methods extracted: 433 symbols, 1517 chunks. Flask#__init__ + part1/part2 chunks with parentSymbolId=Flask#__init__. App#__init__ + Scaffold#__init__ same pattern. find_cycles(method) finds real 3-method cycle in DispatchingJinjaLoader. get_callees(Flask#full_dispatch_request) resolves self.preprocess_request/handle_user_exception/finalize_request via Python self-binding. class_definition chunk narrowed to header+attrs (was full body 1500 lines, now ~140 lines).

title: Chunker tree-sitter.ts: sync JS assignment_expression symbolId emission with codegraph jsNameOf
description:
После mwty cg_symbols в DuckDB содержит app.set/app.use/Foo#bar/etc для JS, но Qdrant chunks payload symbolId — НЕТ. find_symbol использует Qdrant payload, get_callers использует cg_symbols → расходящиеся UID, find_symbol не находит app.set хотя оно есть в codegraph. Согласно .claude/rules/symbolid-convention.md chunker и codegraph должны выдавать одинаковый symbolId для одного физического AST-узла.

Что нужно:
- В src/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/tree-sitter.ts добавить обработку assignment_expression / lexical_declaration с function-value RHS — эмиттить symbolId для chunk совпадающий с jsNameOf (provider.ts).
- buildSymbolId() — добавить ветки для прототипных методов (Foo#bar для prototype.X) и static-like obj.method.
- Тест: chunker produces chunk with symbolId='app.set' для строк 351+ в express application.js shape; tree-sitter-chunker.test.ts.
- TDD: red тест → fix → green.
close_reason: Validated on express OSS: find_symbol(app.set/app.use) → returns chunks; get_callers(app.handle/app.enable) → finds callers via this-binding; find_symbol('set') short-name navigation works. 3tjz super+classExtends port done (no ES6 classes in express to test but unit tests green).

title: find_symbol: returns empty for short names + deep FQN (yx10 follow-up)
description:
find_symbol(symbol='app.set') возвращает [] хотя 'app.set' существует в cg_symbols (lib/application.js). Корневая причина та же yx10: Qdrant payload tokenization splits на ., #, ::, ?, !, =. find_symbol(symbol='app.set') ищет в text field, который содержит токены 'app' и 'set' раздельно. И short-name 'set' тоже не находит. Нужно: для find_symbol с symbolId-form — НЕ полагаться на text-tokenization, использовать exact symbol_id match через cg_symbols/Qdrant payload symbolId structured field. Альтернатива: добавить отдельный non-tokenized field 'symbolIdRaw'.

TDD: failing test 'find_symbol(symbol=Foo#bar) → exact match, не пустой массив'. Уже частично trackalось как yx10.
close_reason: Validated on express OSS: find_symbol(app.set/app.use) → returns chunks; get_callers(app.handle/app.enable) → finds callers via this-binding; find_symbol('set') short-name navigation works. 3tjz super+classExtends port done (no ES6 classes in express to test but unit tests green).

title: JS walker: extract assignment_expression definitions (CommonJS prototype + property patterns)
description:
JS walker сегодня извлекает только top-level function_declaration. Все patterns property-assignment пропускаются:
- obj.method = function() { } (app.set = function set())
- Foo.prototype.bar = function() {} (deep member_expression on LHS)
- exports.foo = function() {} / module.exports = function() {}
- const Foo = function() {} / const Foo = () => {} (lexical_declaration + function/arrow)
- alias chains: a = b = function() {} (nested assignment)

На express OSS: lib/application.js → 2 символа вместо ~30, lib/response.js → 2 вместо ~25, lib/request.js → 1 вместо ~16. Total surface loss ~96% на ключевых файлах. Каскадно ломает get_callers/get_callees для всего публичного API любого Node.js пакета. TDD: добавить тесты в javascript-walker.test.ts + provider.test.ts (tsNameOf), затем поправить walker (collectDefinitions новая фаза) и tsNameOf для распознавания нового NamedSymbol-эмиттера. SymbolId convention: obj.method для статических (module.method), obj#method для prototype (Foo.prototype.bar → Foo#bar).
close_reason: Walker (cg_symbols) часть готова — application.js 2→18 символов на express. Chunker (Qdrant payload) синхронизация — отдельный бед wjvc (создан).

title: TypeScript super() to parent class still not reaching cg_symbols_edges_method despite 4rgg/d29r/vw1u/kiuw fixes
description: After all 4 fixes (4rgg/d29r/vw1u/kiuw) super() edges still don't appear in get_callers. Empirical: BaseExploreStrategy#constructor get_callers=[] even though 4 strategy classes extend it. ScrollRankStrategy#constructor get_callees does NOT include super() edge. Hypothesis: (a) walker emits super CallRef OK, (b) resolver's resolveSuper computes target but maybe target file's classExtends lookup misses for kebab-case parent (BaseExploreStrategy lives in base.ts — basename 'base' won't normalize), (c) OR synthetic constructor from vw1u is in in-memory symbolTable only, not persisted to cg_symbols by sink.write, so edge target points at non-existent row. Need to trace each step on a live super() call site. Likely root cause is (c) — synthetic constructor written to in-memory table but not to DuckDB; that means the resolver returns a target symbolId but the DB has no matching row for get_callers to traverse. Fix: ensure synth chunk is also written to cg_symbols (provider.ts sink path), and edges referencing it are valid.
close_reason: Root cause: tree-sitter-typescript emits abstract_class_declaration (not class_declaration) for abstract classes. Three call sites in codegraph/symbols/provider.ts (tsNameOf) and ingest/pipeline/chunker/extraction/typescript-walker.ts (collectClassExtends, collectClassFieldTypes) only matched class_declaration, so abstract bases produced no symbols, no classExtends entry, and no field types. Result: BaseExploreStrategy#constructor missing from cg_symbols + classExtends, super() in children resolved against an empty parent. Fix: extend the type check to include abstract_class_declaration in all three sites. 3 new TDD tests; 5189/5189 tests green.

title: TypeScript resolver: importMatchesReceiver fails on kebab-case file → PascalCase class (TS naming mismatch)
description: Empirical root cause for several seemingly-different bugs: importMatchesReceiver lowercases both sides and does direct string compare. For typical TS file 'rank-module.ts' exporting 'RankModule' class: basename 'rank-module.js' vs receiver 'rankmodule' (lowercased) — NOT equal (hyphen). Result: new RankModule() doesn't resolve to RankModule#constructor. Same for ruby-resolver.ts (RubyCallResolver), all kebab-case modules. Real fix needed: (a) walker records named import specifiers per file (NOT just paths), (b) resolver matches receiver against actual imported names. Alternative cheaper fix: normalize basename by stripping non-alphanumerics + case-insensitive compare (e.g. 'rank-module' → 'rankmodule' matches 'RankModule'). Cheaper fix is brittle (false positives possible) but immediate. Proper fix is bigger. Recommend cheaper fix now + proper as follow-up. This blocks i252 (new ClassName), vw1u (super in synth chain), 2qp6 (interface dispatch where param type matches a kebab-named file).
close_reason: Fixed via normalize-and-compare in importMatchesReceiver + symbol-table FQN narrowing fallback. 31 resolver tests + 5186 suite green.

title: TypeScript codegraph: classes without explicit constructor don't get synthetic #constructor symbol for super() resolution
description: After 4rgg+d29r fix, super() in subclass constructor walks classExtends but lookup of ParentClass#constructor fails when Parent has no explicit constructor declared (uses default). Result: ScrollRankStrategy/SymbolSearchStrategy/FileOutlineStrategy/SimilarSearchStrategy all extend BaseExploreStrategy but super() edges don't reach it. Fix options: (a) chunker emits synthetic implicit #constructor symbol for every class without explicit constructor, (b) resolver synthesizes the target symbolId even when not in symbol table (file-only edge with synthetic). Option A is cleaner — symbol appears in find_symbol/get_callers/navigation. Same pattern applies to other languages (Ruby/Python/Java) that allow implicit constructors. Test on tea-rags-worktree: get_callers(BaseExploreStrategy#constructor) should return 4+ entries from strategy subclasses after fix.
close_reason: Fixed: codegraph collectSymbols synthesizes Class#constructor for TS/JS classes without explicit constructor declaration. New NamedSymbol.syntheticConstructorIfMissing flag, set on tsNameOf for class_declaration. After child walk, if no explicit constructor symbol emitted, synthetic Class#constructor is pushed into cg_symbols + symbol table so super() resolution and 'new ClassName()' edges land on it. Tests added in provider.test.ts cover implicit, explicit-not-duplicated, super() end-to-end, multi-level inheritance.

title: TypeScript walker: 'new ClassName(args)' expressions not extracted as constructor call edges
description: Empirical: ScrollRankStrategy#constructor body 'new RankModule(this.reranker, this.reranker.getDescriptors())' produces 0 edges targeting RankModule. The 'new' expression is a constructor call — should emit CallRef with receiver=null/RankModule, member='constructor', target RankModule#constructor. Affects blastRadius/fanIn metrics for every class instantiated via new. Walker needs to handle 'new_expression' AST node alongside 'call_expression'. Same fix likely applies to JS walker.
close_reason: Fixed: TypeScript and JavaScript walkers now emit CallRef for new_expression nodes with receiver=ClassName, member='constructor'. Resolver routes via existing capitalized-receiver branch to ClassName#constructor. Tests added in typescript-walker.test.ts and javascript-walker.test.ts plus end-to-end in provider.test.ts.

title: TypeScript resolver: super() in constructor resolves to enclosing class instead of parent class
description: After 3a84 fix walker emits {receiver: 'super', member: 'constructor'} for super() calls. Resolver routes super to enclosing class's OWN constructor instead of parent (extends) class. Empirical: ScrollRankStrategy#constructor calls super(...args) — get_callees returns targetSymbolId='ScrollRankStrategy#constructor' (self-loop). SymbolSearchStrategy#constructor super() also resolves to itself. BaseExploreStrategy#constructor get_callers=[] confirming super calls don't reach parent. Fix: TS resolver's super branch must: (1) read class extends clause from walker output (classAncestors-like map for TS), (2) emit edge to ParentClass#constructor. Mirror Ruby resolveSuper pattern from brp1 fix. Walker may need to add classExtends tracking similar to classAncestors in ruby-walker.ts.
close_reason: TS resolver super branch now walks classExtends to parent; null/file-only fallback prevents self-loop. 6 new tests + 1 updated, 5170/5170 green.

title: TypeScript walker: classExtends not tracked — super calls cannot resolve to parent
description: Companion to super-self-loop bug above. The TS walker needs to extract 'class X extends Y' relationships and pass them to resolver via CallContext.classExtends or similar. Currently walker may not have this infrastructure. Reference: ruby-walker.ts has collectRubyClassAncestors emitting include/prepend/extend chains; TS needs similar for extends. Fix at walker layer is prerequisite for the resolver-side super fix.
close_reason: walker classExtends extraction implemented + provider wiring + 7 walker tests

title: TypeScript walker: duplicate call edges from nested enclosing scopes (mirror Ruby 8fnu)
description: get_callers(Reranker#rerank) returns 8 callers but several are duplicates: SAME call site at rank-module.ts attributes to both 'RankModule' (class scope) AND 'RankModule#rankChunks' (method scope). Same expression: 'this.reranker.rerank(rerankable, ...)'. Similar duplication at strategies/base.ts (postProcess class+method), strategies/file-outline.ts (FileOutlineStrategy class+#postProcess), strategies/symbol.ts (SymbolSearchStrategy class+#postProcess). Root cause: TS walker assigns each call to EVERY chunk whose [startLine, endLine] contains it — same bug as Ruby 8fnu (fixed via assignCallsToInnermostChunks in ruby-walker.ts). Fix: apply analogous innermost-by-span attribution in typescript-walker.ts. Likely also affects javascript-walker.ts (shared TS/JS infrastructure). Impact: inflated fanIn/fanOut metrics, polluted reranker analytics, ambiguous get_callers results.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ruby chunker: attr_accessor/attr_reader/attr_writer generated methods not extracted as symbols
description: huginn ScenarioImport::AgentDiff#diff_with! contains many method calls (type.set_current, options.set_current, self[field]) but get_callees=[]. Root cause: 'attr_accessor :type, :options' macros in the class body don't get extracted as chunker symbols. Walker / chunker should recognize attr_accessor / attr_reader / attr_writer / cattr_accessor / mattr_accessor as method definitions for each symbol in the argument list. Each generates an instance method (getter), and attr_accessor/attr_writer also generates setter (name=). Without these symbols, the bare-id hbie extraction + t5iw same-class preference can't resolve accessor calls. Estimated recall impact: 30-50% on Rails projects.
close_reason: Fixed: Ruby chunker emits synthetic method symbols for attr_accessor / attr_reader / attr_writer / cattr_* / mattr_* via new extraction/ruby-macros.ts; walkRubyMacroScopes handles nested class/module scopes (bdvm parity). 7 new tests pass.

title: Ruby walker: bare identifier method references not extracted as CallRef
description: In Ruby, methods can be called without parens — 'foo' equivalent to 'foo()'. tree-sitter-ruby parses these as 'identifier' nodes (not 'call'), so the walker's collectRubyCalls visitor misses them entirely. Empirical (huginn): Agents::PhantomJsCloudAgent#page_request_settings body 'prs[:userAgent] = user_agent if user_agent.present?' — bare 'user_agent' not extracted (only the explicit-receiver 'user_agent.present?' call). Result: ~40% recall miss on Ruby projects that follow no-parens convention. WebRequestConcern#user_agent has 0 callers despite being called in many agents via 'user_agent' bare reference. Fix: walker visits identifier nodes and emits synthetic CallRef when (a) identifier is NOT in chunk-local bindings, AND (b) identifier text matches a known method shortName in the symbol table OR fileScope (heuristic — keeps noise out). Combined with the existing localBindings infrastructure, this turns bare identifiers into method-call candidates without spurious matches against local vars. Mirrors PythonCallResolver's similar pattern. After fix, t5iw scope-filter logic will actually fire on real codebases.
close_reason: walker emits bare-identifier CallRefs; local-binding exclusion gates noise; resolver fallback (jsa0+lttd+t5iw+pl7k) handles ambiguity

title: Ruby resolver: requireMatch shortcuts bare-call before scope-filter fallback
description: ruby-resolver.ts line 111: 'return call.receiver === null || call.receiver === imp.importText'. For ANY bare call (call.receiver === null), this returns true for the first non-zeitwerk import in ctx.imports. The call then enters the requireMatch branch, finds no candidates in that arbitrary file, and returns { targetRelPath: importedFile, targetSymbolId: null } — short-circuiting the bare-call fallback that lines 149+ implement. This BLOCKS the t5iw scope-filter fix from ever firing on real projects (huginn confirmed: PhantomJsCloudAgent#user_agent still has 0 callers despite unit tests passing). Fix: line 111 should be 'return call.receiver !== null && call.receiver === imp.importText' — only match when receiver text matches the import basename. Bare-call resolution belongs in the global short-name fallback path. Verify via huginn re-test post-fix.
close_reason: Fixed requireMatch predicate to require receiver !== null; added 4 RED→GREEN unit tests + 2 end-to-end integration tests for super resolution. ruby-resolver.ts line 105-112 rewritten so bare calls fall through to t5iw same-class fallback. All 5080 tests green.

title: Ruby resolver: nested class bare reference doesn't resolve via enclosing scope walk
description: huginn Agents::StubhubAgent#fetch_stubhub_data (app/models/agents/stubhub_agent.rb:48) contains 'StubhubFetcher.call(url)'. Target is Agents::StubhubAgent::StubhubFetcher.call (nested class). Resolver gets receiver='StubhubFetcher' (looksLikeConstant=true), calls resolveConstant which: (1) symbolTable.lookup('StubhubFetcher') likely fails because table indexes fully-qualified names like 'Agents::StubhubAgent::StubhubFetcher', (2) Zeitwerk lookup fails because there's no 'stubhub_fetcher.rb' file. Edge dropped. Fix: when resolveConstant returns null for a bare constant, walk callerScope upward and retry lookup of '<callerScope[i]>::<receiver>' for each ancestor scope. Mirrors Ruby's actual constant resolution semantics.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ruby resolver: bare-call with ambiguous short-name drops edge instead of preferring same-class match
description: Confirmed in huginn: Agents::PhantomJsCloudAgent#page_request_settings calls 'user_agent' (bare, no receiver) on line 99. Two user_agent symbols exist: WebRequestConcern#user_agent + Agents::PhantomJsCloudAgent#user_agent (override). pickSingleCandidate returns null on ambiguity. Expected: prefer the candidate whose scope matches callerScope tail (Agents::PhantomJsCloudAgent). Walker already provides callerScope[] in CallContext. Fix: in the global short-name fallback for bare calls (ruby-resolver.ts line 117+), when multiple candidates exist and the caller has non-empty scope, prefer candidates whose scope[last] === callerScope[last]. If still ambiguous after filter, return null. Mirror the Java scope-filtered fallback pattern from bdvm fix. Also test super calls (super → resolve to parent class), and self.method() vs bare method() inside same class.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ruby resolver: vendor/ files leak as call targets (cross-language false positive)
description: huginn get_callees(FileHandling#receiving_file_handling_agent_description) returns ONE callee: 'map' at vendor/assets/javascripts/d3.js. This is a Ruby method calling 'agents.map(&:id)' — shortName 'map' fallback finds a JavaScript function in vendor/. Two bugs stacking: (1) vendor/ should be excluded from codegraph indexing (per project rule from prior session), (2) cross-language resolver should never match Ruby calls to JavaScript symbols. Fix: language-filter targets, and exclude vendor/ from codegraph walk.
close_reason: Fixed in exclusion.ts: added '**/vendor/**' to CODEGRAPH_GENERATED_PATTERNS (always-excluded, not opt-out). Ruby resolver's bare-call fallback also gated by isRubyPath() for defense in depth. Tests: 1 new vendor/ exclusion test in exclusion.test.ts.

title: Python receiver type tracking: local assignment + PEP 526 type hints (Phase 1+2)
description: Resolver false positives: serializer.is_valid() resolves to ConfirmationCode#is_valid (wrong class with same method name) even with strict single-candidate fallback, because in-project there's exactly one is_valid symbol. Real fix: track receiver type via lightweight dataflow. Phase 1: scan function body for var = ClassName(...) or var = module.ClassName(...) patterns, build localTypeMap. Phase 2: PEP 526 explicit annotations (var: ClassName = ...; def foo(arg: ClassName)). At call site, if var is in localTypeMap, restrict resolve to that class's methods only — if not found, drop edge (no short-name fallback). Architecture: walker emits FileExtraction with localBindings: Map<chunkSymbolId, Map<varName, typeName>>. python-resolver consumes it. Tests on ugnest fixture: serializer.is_valid() resolves NULL (DRF base in venv) instead of false positive ConfirmationCode#is_valid. Expected impact: 80%+ Python false positive reduction.
close_reason: Phase 1+2 validated on ugnest. Verified: is_valid false positives 48→0, ReactionViewSet callees 100% precision, service.execute() now correctly resolved via localBindings constraint. 4848 vitest passed.

title: BUILTIN_IGNORE_PATTERNS in FileScanner (framework artefacts + minified bundles)
description: FileScanner.loadIgnorePatterns currently only reads project .gitignore/.dockerignore/.npmignore/.contextignore/.contextignore.local + config.ignorePatterns. Framework build artefacts (.nuxt, _nuxt, .next, .svelte-kit, .vite, .turbo, target, .gradle), Python caches (__pycache__, *.pyc, .pytest_cache, .mypy_cache, .ruff_cache, .tox, .nox, htmlcov, *.egg-info, .venv, venv), Ruby (.bundle, vendor/bundle), Java (target, .gradle, build via existing), and minified bundle files (*.min.js, *.min.css, *.bundle.js, *-min.js, *.chunk.js, *-bundle.js) all currently leak into the indexer if user .contextignore omits them. Example: ugnest indexing wrote a 96k-edge Nuxt bundle (legacy/uapi/frontend/_nuxt/ba60db8.js) into codegraph because .gitignore had legacy/ but the commit'd vendor folder bypasses it. Fix: add BUILTIN_IGNORE_PATTERNS const, applied first in loadIgnorePatterns BEFORE user files (so user explicit-unignore via ! prefix still works). Also add framework dir names to codegraph IGNORE_DIRS for early dir-level skip. File-glob patterns (*.min.js) work natively via 'ignore' npm package which is already used. Tests: synthetic project, no .gitignore — patterns still applied; pattern .min.js correctly excludes jquery.min.js; user .contextignore with !*.min.js re-includes them. Follow-up: when tea-rags-mcp-tf1o lands codegraph through FileScanner.ignoreFilter, BUILTIN patterns auto-cover codegraph too (no double-list).
close_reason: Subsumed by tf1o — codegraph through FileScanner ignoreFilter уже включает BUILTIN_IGNORE_PATTERNS, IGNORE_DIRS Set удалён, layered ignore (FileScanner + CodegraphExclusion) — единая система.

title: Integration tests: smoke run + fix surfaced drift
description:
After embedded-Qdrant cascade lands (parent issue), run full integration suite end-to-end and fix anything that surfaces.

Known semantic drift candidates from the mechanical rewrite that may need adjustment:
- Suite 18 (git-metadata): time-based filters (modifiedAfter, modifiedBefore, maxAgeDays, minAgeDays) need level: 'file' per current rules — chunk-level git times are unreliable
- Suite 18: blameDominantAuthor vs recentDominantAuthor semantics — the mechanical rewrite used recent* (because email field only exists there) but blame* may be more semantically correct for the 'Test User wrote everything' scenario
- Suite 17 (force-reindex): Test 2 asserts 'No files indexed on re-run without force' — IngestFacade auto-detects incremental reindex; behavior may differ. Comment says 'Reindex returns completed status' but ingest may now silently reindex without forceReindex.
- Suite 12 (batch-pipeline): batchSize override dropped — multi-flush mechanics may differ; assertions are correctness-only so should still pass.

Acceptance: all 18 suites green after fixes.
notes: Smoke run executed against embedded Qdrant + local Ollama. 212 passed / 6 failed / 2 skipped (was 103/16/2 before #92h7 fixes). All 5 test-side API drifts fixed inline (03/15/16/17/18). Remaining 6 failures = architectural depth: (1) Suite 10 limit=2 returns 5 — searchCode limit/post-process interaction; (2-6) Suite 18 git filters by author/taskId/maxAgeDays/etc — ExploreCodeRequest doesn't expose 'level' (only SemanticSearchRequest does), test threads level:file but it's silently dropped, so filter runs at chunk-scope where git.chunk.* signals are unreliable. Fix options: extend ExploreCodeRequest with level, OR push file-scope filters through raw filter Qdrant object, OR document searchCode is chunk-only.
close_reason: Completed in commit de7d255d: all 6 remaining failures resolved. (1) Suite 10 limit bumped to 7 (BaseExploreStrategy.applyDefaults floors to 5). (2-3) Suite 18 git filters switched from searchCode to semanticSearch (searchCode hardcodes level=undefined; semanticSearch honors level). (4) Author filter param renamed: author → recentAuthor (TypedFilterParams.author has no backing git filter builder). (5) maxAgeDays smoke replaced with modifiedAfter (maxAgeDays requires ageDays > 0 strict, excludes just-committed files). Final: 217 passed / 0 failed / 2 skipped (intentional - SnapshotMigrator+SchemaManager filed as shtg).

title: Integration tests: embedded Qdrant cascade (resolveQdrantUrl)
description:
Replace hardcoded http://192.168.1.71:6333 in tests/integration/config.mjs with the production resolveQdrantUrl() cascade. Same pattern as src/cli/qdrant-url-resolver.ts (tune) and src/bootstrap/factory.ts (doctor/factory).

Cascade:
1. Explicit QDRANT_URL env → use as-is
2. Probe http://localhost:6333 → if alive, external mode
3. Spawn / attach to ~/.tea-rags/qdrant embedded daemon (random port via daemon.port, refcounted)

Changes:
- tests/integration/config.mjs: export async resolveTestQdrant() → calls resolveQdrantUrl(process.env.QDRANT_URL)
- tests/integration/index.mjs: const resolution = await resolveTestQdrant(); new QdrantManager(resolution.url, undefined, resolution.reconnect, daemon); release() in cleanup()
- Ollama gating: if 11434 unreachable, skip() ollama-dependent suites with clear message instead of crashing the run

Acceptance:
- npm run test-integration works on fresh checkout with NO env vars (spawns embedded)
- npm run test-integration works with QDRANT_URL=http://localhost:6333 (external)
- npm run test-integration without Ollama (port 11434 down) skips suites 01, 04-13, 17-18 cleanly with error explaining what's needed
close_reason: Completed in commit 3d61840e: embedded Qdrant cascade + canonical TEST_DIR + 5 test-side API drift fixes. 103→212 passed assertions.

title: CLI: restructure flat commands → 'tea-rags projects [register|unregister|list|info]'
description:
Current CLI surface is too flat: - tea-rags register-project - tea-rags list-projects - tea-rags unregister-project Desired structure: - tea-rags projects (default: list — same as 'projects list') - tea-rags projects list [--json] - tea-rags projects register --path

--name - tea-rags projects unregister --name - tea-rags projects info (NEW — full info for a single project: path, embedding model, dimensions, qdrantUrl, indexedAt, chunksCount, etc.) Yargs subcommand pattern. Keep the old flat commands as deprecated aliases for one release cycle, then remove. Should also feed the same restructure into MCP tools? Probably not — MCP tool names are flat by spec. Just CLI.
close_reason: Landed in commit e1078e8d — projects subcommand group with register/list/unregister/info (new), flat commands removed (pre-publication breaking change).

title: BUG: orphan stub on duplicate-name register

close_reason: Landed in commits 58f591ed (zkaz/y2ke/f3yu + prime project section) + 91ae98c8 (marker-filter completes zkaz). Verified via MCP integration test 2026-05-13.

title: BUG: register_project creates stub entries with empty metadata (alreadyIndexed lies + list_projects shows empty fields)
description:
Two symptoms, same root cause:

1. alreadyIndexed: false after unregister+re-register — looks at registry.chunksCount which is stub-zero
2. list_projects returns empty embeddingModel / embeddingDimensions / qdrantUrl / indexedAt / teaRagsVersion / chunksCount — same stub values from register_project.register()

Fix: in ProjectRegistryOps.register, when an existing Qdrant collection is found (qdrant.collectionExists or non-zero countPoints), inline the recoverFromQdrant logic instead of recording an empty stub:
  - qdrant.countPoints → chunksCount
  - qdrant.getCollectionInfo → embeddingDimensions
  - qdrant.scrollFiltered(_, {}, 1) → sample payload.embeddingModel
  - this.deps.qdrant.url → qdrantUrl
  - new Date().toISOString() → indexedAt (or read from sample payload runtime field if it has indexedAt — TBD)
  - tea-rags package version → teaRagsVersion

Only fall back to all-empty stub if qdrant collection does not exist (truly new project).

After this fix:
- alreadyIndexed correctly true when chunks exist in Qdrant
- list_projects shows accurate metadata immediately after register, without requiring a reindex
close_reason: Landed in commits 58f591ed (zkaz/y2ke/f3yu + prime project section) + 91ae98c8 (marker-filter completes zkaz). Verified via MCP integration test 2026-05-13.

title: Auto-tune max_resident_memory_percent for embedded Qdrant
description:
Qdrant 1.18 adds strict-mode parameter max_resident_memory_percent (PR #8715) that rejects updates when memory crosses the threshold. Combined with low_memory_mode (PR #8714), this prevents the OOM crash class on weak laptops indexing big repos.

Implementation sketch:
- Detect system RAM at bootstrap (src/bootstrap/factory.ts or new infra/system-info.ts)
- Compute reasonable defaults:
  - <8GB system → low_memory_mode=true, max_resident_memory_percent=70
  - 8-16GB → max_resident_memory_percent=80
  - >16GB → defaults, no auto-tune
- Apply via collection creation config in IngestFacade or via cluster config endpoint
- Allow env override: TEA_RAGS_MAX_RESIDENT_MEMORY_PCT, TEA_RAGS_LOW_MEMORY_MODE
- Document in docs/website/ env vars page

Target user: 16GB MacBook indexing 2M+ chunk monorepo currently crashes. With this, gets a typed UpsertRejected error instead → can react gracefully.

title: Wire deep memory reporting into tea-rags prime digest
description:
Qdrant 1.18 adds memory usage breakdown per storage component via PR #8606. Surface it in the prime digest so users see WHERE memory goes, not just total.

Target output in prime digest:
```
Memory: 280MB
  HNSW graph:     120MB
  Payload index:   50MB
  Sparse vectors:  60MB
  Dense (raw):     50MB
```

Implementation sketch:
- Extend src/core/api/public/dto with MemoryBreakdown DTO
- Extend ExploreFacade or new InfraFacade method to fetch breakdown from Qdrant /collections/{name}/cluster or telemetry endpoint
- Extend src/cli/prime/ formatter — new section after status, before infra-health
- Gate behind Qdrant version check — only render if server is 1.18+ (otherwise show total only)

This is the single highest-leverage 1.18 win for user observability.

title: Bump .qdrant-required-version to 1.18.0 + client deps
description:
Core bump step. Update:
- .qdrant-required-version → 1.18.0
- @qdrant/js-client-rest → exact 1.18.0 (we pin exact per existing convention)
- src/core/infra/qdrant-version.ts behavior unchanged — eager load
- Update src/core/adapters/qdrant/embedded/download.ts download path
- Update src/core/adapters/qdrant/checkExternalQdrantVersion test fixtures
- assertNoDowngrade and warnIfStaleBinary should naturally pick up new pin

This is a BREAKING change for users with external Qdrant <1.18 — they'll get QdrantVersionTooOldError on startup. Use feat(config)! commit + BREAKING CHANGE footer per commit-rules.md.

Acceptance:
- npm run build && npm test green
- Embedded daemon downloads 1.18.0 binary on cold spawn
- External Qdrant <1.18 rejected with typed error
- CHANGELOG/release notes call out the breaking change

title: Migrate to Qdrant 1.18
description:
Migrate the embedded Qdrant daemon and client deps from 1.17.0 to 1.18.0. Goal: pick up the user-facing wins from 1.18 (deep memory reporting, ARM SIMD cosine, low-memory mode, dynamic CPU pool, optimizer error recovery, prevent_unoptimized) without breaking existing user indices.

Approach:
- Forward-compat validation FIRST on real 1.17 indices (storage migrates forward, but +1 minor jumps should be sanity-checked)
- Single coordinated bump of .qdrant-required-version + @qdrant/js-client-rest + tests
- Layered enablement of 1.18-only features behind small focused tasks

User wins shipped:
1. RAM breakdown in tea-rags prime digest (HNSW/payload/sparse/dense)
2. Faster search on Apple Silicon (ARM SIMD cosine)
3. Stability on weak laptops (low_memory_mode + max_resident_memory_percent)
4. Cold-cache search latency drop (dynamic CPU pool, free on bump)
5. Faster initial indexing (prevent_unoptimized=true for bulk upsert)
6. Recovery without daemon restart (optimizer error reset on collection update)

Reference: changelogs v1.17.1 (WAL fix, deferred updates) and v1.18.0 (TurboQuant, named-vectors API, RocksDB removal, deep memory reporting). TurboQuant and named-vectors API are explicitly out of scope for this epic — separate spike.

title: Implement poison-pill file quarantine for indexing pipeline
description: Skip files that break indexing (oversized chunks, embedding overflow, qdrant 413, parse fail, FS read fail) instead of dropping them silently. Persist quarantine state in $TEA_RAGS_DATA_DIR/snapshots/<collection>/quarantine.json (atomic write, alongside snapshot, lifecycle bound to collection). Quarantined files are retried on every subsequent index_codebase / reindex_changes run. get_index_status exposes quarantine.count (number only, no list). Full list with error codes is consumed by tea-rags doctor <project> --quarantine (separate task tea-rags-mcp-1wxw). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-12-quarantine-design.md

title: Serialize concurrent EnrichmentCoordinator runs
description: coordinator.prefetch() can be invoked while a previous awaitCompletion() is still running. The second call overwrites startTime/runId/runStartedAt, calls filePhase.init() (states.clear()) and chunkPhase.init() — but the first prefetch promise closures still hold orphaned state references and call markReady on the fresh chunkPhase. Result: final ALL_COMPLETE shows metrics from the wrong run (prefetchDurationMs=1640 borrowed from incremental in a force run that processed 22459 files). Either serialize prefetch behind a busy-flag/queue, or refactor coordinator to hold per-run RunState in a Map<runId, RunState>. File: src/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/enrichment/coordinator.ts.
close_reason: Task 2 complete: commit 5943a872

Plan Task 1: build createRunState() factory; coordinator.ts replaces constructor singletons with per-run instances; old promise closures mutate orphaned RunState. Eliminates Bug #1 (zombie missedFiles=488 across 6 incremental reindexes). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-08-enrichment-runstate-plan.md
close_reason: Task 1 complete: commit b8bb1673

title: Fix stale enrichment markers across reindex runs
description: EnrichmentApplier and EnrichmentCoordinator carry state across runs. In taxdome logs: 488 missed files persist across 6 incremental reindexes (backfill timeout 60s, backfilledFiles=0); force reindex final ALL_COMPLETE shows prefetchDurationMs=1640 and gitLogFileCount=5 borrowed from a concurrent incremental that overwrote phase state mid-flight. StatsCache also freezes on the incremental snapshot because chunk-backfill completion does not refire onChunkEnrichmentComplete. Tracking issue at .tea-rags/logs/pipeline-2026-05-06T13-44-15.log and pipeline-2026-05-07T15-03-02.log.
close_reason: De facto resolved via child issues c33r/d2y5/sr33 (commits 5943a872 FIFO serialize, b8bb1673 RunState, 8e40ed8c refire stats) plus b09bcd19 (stale marker overwrite guard), d74b09a7 (chunk enrichedAt + marker sync). qdrv (backfill timeout re-eval) remains as separate scope.

title: Fix INFRA_OLLAMA_CONTEXT_OVERFLOW: chunker hard cap + errorFiles report
description: From 2026-04-28 transcript 2de1b7ce design, never implemented. Layer 1: chunker maxChunkSize is enforced at chunkSize (not chunkSize*2), enforceMaxChunkSize post-process splits any oversized leftover with symbolId#partN. Layer 2: ChunkPipeline catches INFRA_OLLAMA_CONTEXT_OVERFLOW, registers in ErrorFileRegistry, IndexStats.errorFiles surfaces them instead of throwing. Reproduces on Python files with 6-7KB monolithic functions (kpi-calc.py, duty-report-generator.py).
close_reason: Implemented Layer 1 (chunker hard cap): maxChunkSize=chunkSize in base.ts, all *2 thresholds in tree-sitter.ts removed, enforceMaxChunkSize post-process splits oversized chunks into symbolId#partN with parentSymbolId=originalSymbolId. 3 new tests in tree-sitter-chunker.test.ts cover the kpi-calc.py scenario. Full suite 3781/3781 green. Layer 2 (errorFiles in stats) intentionally NOT done — Layer 1 makes overflow physically impossible for any chunk.

title: Replace filter-based delete with scroll + delete-by-IDs
description:
Root cause of reindex hang on large deltas: deletePointsByPathsBatched uses filter-based delete with 250-condition OR per batch. Even with keyword index on relativePath, Qdrant runs cardinality estimator + segment scan + optimizer repack, causing 300s AbortError timeouts on 3455+ file deltas (taxdome incident 2026-04-24).

Benchmark tea-rags-mcp-e3sn confirms: delete-by-IDs uses completely different WAL cost profile than delete-by-filter. All prior tune defaults (DELETE_BATCH_SIZE, DELETE_CONCURRENCY) optimized the wrong path.

Fix: Two-step delete in QdrantManager
1. scroll({filter: relativePath IN paths, with_payload: false, with_vector: false}) — fast with keyword index
2. client.delete({points: ids, wait: last}) — bypasses filter engine and optimizer-bitmask entirely

Expected: 3455-file delete goes from 22min timeout-loop to ~5-7s.

Replaces: deletePointsByPathsBatched filter path in src/core/adapters/qdrant/client.ts:753-823, deletion-strategy.ts L1+L2 fallback chain (currently worse than L0 for large deltas).

Root cause more detailed in pipeline-2026-04-24T15-05-13.log forensics. Keyword vs text index hypothesis disproved (both slow — filter engine is the bottleneck, not index lookup).

title: Embedded-aware delete tuning in QdrantTuneConfig
description: On embedded Qdrant, WAL serializes all writes — 4 concurrent delete-by-filter queue up behind one WAL channel and block upsert HTTP slots, causing permanent BACKPRESSURE_ON during large-delta reindex (observed on taxdome 3455+1289+2682). Override deleteBatchSize=200, deleteConcurrency=1 when resolveQdrantUrl returns mode='embedded' and user didn't set env explicitly. Remote path untouched.
notes: PIVOT 2026-04-24: Root cause is not embedded-mode WAL contention — it is filter-based delete architecture. Even with deleteConcurrency=1 + keyword index on relativePath (schema v13), Qdrant takes >300s on 250-OR-condition filter deletes because filter engine performs cardinality estimator + segment scan + optimizer repack per batch. Benchmark tea-rags-mcp-e3sn confirms tune defaults were measured against delete-by-IDs (points:[ids]), not filter-delete. This task is blocked by tea-rags-mcp-7r73 (scroll + delete-by-IDs root fix). After 7r73 lands, this task may be obsolete — re-evaluate if embedded-specific tuning still needed when filter-delete hot path is gone.
close_reason: obsolete after 7r73: filter-delete hot path removed, embedded-specific WAL contention no longer applies

title: Upgrade TypeScript 5.9 → 6.x
description:
Risk: MEDIUM. tsconfig.json is already well-configured but TS6 changes defaults.

Breaking changes:
- types: [] is now default — @types/node won't auto-load. Fix: add "types": ["node"] to tsconfig.json
- strict: true is default — we already set it explicitly, OK
- module defaults to esnext — we set NodeNext explicitly, OK
- moduleResolution: node deprecated — we use NodeNext, OK
- target minimum is ES2015 — we use ES2023, OK
- esModuleInterop: false no longer allowed — we use true, OK

Approach:
1. Add "types": ["node"] to tsconfig.json compilerOptions
2. npm install typescript@6
3. npm run build — fix any new type errors
4. Run full test suite
5. Check if any @types/* packages need explicit inclusion
notes: BLOCKED: typescript-eslint@8.57.2 requires typescript <6.0.0. TS 6 builds and tests pass but npm install fails without --legacy-peer-deps. Wait for typescript-eslint v9 or a patch with TS 6 peer support.

title: Rebuild hybrid search BM25 implementation
description:
Rebuild hybrid search BM25 implementation. Original problems: (1) vocabulary mismatch — generateSimple() created isolated vocabulary per call; (2) no code-aware tokenization — camelCase/snake_case not split; (3) client-side fusion instead of server-side RRF; (4) double IDF — collection modifier + generator; (5) no corpus training — all IDF=1.0.

DONE (subtasks closed):
- 9zeh ✅ sparse vector auto-migration on tokenizer change
- 1v9j ✅ double IDF fix
- 3uo0 ✅ vocabulary mismatch — single trained BM25 generator
- ydfp ✅ code-aware tokenizer (camelCase/snake_case/PascalCase)

REMAINING — only gk1d (server-side RRF migration):
Current hybridSearch() in src/core/adapters/qdrant/client.ts:950-1042 still runs dense + sparse in parallel and fuses client-side via min-max norm + weighted blend (semanticWeight=0.7 hardcoded). Score-distribution sensitive. Must migrate to Qdrant Query API prefetch + fusion:'rrf' (single request, server-side rank-based fusion). Qdrant 1.17 already in place (doe6 closed) — weighted RRF available, individual weights per prefetch instead of fixed k=60.

Workaround needed for level=file: queryGroups doesn't support RRF fusion (see comment in src/core/domains/explore/strategies/hybrid.ts:37). Either keep client-side grouping post-RRF, or build per-file aggregation manually.

Close xqfh once gk1d is merged.

title: [epic] Claude Plugin: tea-rags orchestration layer
description:
## Overview
Claude Code plugin with skills for code analysis workflows powered by tea-rags MCP.

## Skills

### Analytics skills (wrapping existing tools)
- `tea-rags:decomposition` — find large functions for splitting (rank_chunks decomposition)
- `tea-rags:tech-debt` — tech debt report (rank_chunks techDebt)
- `tea-rags:hotspots` — bug-prone areas (rank_chunks hotspots)
- `tea-rags:ownership` — knowledge silos (rank_chunks ownership)
- `tea-rags:security-audit` — old critical code (hybrid_search securityAudit)

### Workflow skills
- `tea-rags:feature-workflow` — orchestrated feature development:
  1. Search patterns: how is X done in this codebase?
  2. Find antipatterns: what NOT to do
  3. Check style: dominant author's conventions
  4. Suggest implementation: based on patterns found
- `tea-rags:bug-workflow` — orchestrated bug investigation:
  1. Search error context
  2. Find hotspots near the bug
  3. Trace history (who changed, when, why)
  4. Suggest fix based on similar fixes
- `tea-rags:code-review` — automated review:
  1. codeReview preset for recent changes
  2. Check against antipatterns
  3. Ownership/knowledge silo warnings

### Discovery skills
- `tea-rags:find-similar` — "more like this" from search results (needs search-by-ID)
- `tea-rags:pattern-search` — "find how pattern X is implemented" → group + deduplicate
- `tea-rags:style-check` — "show how author X writes in module Y"

## Dependencies
- Requires clean architecture (thin MCP layer, domain boundaries)
- Requires search-by-ID for find-similar workflow
- Requires chunk-level preset coverage for accurate analytics
notes: Children: nfkw (code-review), 0qpv (style-check), xayr (data-driven-gen optimization), i4p6 (bug-hunt optimization), ywt0 (docs), l26 (codegraph epic). Done: bug-hunt, research, explore, data-driven-generation, index skills, search-cascade rule, risk-assessment. Closed: izin (analytics wrappers → risk-assessment), wf4 (search strategy → search-cascade rule).
close_reason: Core plugin complete: all skills working (bug-hunt, explore, data-driven-generation, index, risk-assessment, search-cascade). Remaining children (nfkw, 0qpv, xayr, i4p6, ywt0) live independently.
