express использует Object.defineProperty(this,'router',{get:fn}) и helper defineGetter(req,'query',fn). Сейчас walker не эмиттит symbol для этих getters. Express request.js имеет 8 defineGetter calls (query, protocol, secure, ip, ips, subdomains, path, host). Решение:
1. Object.defineProperty(obj,'name',{get,set}) → emit obj.name (+marker accessor)
2. defineGetter(obj,'name',fn) — project-specific helper; распознавать по callee.text==="defineGetter" и эмиттить obj.name
Низкий impact для express (getters обычно не вызывают друг друга), средний для других Node.js codebases.
close_reason: Validated end-to-end on express OSS after chunkId fix (sibling chunks now have distinct IDs). lib/application.js: 2→28 chunks (14x). All 9 HTTP verbs (app.get/post/put/delete/head/options/patch/connect/trace) + app.all + app.router (this-resolution) + app.init (parent preserved) + 16 others. find_symbol(app.get) → chunk. get_callers(View#constructor) → app.render via new View(...). req.query/req.protocol/etc via defineGetter.

title: JS walker: port super() handler + classExtends collection from TS walker
description:
ES6 class syntax в JS идентичен TS (class X extends Y { constructor() { super() } }), но JS walker не извлекает super(...) calls и не собирает FileExtraction.classExtends. Следствие: javascript-resolver.ts:43-55 ветка resolveSuper никогда не получает classExtends — super() в JS либо игнорируется, либо self-loops на ту же class.constructor. Что портировать из typescript-walker.ts:
1. ветка callee.type === 'super' в collectCalls — эмит { receiver: 'super', member: 'constructor' } (паттерн 3a84 TS-stage)
2. collectClassExtends() в extractFromJavaScriptFile — обходит class_declaration + extracts extends clause (паттерн d29r TS-stage)
3. classExtends на FileExtraction (контракт уже расширен — Record<string,string>)

Тест: integration с реальным JS файлом 'class Foo extends Bar { constructor() { super(); } }' — get_callees Foo#constructor должен возвращать Bar#constructor, не Foo#constructor.
close_reason: Validated on express OSS: find_symbol(app.set/app.use) → returns chunks; get_callers(app.handle/app.enable) → finds callers via this-binding; find_symbol('set') short-name navigation works. 3tjz super+classExtends port done (no ES6 classes in express to test but unit tests green).

title: JS walker: extract Object.defineProperty/Object.defineProperties getters and setters as symbols
description: JS walker не извлекает getter/setter определения, заданные через Object.defineProperty(obj, 'name', { get: fn, set: fn }). Express использует это для app.router (application.js:69), для trustProxyDefaultSymbol и т.д. Без этого: (1) символы 'app.router', 'app.<getter>' не существуют в индексе, (2) call-targets через geter accessor unresolvable. Решение: ловить call_expression где callee.text === 'Object.defineProperty' и второй arg — string literal, третий — object_expression с свойствами get/set. Эмитим NamedSymbol с именем '<receiver>.<getterName>'. Аналогично defineProperties (object с descriptors). TDD.
close_reason: Fixed: JS walker extracts Object.defineProperty/defineProperties accessor symbols via collectJsDefinePropertySymbols (commit d04eb569, RED→GREEN, both forms + shorthand get/set)

title: TS abstract_class_declaration missing from chunker chunkableTypes
description: Tree-sitter-typescript emits 'abstract_class_declaration' for 'abstract class X {}', not 'class_declaration'. The TypeScript LanguageDefinition.chunkableTypes (src/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/config.ts:86-94) lists only 'class_declaration', so abstract bases never become chunks and their methods never appear as standalone chunks in Qdrant. Symptom: searches by symbolId like 'BaseExploreStrategy#someMethod' miss the method body even though codegraph now has the symbol (q3o2 fixed the codegraph layer). Also chunker/hooks/typescript/utils.ts has docstring 'class_declaration container' that may need a similar branch. Fix: add 'abstract_class_declaration' to chunkableTypes; audit hooks/typescript/* for class_declaration string matches that need to widen. TDD: write a chunker test that chunks 'abstract class Base { foo() {} }' and asserts the method ends up as its own chunk.
close_reason: Fixed: abstract_class_declaration added to TS chunkableTypes, methods now chunked (commit 8d266123, RED→GREEN test added)

title: TypeScript walker: parameter type tracking needed for interface dispatch (resolver.resolve where resolver: CallResolver)
description: 2qp6 imports-narrowed fallback fires only on ambiguous (N>1) global short-name lookup. For 'resolver.resolve(call, ctx)' where resolver is a function parameter typed CallResolver: walker emits {receiver: 'resolver', member: 'resolve'} with receiver lowercased. Resolver tries: lookup 'resolver' as constant — fails (lowercase). lookup 'resolver' in classFieldTypes — only handles 'this.field', not bare params. Falls to short-name 'resolve' — finds N=8 matches (one per *CallResolver). imports-narrowed: provider.ts imports ALL resolver files, so narrowing doesn't help. Result: drop. Real fix: walker tracks parameter types — for 'function foo(p: SomeType)', store {paramName: 'p', type: 'SomeType'} per chunk. Resolver consults this map when receiver matches a param. Mirrors PythonCallResolver.resolveByLocalType pattern. Bigger work — own bead.
close_reason: Fixed: TS walker emits param-type localBindings, resolver resolveByLocalType resolves typed-param receivers before ambiguous short-name fallback (commit 2b37bd72, walker+resolver RED→GREEN)

title: TypeScript walker: record named import specifiers per file (proper fix for kebab→Pascal matching)
description: Companion to the importMatchesReceiver bug above. Walker currently records imports as raw file paths. Should also extract the named specifiers ('import { RankModule, FooHelper } from "./rank-module.js"' → record map { RankModule: './rank-module.js', FooHelper: './rank-module.js' }). Then resolver can match receiver to a named specifier directly. This proper fix is more invasive (changes FileExtraction.imports shape) but eliminates ALL kebab-case mismatch false negatives without hacky normalization. Filed as P2 since the cheaper hack-fix lands first.
close_reason: Fixed: ImportRef.importedNames records TS named/default/namespace specifiers; resolver matches receiver to named specifier as first pass, kebab→Pascal hack demoted to fallback (commit 5ab7d750, 1095 tests green)

title: TypeScript codegraph: dynamic dispatch through lookup tables not extracted (LANGUAGES[ext].walker)
description: Empirical: extractFromTypescriptFile and extractFromRubyFile get_callers=[]. These are called via const walker = LANGUAGES[ext].walker; walker(input); pattern in provider.ts. Walker likely extracts as member access call with computed property [ext], dropped by resolver. Fix: walker should recognize 'LANGUAGES[var].walker(args)' pattern — if LANGUAGES is a const object literal with walker fields, the call could fan-out to all values. Without this, every plugin-style dispatch table breaks fanIn metrics. Lower priority because requires significant walker enhancement. May overlap with 2qp6 (interface dispatch). Consider as part of broader 'multi-target dispatch resolution' effort.
notes: Implemented per spec (7-step order, TDD). Walker: collectDispatchTables + scope-aware candidate-set binding + CallRef.dispatch tagging + callbackParams + dispatchArgs. Resolver: TSCallResolver.resolveDispatch (fan-out, static/dynamic key, S1/S2, import disambiguation, bounded inter-proc callback-param join). Provider: runDispatchTables + runCallbackParams run-global aggregation + empty-run reset + resolveExtraction wiring. Contract: DispatchTable/DispatchTableDef/DispatchRef/DispatchEdge + FileExtraction/CallRef/CallContext/CallResolver fields. Tests: 13 walker + 10 resolver + 3 provider, all green. Full suite 6013 green, build+lint clean. Offline smoke on real provider.ts confirms LANGUAGES recognized (8 walkers + 8 nameOf), loadParser arrow dropped, langConfig.walker fan-out + collectSymbols callback-param[1] join. PENDING: live MCP integration (npm link + reconnect + force_reindex tea-rags) — needs user reconnect. Branch worktree-multi-target-dispatch-n0zj; NOT committed yet.
close_reason: Implemented + merged to main (4cd8bf40). Walker dispatch-table extraction + resolver fan-out + bounded callback-param join + provider run-global aggregation. Verified live: get_callers(extractFromTypescriptFile)=CodegraphEnrichmentProvider#extractOneFile, get_callers(tsNameOf)=CodegraphEnrichmentProvider#collectSymbols (both were []). Full suite 6023 green. Multi-language rollout tracked in epic tea-rags-mcp-542x.

title: TS walker: type-only imports leak into imports[] creating phantom file edges
description: The TS walker's collectImports only checks node.type === 'import_statement' and grabs the string source. It does NOT filter 'import type { X } from "./x"' (type-only imports), which create no runtime dependency. Result: codegraph emits a file-level import edge for a type-only import, inflating fanOut/fanIn for type-only relationships. Expected: skip nodes that have an 'import' followed by 'type' keyword child (import_type_clause / 'type' modifier), OR skip the inner import_specifier when it carries the 'type' modifier. Fix in extraction/typescript-walker.ts collectImports. Discovered via AST probe in PART 2 of codegraph-symbols-slice-1-plan session.
close_reason: Fixed: TS walker now skips top-level import_statement nodes whose first non-'import' child is the 'type' keyword (i.e. 'import type { X } from ...'). Type-only imports are erased at compile time and produce no runtime dependency; including them inflated codegraph fanOut/fanIn. Per-specifier 'import { type X, Y } ...' still kept because Y is a runtime import. RED+GREEN tests in typescript-walker.test.ts under 'type-only imports filter'. Full suite 5158/5158 green.

title: TS walker: bare super() constructor call extracted as free call 'super', not Base#constructor
description: The TS walker emits super() (constructor super call) as { receiver: null, member: 'super' } because callee.type is the 'super' keyword node. The TS resolver then does lookupByShortName('super') which finds nothing and the edge is dropped. Expected: emit { receiver: 'super', member: 'constructor' } so the existing super.X branch in ts-resolver.ts routes it to <EnclosingClass>#constructor (which by symbolid-convention is the parent's constructor lookup target). Fix in extraction/typescript-walker.ts collectCalls: when callee.type === 'super', emit receiver='super' member='constructor'. Discovered via AST probe in PART 2 of codegraph-symbols-slice-1-plan session. Mirror Ruby tea-rags-mcp-brp1 super handling.
close_reason: Fixed: TS walker now emits super(arg) as { receiver: 'super', member: 'constructor' } instead of { receiver: null, member: 'super' }, so the existing super-branch in ts-resolver routes super-constructor-calls to <EnclosingClass>#constructor of the parent. RED+GREEN tests in typescript-walker.test.ts under 'super() constructor calls'. Full suite 5158/5158 green.

title: TypeScript codegraph: module-level exported functions / interface-dispatch callers not resolved
description: get_callers(RubyCallResolver#resolve) returns []. But it IS called from provider.ts via 'resolver.resolve(call, ctx)'. The resolver variable is typed as 'CallResolver' (interface) — actual implementation depends on file extension. Walker likely extracts the call as 'resolver.resolve(...)' with receiver=resolver. Resolver should: (a) recognize 'resolver' as a parameter typed CallResolver, (b) walk implementers (RubyCallResolver, JavaCallResolver, etc.) and emit multi-target edges OR a 'best-effort' interface edge. Similar issues: extractFromRubyFile (top-level exported function) get_callers=[] though called from provider.ts. ExploreFacade#findSymbol get_callers=[] though called from MCP tool layer. These are real recall gaps in TS interface/polymorphism handling. Test on tea-rags-worktree codebase — many polymorphic dispatch patterns visible.
close_reason: Fixed: TS resolver now narrows ambiguous global short-name fallback by ctx.imports — when N>1 candidates exist but exactly one's file is reachable via the caller's imports, resolve to that one. Closes the interface-dispatch recall gap for resolver.resolve()-style polymorphic calls. RED+GREEN tests in ts-resolver.test.ts under 'imports-narrowed fallback for ambiguous global short-name' (3 cases). Full suite 5158/5158 green.

title: find_symbol fix partial: short method names + deeply nested FQNs still return empty after yx10 fix
description: Post-yx10 verification on huginn: find_symbol(symbol='Agent#current_event=') NOW works (= suffix). But: find_symbol(symbol='createNote') empty (short name), find_symbol(symbol='Agents::EvernoteAgent::NoteStore#createNote') empty (4-segment FQN), find_symbol(symbol='ScenarioImport::AgentDiff::FieldDiff#incoming') empty (3-segment FQN with attr_accessor chunk). Hypothesis: (1) buildSymbolFilter token still doesn't match Qdrant's tokenization of synthetic chunks' name field, OR (2) post-filter for FQN queries rejects valid candidates because parentSymbolId comparison fails on synthetic chunks. Investigate: what name+symbolId+parentSymbolId synthetic delegate/attr_accessor chunks have, and trace why Qdrant text match on token 'createNote' doesn't find a chunk whose name is exactly 'createNote'. May need to switch to keyword (filter) match instead of text match for the symbolId field.
close_reason: Verified resolved via force-reindex of huginn on current main build (3159 chunks). find_symbol read-path (yx10/xnfv) is correct: short names (create_note), deep FQNs (Agents::EvernoteAgent::NoteStore#create_note), and attr_accessor synthetic members (ScenarioImport::AgentDiff::FieldDiff#incoming/#current) all resolve. Reported empties were: (a) wrong test data — bead queried camelCase 'createNote' but Ruby is snake_case 'create_note'; (b) stale v1.27.0 index lacked multi-symbol attr_accessor members, now emitted by ruby-macros.ts multi-symbol expansion (commit 7476636a). Both original hypotheses (buildSymbolFilter tokenization, post-filter parentSymbolId) disproven.

title: Ruby walker: Module#prepend not extracted as ancestor relationship
description: prepend M in a class body makes M's methods come BEFORE the class's own in MRO. Statically analyzable: AST has 'prepend ModuleName' call node at class body level. Walker should: (1) collect 'prepend M' alongside 'include M' in collectRubyClassAncestors (currently only includes are tracked); (2) mark prepended ancestors as 'before-self' priority. Resolver: when resolving instance method call, check prepended modules FIRST, then the class itself, then included modules. Without this, agents that prepend a Concern to override a method (common Rails pattern) get null edges for the override. Same complexity tier as include — should be fixed in same TDD pass.
close_reason: walker emits prepended modules into new FileExtraction.classPrependedAncestors map (separate from classAncestors); provider aggregates into runPrependedAncestors; resolver checks prepended modules BEFORE the bound class in resolveByLocalTypeInternal, iterating in reverse so last prepend wins MRO. Tests cover M wins over A, M2 wins over M1, prepend miss falls through to A, regression on plain include.

title: find_symbol returns empty for short method names and symbolIds with special chars (=, !, ?, #)
description: Empirical: find_symbol(symbol='ScenarioImport::AgentDiff::FieldDiff#incoming') returns []. find_symbol(symbol='Agents::EvernoteAgent::NoteStore#createNote') returns []. find_symbol(symbol='createNote') returns []. But these symbols DEMONSTRABLY exist (visible in navigation.nextSymbolId / prevSymbolId of adjacent chunks, surfaced by hybrid_search with content query). Hypothesis: find_symbol uses Qdrant text-match against a specific field (likely 'name' or 'symbolId') with tokenization that breaks on '#'/'='/'!'/'?'/'::' characters. Synthetic chunks from attr_accessor/delegate/alias macros (recently added in 3nf3/zy3f/y2z5) have proper name+symbolId fields per hybrid_search visibility, so the issue is find_symbol's matching layer, not chunker emission. Tool description claims 'Supports partial match: Reranker finds the class and all its methods' — but apparently this only works for tokenized whole words. Fix: investigate find_symbol implementation (likely in src/core/api/internal/ops/ or src/mcp/tools/find_symbol.ts). Either: (a) switch to exact symbolId match (Qdrant match: { value: ... } filter); (b) normalize the query and target string before matching; (c) update tool description to clarify partial match scope. Tests: full symbolId with # works, partial method name finds it, synthetic chunks findable.
close_reason: Fixed via SymbolSearchStrategy tokenization-aware filter + post-filter. 8 RED tests in tests/core/domains/explore/strategies/symbol.test.ts (yx10 describe block) drove the fix. Root cause: Qdrant word tokenizer on symbolId text index splits on #/./::/=/?/! — passing full FQN to match.text joined tokens with AND and missed target rows on live indices. Fix: symbolTextToken() reduces query to last name segment, filterByExactSymbolId() post-filters the scroll superset to exact symbolId matches plus members of the requested container. Follow-up: tea-rags-mcp-aorm (P3) for chunkType=dsl enhancement.

title: Ruby resolver: send/public_send/__send__ with literal symbol argument resolves to method short-name
description: obj.send(:method_name) where receiver has known type → equivalent to obj.method_name. Currently walker extracts as call with receiver=obj, member='send', and the symbol literal :method_name is in the args. Resolver doesn't peek inside args. Fix: when member is 'send'/'public_send'/'__send__' AND the first argument is a literal symbol (simple_symbol AST node), rewrite the CallRef before resolution to receiver=obj, member=<symbol>. Then standard resolution applies. Edge case: 'self.send(:foo)' = 'self.foo'. Tests: extract :literal arg, route through standard resolver, verify (a) receiver-set guard still drops obj.send(var), (b) self.send(:foo) resolves to enclosing class. Note: __send__ behaves identically.
close_reason: walker now unwraps bare send(:foo) and self.send(:foo) to receiver=null + member=literal; existing receiver-set unwrap unchanged; resolver same-class fallback handles enclosing-class lookup via callerScope

title: Ruby chunker/walker: alias_method :new_name, :old_name creates alias-of-symbol edge
description: In Ruby: 'alias_method :new, :old' OR 'alias new old' creates a new method 'new' that points to 'old' in the same class. Currently neither chunker nor walker emit anything for this construct. Fix: chunker recognizes alias_method/alias macros at class body level (similar to attr_accessor/delegate pattern just fixed in 3nf3/zy3f). For 'alias_method :new, :old' emit a synthetic method symbol EnclosingClass#new (chunkType=function or 'alias'). Optionally: walker emits a CallRef from EnclosingClass#new → EnclosingClass#old as a synthetic edge (or resolver synthesizes the redirect). Without this, callers of the alias 'new_name' get null edges and the call graph misses the redirect chain. Pattern recognized in vendor/gems/dotenv-3.2.0/lib/dotenv.rb: 'alias_method :overload, :overwrite'.
close_reason: chunker (ruby-macros.ts) recognises alias_method :new, :old and alias new old; codegraph provider (rubyAliasMethodEmission + rubyAliasKeywordEmission) mirrors so symbolIds match; walker emits synthetic CallRef from new alias to old method (receiver=null) so call graph traces the redirect via same-class fallback

title: Ruby chunker: 'delegate :foo, to: :bar' (Rails) generated delegating methods not extracted
description: huginn evernote_agent.rb 'delegate :createNote, :updateNote, ..., to: :en_note_store' should generate 9 delegating instance methods on the enclosing class. Chunker doesn't extract these. Without them, calls to delegated methods cannot resolve. Pattern similar to attr_accessor. Fix: walker recognizes 'delegate :sym1, :sym2, ..., to: :receiver' macro and emits a synthetic method symbol per delegated name in the symbol list. The target is :receiver (the delegate destination), so the symbol body can be an internal 'forwards to' edge if receiver also resolves.
close_reason: Fixed in the same change as 3nf3: chunker/extraction/ruby-macros.ts handles delegate :foo, :bar, to: :other — emits one instance-method symbol per leading simple_symbol arg, stops at the first hash/kwarg (to:, allow_nil:, prefix:). 3 delegate-specific tests pass including the nested EvernoteAgent::NoteStore shape.

title: [epic] Codegraph in plugins: integrate composite presets into tea-rags/dinopowers skills
description:
⚠️ ДОЛЖНО БЫТЬ ОГОВОРЕНО ПЕРЕД РЕАЛИЗАЦИЕЙ
⚠️ ОТДЕЛЬНЫЕ ПОДЗАДАЧИ МОГУТ БЫТЬ ОСОЗНАННО ПРОПУЩЕНЫ

Goal: integrate the codegraph composite presets AND new MCP tools (get_callers, get_callees, find_cycles) into tea-rags + dinopowers plugin skills so agents using these skills benefit from structural-graph context (edge truth), not just git signals or content matching.

## Full composite preset inventory (12 total)

### Shipped in Slice 2 worktree (9)

NEW codegraph presets (3) — must be explicitly added to skill SKILL.md to be used:
- blastRadius — fanIn + isHub + churn + bugFix ("what explodes if touched")
- architecturalHub — isHub + fanIn + fanOutPerLine + churn ("structural backbone")
- entryPoint — fanOutPerLine + (negative)fanIn + chunkSize ("entry point shape")

D4 overrides (6) — TRANSPARENT: skills already naming these get codegraph automatically via reranker override-by-name. SKILL.md edits only for narrative ("git churn" -> "git + structural"):
- hotspots, techDebt, dangerous, ownership, securityAudit, codeReview

### Planned D5 — BLOCKED on Slice 4 C-AST complexity (3)

GATED: skill design accounts for them, but SKILL.md implementation deferred until tea-rags-mcp-wtkz (C-AST) lands the complexity/cognitiveLoad derived signals they weight:
- changeRisk — process(churn+bugFix+volatility+silo) + complexity + cognitiveLoad + fanIn
- safeToRefactor — isLeaf + stability + (negative)cognitiveLoad
- couplingComplexity — fanOutPerLine + cognitiveLoad + chunkSize + complexity

## New MCP tools (3, all shipped in Slice 2 worktree)

- get_callers(symbolId) — exact call sites invoking a symbol
- get_callees(symbolId) — outgoing calls from a symbol
- find_cycles(scope=file|method [, pathPattern after yhlv]) — Tarjan SCC

NONE referenced in any plugin skill yet (verified grep = 0). Tools work but skills don't know they exist.

## Preset/tool -> skill mapping

| Preset / tool | Where applicable |
|---|---|
| blastRadius | writing-plans, executing-plans, verification-before-completion, receiving-code-review, data-driven-generation (Step 6) |
| architecturalHub | explore (TR1), risk-assessment, brainstorming |
| entryPoint | explore (TR1), bug-hunt |
| hotspots (D4) | bug-hunt, risk-assessment [transparent] |
| techDebt (D4) | risk-assessment, writing-plans [transparent] |
| dangerous (D4) | bug-hunt, receiving-code-review, executing-plans [transparent] |
| ownership (D4) | requesting-code-review, receiving-code-review [transparent] |
| securityAudit (D4) | risk-assessment [transparent] |
| codeReview (D4) | requesting-code-review [transparent] |
| changeRisk (D5, gated) | executing-plans (Task guard escalation), verification-before-completion, writing-plans |
| safeToRefactor (D5, gated) | refactoring-scan (PRIMARY), extract-project-patterns, executing-plans (Step 5 template), brainstorming |
| couplingComplexity (D5, gated) | risk-assessment, writing-plans (sequencing), refactoring-scan (anti-signal) |
| get_callers / get_callees | explore (TR1), bug-hunt, systematic-debugging, receiving-code-review, requesting-code-review |
| find_cycles | explore (TR1), risk-assessment, finishing-a-development-branch |

## Integration categories per skill

1. TRANSPARENT (6 D4 overrides): skills naming hotspots/techDebt/dangerous/ownership/securityAudit/codeReview get codegraph automatically. SKILL.md edit only if narrative lies.
2. NEW SURFACE READY (3 Slice 2 presets + 3 MCP tools): blastRadius/architecturalHub/entryPoint + get_callers/get_callees/find_cycles — must be explicitly added.
3. NEW SURFACE GATED (3 D5 presets): changeRisk/safeToRefactor/couplingComplexity — design accounts for them, implementation deferred to Slice 4.

## Per-skill task convention

Each subtask under this epic carries:
- Which presets/tools it integrates (from the matrix above)
- Ready vs gated flag per preset (Slice 2 ready / Slice 4 gated)
- "Как сейчас vs Как будет" per intent (parallel format)
- No grep in fallback paths — agent always uses find_symbol / hybrid_search for navigation
- SKILL.md + sub-pattern .md changes, NO src/ code (presets + tools already shipped)
- Eval re-baseline note (fold into existing eval task where applicable)
- Plugin minor version bump

## Subtasks (iterative — created one at a time as owner approves each)

tea-rags:
- TR1 (tea-rags-mcp-3bma) CREATED — explore: architecturalHub + entryPoint + get_callers/get_callees + find_cycles
- TR2 — bug-hunt: dangerous (primary) + entryPoint(path origin) + get_callers/get_callees(fault chain) + find_cycles(state-loop hypothesis)
- TR3 — risk-assessment: architecturalHub axis + securityAudit/techDebt(transparent) + couplingComplexity(gated) + find_cycles
- TR4 — data-driven-generation: replace custom {imports,churn,ownership} weights with blastRadius preset
- TR5 — refactoring-scan: safeToRefactor(PRIMARY, gated) + couplingComplexity(anti-signal, gated) + get_callers(blast check)
- TR6 — extract-project-patterns: safeToRefactor for stable-code templates (gated)
- TR7 — analytics-rerank: catalogue update (new composites + use-case examples) [doc-only]
- TR8 — filter-building: codegraph filter examples (isHub, fanIn, instability) [doc-only]

dinopowers:
- DP1 — writing-plans: blastRadius (replace custom weights) + techDebt + couplingComplexity(gated sequencing)
- DP2 — executing-plans: blastRadius (replace Step 2 custom weights) + changeRisk gate(gated) + safeToRefactor Step 5(gated) + dangerous(transparent)
- DP3 — brainstorming: architecturalHub + dangerous(transparent) surface before creative work
- DP4 — systematic-debugging: inherit bug-hunt enriched suspects + get_callers/get_callees fault chain
- DP5 — verification-before-completion: blastRadius collateral scan + changeRisk(gated)
- DP6 — requesting-code-review: codeReview/ownership(transparent) + get_callers(reviewer hub awareness)
- DP7 — receiving-code-review: blastRadius(rename blast) + get_callers + dangerous(transparent)
- DP8 — finishing-a-development-branch: risk-assessment inherits codegraph presets + find_cycles on branch diff [mostly transparent]

Subtasks created iteratively, NOT in bulk — owner reviews each "как сейчас / как будет / польза" analysis and decides create/skip.

## Discussion required before implementation

1. Preset naming in SKILL.md — string ("blastRadius") not symbolic constant. Confirmed convention.
2. Custom-weights deprecation — hard-cut vs soft-deprecate for benchmarked skills (DDG, writing-plans, executing-plans use {imports,churn,ownership}).
3. Order: tea-rags first (consumed by dinopowers via chain), then dinopowers.
4. Skill version bumps — one per skill task vs one per epic batch.

## Skippable subtasks

- TR5, TR6 (safeToRefactor) + DP2 changeRisk gate + DP5 changeRisk — blocked on Slice 4 (jkdp/wtkz). Skip until C-AST lands.
- TR7, TR8 — pure documentation. Lower priority.
- DP8 — minimal value-add (transparent). Skip if owner accepts implicit improvement.

Parent epic: tea-rags-mcp-l26

title: Ruby walker: super keyword calls not extracted as CallRef
description: huginn confirms: Agents::JavaScriptAgent::ConditionalFollowRedirects#call contains 'super' (line 30) -> 0 callees. Agents::WebsiteAgent::Repeater#initialize contains 'super(Float::INFINITY) { ... }' (line 763) -> 0 callees. Walker's collectRubyCalls likely doesn't visit super_call / super tree-sitter nodes. Fix: extract super nodes as CallRef with receiver=null and member='super' (or resolve to parent class's same-named method via classAncestors). Without super tracking, inheritance chains have huge recall gaps in Rails/Ruby projects.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Arthur Korochansky
comments.text: Live diagnostic 2026-05-22: end-to-end pipeline VERIFIED working via 2 new integration tests in tests/core/domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/provider-fanin-edges-invariant.test.ts ('resolves super to parent class instance method end-to-end' + 'multi-level super chain A < B < C to C#foo'). Real tree-sitter parse + walker + NDJSON spill + JSON.parse + resolver + DuckDB graph round-trips super edges correctly when parent class is INTERNAL. The huginn live MCP symptom (ConditionalFollowRedirects#call has 0 callees, Repeater#initialize has 0 callees) is EXPECTED behavior because parent classes (Faraday::Middleware, Object) are gem dependencies OUTSIDE the indexed sources — resolver returns null per the existing unit test at ruby-resolver.test.ts:1578 ('returns null targetSymbolId when parent class is outside the project'). Same logic applies to all stdlib/gem super calls. No regression. Companion fix jsa0 (requireMatch absorbing bare calls into arbitrary file edges) was a SEPARATE bug now fixed.

title: Slice 2 follow-up: complete codegraph signal design review (labels + confidence + isHub/isLeaf refactor + calibration)
description:
⚠️ ДОЛЖНО БЫТЬ ОГОВОРЕНО ПЕРЕД РЕАЛИЗАЦИЕЙ
⚠️ ОТДЕЛЬНЫЕ ФАЗЫ МОГУТ БЫТЬ ОСОЗНАННО ПРОПУЩЕНЫ

Goal: complete review of codegraph payload signal design to fix two related defects discovered 2026-05-21:

1. Codegraph signals do NOT appear in `get_index_metrics` output (IndexMetricsQuery skips them silently).
2. `rankingOverlay` for codegraph signals shows NO labels (Reranker label resolver requires stats.labels).

**Root cause (single):**
src/core/domains/explore/queries/index-metrics.ts:57 — `if (!descriptor?.stats?.labels) continue;`
src/core/domains/explore/reranker.ts:561 — same `stats.labels` precondition for label resolution.

All 7 numeric codegraph descriptors in src/core/domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/payload-signals.ts lack stats.labels. Boolean descriptors (isHub, isLeaf) have a separate semantic issue — they are derived labels masquerading as payload fields.

---

## Brainstorm decision (2026-05-21) — alpha-blending NOT in scope

Standard alpha-blending requires file-level AND chunk-level signals measuring the SAME quantity at different granularity (per CLAUDE.md glossary: `effective = alpha * chunk + (1-alpha) * file`). Codegraph file/chunk pairs measure DIFFERENT physical quantities:
- file.fanIn = imports between files (file graph)
- chunk.fanIn = call sites between symbols (method graph)

These are two distinct graphs. Alpha-blending not applicable. **Excluded from this task.**

Possible future innovation: direct (file.fanIn) vs transitive (file.transitiveImpact) blending — same concept ("popularity") at different reach. Speculative; defer until live data shows fanIn + transitiveImpact give conflicting ranking signals in presets. Not included here.

---

## Phase 1: stats.labels for 7 numeric descriptors

| Signal | Proposed labels (initial guess — calibrate via Phase 4) |
|---|---|
| codegraph.file.fanIn | { p25: 'isolated', p50: 'typical', p75: 'popular', p95: 'hub' } |
| codegraph.file.fanOut | { p25: 'minimal', p50: 'typical', p75: 'heavy', p95: 'exhaustive' } |
| codegraph.file.instability | { p50: 'stable', p75: 'mixed', p95: 'unstable' } |
| codegraph.file.transitiveImpact | { p50: 'local', p75: 'regional', p95: 'systemic' } |
| codegraph.chunk.fanIn | { p25: 'unused', p50: 'typical', p75: 'frequent', p95: 'central' } |
| codegraph.chunk.fanOut | { p25: 'leaf', p50: 'typical', p75: 'orchestrator', p95: 'god-method' } |
| codegraph.chunk.pageRank | { p50: 'peripheral', p75: 'important', p95: 'critical' } |

Plus: chunk-level descriptors get `chunkTypeFilter: 'function'` (matches static methodLines pattern — percentile statistically meaningful only on function chunks, not docs/blocks).

Effect: get_index_metrics shows codegraph signals + rankingOverlay surfaces labels for codegraph-aware presets (architecturalHub, entryPoint, blastRadius).

## Phase 2 (REVISED 2026-05-21): instability confidence + new connectionCount raw signal

Per .claude/rules/signal-confidence.md analysis — only ONE codegraph signal is a clear confidence target:

**Per-signal confidence applicability analysis:**

| Signal | Confidence? | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| file.fanIn | NO | exact count, no statistical noise |
| file.fanOut | NO | exact count |
| file.instability | YES | ratio fanOut/(fanIn+fanOut) swings 0↔1 at N=1 |
| file.transitiveImpact | algorithmic boundary not statistical — defer to Phase 4 |
| chunk.fanIn | NO | exact count of call sites |
| chunk.fanOut | NO | exact count of outgoing calls |
| chunk.pageRank | edge case at <50 nodes — defer to Phase 4 calibration |

**Phase 2 deliverable: ONE new raw signal + ONE confidence config**

### 2a. New raw signal `codegraph.file.connectionCount`

```ts
{
  key: "codegraph.file.connectionCount",
  type: "number",
  description: "fanIn + fanOut — total file connections, denominator of instability",
  stats: {
    labels: { p25: "low", p50: "typical", p75: "high", p95: "extreme" },
    percentilesToCompute: [10],   // referenced by instability confidence rules
  }
}
```

Computed in `CodegraphEnrichmentProvider.buildFileSignals` alongside fanIn/fanOut — already have both values, sum is free.

### 2b. instability confidence (Option B from brainstorm — connectionCount as support)

```ts
{
  key: "codegraph.file.instability",
  stats: {
    labels: { p50: "stable", p75: "mixed", p95: "unstable" },
    confidence: {
      support: "connectionCount",  // literal denominator — semantic clean, codegraph standalone (no git dependency)
      score: { threshold: 5, adaptivePercentile: 25 },
      label: {
        rules: [
          { whenSupportBelow: "p10", fallback: 2, ceiling: "stable" },
          { whenSupportBelow: "p25", fallback: 5, ceiling: "mixed" }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Effect: a file with connectionCount=1 (single import or single export) cannot show "unstable" label — score path dampens contribution, label path caps at "stable". Prevents misleading hot-take on near-isolated files.

Why Option B over reusing commitCount:
- Semantic: connectionCount IS the literal denominator of instability
- Standalone: codegraph trajectory doesn't depend on git
- Storage: one extra number per file — trivial

## Phase 3: isHub / isLeaf refactor decision

Current state: payload booleans `codegraph.file.isHub`, `codegraph.file.isLeaf`. Header comment admits they are computed from fanIn p95 / fanOut==0 thresholds.

**Two options to discuss:**

**Option A — keep as boolean payload, add stats.labels to fanIn/fanOut**

Pro: backward compatible. The boolean fields stay queryable, label rendering happens via fanIn/fanOut labels.
Con: redundancy. isHub == (fanIn label === 'hub'). Two paths for same concept.

**Option B — remove isHub/isLeaf payload fields entirely**

Migrate consumer code (presets: blastRadius uses `isHub`, architecturalHub uses `isHub` — see preset descriptions) to read fanIn labels via stats system. Breaking change — schema migration to drop fields. Cleaner final state.

**Recommended: Option B**, but gated on Phase 1 being live first (so consumers can switch over before fields are dropped).

## Phase 4: Calibration round after first live reindex (+ deferred confidence)

Threshold names ('isolated', 'popular', 'hub') are guesses BEFORE seeing actual distribution. Per .claude/rules/.local/ pattern used for git signals: after first reindex with new labels, inspect `get_index_metrics` per-language labelMap, adjust names to match the percentile reality.

Specifically watch for:
- Long-tail distributions where p95 > p75 by 10x — name should reflect 'extreme' (e.g. 'god-method' vs 'orchestrator')
- Bimodal distributions (lots of leaves + few hubs) — labels at low percentiles may collapse
- Polyglot variance — TypeScript graph shape differs from Python; per-language labelMap calibration may differ

**Plus deferred confidence decisions:**

- **pageRank confidence** — activate if small projects (<50 method nodes) show misleading 'critical' labels in live data. Would require introducing `codegraph.chunk.graphSizeAtCompute` sentinel as support key. Skip unless evidence appears.
- **transitiveImpact saturation flag** — algorithmic boundary, not statistical. If live distribution shows many files hitting depth cap=5 (`transitiveImpact == cap`), introduce `codegraph.file.transitiveImpactSaturated` boolean rendered as label modifier `"systemic (saturated)"`.

Outcome: refined labels committed; documented in website/docs/signals/codegraph.md.

## Phase 5: Live verification

| What | Where |
|---|---|
| Unit: buildSignalMetrics returns codegraph entries (including connectionCount) | tests/core/domains/explore/queries/index-metrics.test.ts |
| Unit: label resolver matches codegraph values | tests/core/domains/explore/reranker.test.ts |
| Unit: instability confidence clamps 'unstable' at connectionCount<p25 | tests/core/domains/explore/reranker.test.ts confidence path |
| Live: get_index_metrics on tea-rags-self shows codegraph.* with labelMap | mcp__tea-rags__get_index_metrics |
| Live: semantic_search with architecturalHub preset shows isHub label in rankingOverlay | mcp__tea-rags__semantic_search rerank=architecturalHub |
| Live: confidence dampening prevents 'unstable' label on connectionCount=1 file | manual probe with synthetic file |

---

## Discussion-required items (BEFORE implementation)

1. **Label naming** — subjective. Walk through proposed labels per signal, agree on naming convention.
2. **Phase 3 isHub/isLeaf decision** — A vs B is architectural choice. Need owner to commit.
3. **Phase 2 connectionCount placement** — confirm new raw signal goes into codegraph payload-signals.ts (as proposed), not into a separate aggregates file.
4. **Phase order** — Phase 1+2 can ship together (one schema bump adds 1 new field + labels). Phase 3 separate (breaking). Phase 4 reactive after live data.

## Skippable phases

- Phase 4 calibration — pure follow-up, can defer indefinitely if guess labels are acceptable.
- Phase 3 isHub/isLeaf refactor — skip if Option A consensus is sufficient.
- Phase 4 deferred confidence (pageRank, transitiveImpact saturation) — skip unless live evidence appears.

## Architecture references

- src/core/domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/payload-signals.ts — descriptor file
- src/core/domains/trajectory/git/payload-signals.ts — example: descriptors with stats.labels + stats.confidence
- src/core/domains/explore/queries/index-metrics.ts:57 — silent skip without labels
- src/core/domains/explore/reranker.ts:561 — label resolver
- .claude/rules/signal-confidence.md — confidence procedure
- .claude/rules/imports-field-semantics.md — codegraph.file.fanOut canonical efferent-coupling source

Parent epic: tea-rags-mcp-l26
Triggered by: live discovery 2026-05-21 — codegraph signals missing from metrics + overlay labels.
Brainstorm refined: 2026-05-21 — alpha-blending excluded (file/chunk measure different graphs); Phase 2 narrowed to instability + connectionCount only; pageRank/transitiveImpact saturation deferred to Phase 4.
close_reason: Closed via dbdec9ef on fix/codegraph-nested-payload: stats.labels on 7 numeric codegraph descriptors, new codegraph.file.connectionCount raw signal (fanIn+fanOut) + percentilesToCompute=[10], instability confidence block referencing connectionCount as support. isHub/isLeaf refactor deferred to follow-up task. 13 new tests green, full suite 5824 passed.

title: Ruby walker: method-chain calls not extracted (params.require(:x).permit(...))
description: huginn ApplicationController#agent_params has body 'params.require(:agent).permit(...)' which is a 2-link method chain (require returns params, permit on result). get_callees returns []. Either: (1) chain receivers are dropped (similar to Go chained receiver handling), or (2) Ruby walker doesn't emit calls when receiver is a method call. Validate by adding chained-call test case from huginn ScenarioImport / ApplicationController.
close_reason: walker correctly extracts chained calls (3 regression tests added in tests/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/extraction/ruby-walker.test.ts). The user-visible symptom (get_callees=[]) is RESOLVER behavior — receiver-set drop guard (bug lttd) intentionally drops chained-receiver calls with no Zeitwerk/local-binding/import resolution to prevent cross-language false positives. agent_params chain has receivers 'params' and 'params.require(:agent)' — neither constants nor locally-bound — so resolver drops both edges by design (see tests in describe block 'receiver-set drops edge when no resolution succeeds (bug lttd)' lines 1098-1214). Raising recall here without regressing lttd needs a new design decision, not a walker fix.

title: Ruby codegraph: chunk.fanIn metadata mismatches get_callers edges count
description: huginn WebRequestConcern#user_agent has codegraph.chunk.fanIn=6 in payload (file-level fanIn=6, transitiveImpact=5) but get_callers returns []. Means the metric was computed during enrichment from edges that get_callers cannot return. Either edges got pruned post-metric or edges are stored under different symbolId. Investigate edge-to-metric pipeline: who computes fanIn vs who serves edges, and why they disagree.

comments.author: Arthur Korochansky
comments.text:
Investigation outcome (Outcome B — resolved by recent fixes, regression guard added).

Findings:
- Both buildChunkSignals (computes chunk.fanIn) and getCallers query the SAME table cg_symbols_edges_method with the same predicate (WHERE target_symbol_id = ?). They cannot diverge in the live code path.
- Reported huginn discrepancy (chunk.fanIn=6 vs get_callers=[]) was a stale-payload artifact: huginn was indexed before recent Ruby resolver fixes (8fnu/lttd/pl7k/bdvm) which made the resolver stricter. Qdrant payload retained old fanIn values; live edge table now correctly returns empty for that symbolId.
- No code change needed in production paths.

Regression guard added at tests/core/domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/provider-fanin-edges-invariant.test.ts (4 tests, all passing). Covers:
  1. Multi-caller TS shape - fanIn equals getCallers length when method has multiple callers.
  2. Zero-caller TS shape - fanIn=0 and getCallers=[] both hold.
  3. Incremental upsert - fanIn stays consistent with getCallers after adding a caller.
  4. Ruby module/concern shape - exercises real walker + RubyCallResolver path with WebRequestConcern-style fixture.

To regenerate huginn correctness: mcp__tea-rags__index_codebase project=huginn forceReindex=true.

title: Ruby chunker scope-tracking vs codegraph walker scope-tracking mismatch
description: sinatra: chunk symbolId for def escape inside Rack::Protection::EscapedParams is reported as 'Rack#escape' (only outermost module) while codegraph correctly resolves to 'Rack::Protection::EscapedParams#escape'. As a result get_callers('Rack#escape') returns [] but the chunker emits this symbolId in find_symbol/hybrid_search output. Either chunker should emit fully-qualified scope (preferred) or codegraph should also use chunker's scoping. Inconsistency breaks the agent's ability to cross-reference symbol -> callgraph.
close_reason: Fixed in tree-sitter.ts + config.ts: added scopeContainerTypes + scopeSeparator to LanguageDefinition. Chunker now accumulates intermediate module/class scope names when emitting leaf method symbolId. Ruby config wires class/module/singleton_class with :: separator. Test: 1 new RED→GREEN case in tree-sitter-chunker.test.ts; matches codegraph's existing behaviour per .claude/rules/symbolid-convention.md.

title: Ruby resolver: shortName global fallback fabricates false-positive edges
description: Found via sinatra: Regexp.escape(domain) in host_authorization.rb resolves to Rack::Protection::EscapedParams#escape because shortName 'escape' is unique-enough to win global fallback. Same precision bug as fixed in Go (m46z), Java (ttv4), and pending in Rust (q4pz). Other example: object.map { |o| escape(o) } resolves to Sinatra::Streaming::Stream#map (cross-module). Fix: drop edge when receiver is set but no import/include match (Java/Go pattern).
close_reason: Fixed in ruby-resolver.ts: receiver-set drop guard returns null when no import/zeitwerk/local-binding match (mirrors Java/Go pattern). Defense-in-depth language filter (isRubyPath) applied to bare-call fallback. Tests: 5 new RED→GREEN in ruby-resolver.test.ts; 1 existing test updated to assert new business logic.

title: Ruby walker: duplicate call edges from nested enclosing scopes
description: Found via sinatra get_callers(Rack::Protection::EscapedParams#escape): a single call site (e.g. escape(hash) inside #handle method) produces 4 edges — one per enclosing scope: Rack, Rack::Protection, Rack::Protection::EscapedParams, AND Rack::Protection::EscapedParams#handle. Walker should emit ONLY the innermost containing function/method (or the innermost class/module if call is module-level). Impact: callers/callees results inflated 3-4x, breaks fanIn/fanOut metrics, breaks reranker analytics.
close_reason: Fixed in ruby-walker.ts: assignCallsToInnermostChunks routes each call to the smallest containing chunk (tie-break by scope depth). Tests: 2 new RED→GREEN cases added in ruby-walker.test.ts.

title: Ruby codegraph validation epic
description: Comprehensive Ruby codegraph testing on huginn (Rails OSS) + sinatra (pure Ruby OSS). Test checklist: find_cycles file/method, 10 distinct get_callers, 10 distinct get_callees, symmetry roundtrip, self-recursion, mutual recursion, polymorphism dispatch, metaprogramming corner cases (method_missing, define_method, send), generic delegation. Iterate fixes via TDD subagent until 100% green.

title: Rust resolver has same precision bug as Go/Java + needs receiver-type tracking
description: rust-resolver.ts lines 45-47 fall back to global lookupByShortName even when call receiver is set but no import matches - the same pattern that produced false-positive cycles in Go and Java. Not surfaced on ripgrep test corpus because method names there are mostly unique, but the bug is structural: any Rust project with duplicated method shortnames across distinct types will mis-route edges. Why: e.g. a local variable of type Vec calling push will match any unique struct having a push method. How to apply: 1 precision fix mirroring Go and Java - when receiver is non-null and no import matches drop the edge or scope-filter fallback like Java. 2 follow-up recall fix: rust-walker collects local var declarations and impl-block receiver types, emit FileExtraction with receiver and local bindings. rust-resolver consults bindings to look up type member directly. Mirror TS classFieldTypes pattern.
close_reason:
Fixed alongside c5by in rust-resolver.ts.

CHANGES:
- src/core/domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/resolvers/rust/rust-resolver.ts: added lookupEnclosingMember helper (mirrors java-resolver/9t8z pattern). Three resolution improvements:
  1. self.method() now resolves to <enclosingType>#method via callerScope BEFORE global short-name lookup.
  2. Bare method() inside impl block probes enclosing-type first.
  3. localBindings type-bound receivers (obj: Worker) drop the edge when the type lacks the member — no global short-name fallback (mirrors Java 9t8z / Go e6xx "drop unsafe fallback when receiver type known but member missing").

TESTS:
- tests/core/domains/trajectory/codegraph/symbols/resolvers/rust/rust-resolver.test.ts: 3 new tests covering self.clone() routing, drop on type-bound miss, bare helper() inside impl.

All 5304 tests green.

title: Java receiver-type tracking for cross-receiver method calls (full recall fix for ttv4)
description: Current state (post-ttv4 fix): Java resolver scope-filtered short-name fallback resolves Bar.method() with wildcard imports, but drops 'cs.charAt(i)' (local CharSequence var) and 'this.foo.method()' chained calls. Why: Java walker does not track local variable types or field declarations, so resolver cannot know dynamic receiver type. How to apply: (1) java-walker collects class field declarations (similar to TS collectClassFieldTypes) and local var declarations inside method bodies, emits FileExtraction.classFieldTypes + per-chunk localBindings. (2) java-resolver consults classFieldTypes for 'this.field.method()' and localBindings for 'localVar.method()'. (3) For chained 'a.b.c.method()' — track return types of common patterns (e.g. builder pattern). Mirror TS pattern. Verify commons-lang StringUtils#isBlank callees include Character.isWhitespace and CharSequence#charAt after fix.
close_reason: Fixed + integration-verified on commons-lang. Param/field/local-var type tracking (commit b33d239c) + java.lang static whitelist (commit 6d6c4ab1). Live: StringUtils.isBlank callees now include CharSequence#charAt (cs param) + Character.isWhitespace (java.lang static) + StringUtils.length. Unit 5931 green, negatives drop (no FP).

title: Go receiver-type tracking for cross-receiver method calls (full recall fix for m46z)
description: Current state (post-m46z fix): Go resolver drops edges when receiver != null and no import matches. This eliminates false positives but also drops legitimate edges like 'engine.Use()' inside func Default() (Default() callees now miss Engine#Use and Engine#With) and self-receiver intra-class calls 'c.GetQuery()' inside (c *Context) Query(). Why: Go does NOT have explicit 'self/this' keyword — receivers are user-named (c, s, b). Without tracking receiver var name + struct type per method, resolver cannot know that 'c.X()' in Context method body should resolve to Context#X. How to apply: (1) go-walker emits per-chunk localBindings: map of receiver-var-name -> receiver-type (parse method_declaration.receiver). (2) Additionally collect local var declarations 'var x Foo' and short var decls 'x := Foo{}' inside method bodies — populate same localBindings. (3) go-resolver: when receiver matches a localBindings key, look up <type>#<member> directly. Mirror TS classFieldTypes pattern. Verify gin Default() callees include Engine#Use and Engine#With after fix.
close_reason: Fixed + integration-verified on gin. Receiver/self-receiver + var-literal bindings (commit 7ed1afa1), function return-type binding for x:=New() (commit 18027ff6). Live gin: Default() callees now include Engine#Use + Engine#With via engine:=New(); Context#GetQuery→GetQueryArray. Unit suite 5915 green, no false edges.

title: Java resolver creates false-positive edges by ignoring receiver type
description: Java cycle (RandomUtils#randomBytes, RandomUtils.nextBytes) is a false positive. Code: instance method RandomUtils#randomBytes calls random().nextBytes(result) where random() returns java.util.Random - so the real callee is java.util.Random#nextBytes (external). Resolver instead matches the shortName 'nextBytes' against the same-class static method RandomUtils.nextBytes, fabricating the cycle. Why: Java resolver lacks receiver-type tracking (similar to TS classFieldTypes pattern). For chained calls like local().method() it must infer the return type of local() before resolving method. How to apply: extend java-walker to collect field/local declared types (mirroring collectClassFieldTypes for TS), java-resolver to consult them for non-static receivers, and drop edges where the receiver type doesn't match the candidate's owning class. Verify via commons-lang find_cycles - the bogus randomBytes/nextBytes cycle should disappear.
close_reason: fixed: Go walker emits Receiver#Method, Go resolver drops chained-receiver fallback; Java resolver filters short-name fallback by candidate scope match

title: Go walker emits symbolId without Receiver# prefix - false-positive cycles, broken cross-receiver edges
description: Go method extraction puts top-level shortName as symbolId (e.g. 'Query', 'GetQuery') instead of receiver-qualified form 'Context#Query' per symbolid-convention.md. Why: discovered while running find_cycles on gin codebase - resolver returned a false-positive cycle (initQueryCache, GetQueryArray, GetQuery, Query) for what is actually a linear chain Query -> GetQuery -> GetQueryArray -> initQueryCache. Several Go packages declare different methods with the same shortName (Context.Query vs Bind.Query etc.), and shortName-only lookup merges them, fabricating cycles. Convention rule (.claude/rules/symbolid-convention.md): Go instance methods MUST be 'Receiver#Method', top-level functions stay bare. How to apply: go-walker.ts nameOf for method_declaration must compose Receiver#Method using the receiver type token, mirroring rb/ts walker behavior. Update go-resolver to look up by the qualified form. Verify with gin find_cycles - the bogus Query cycle should disappear.
close_reason: fixed: Go walker emits Receiver#Method, Go resolver drops chained-receiver fallback; Java resolver filters short-name fallback by candidate scope match

title: codegraph signals not consumed by reranker (transitiveImpact/fanIn/fanOut weights produce score=0)
description: Reranker schema lists weight keys transitiveImpact/fanIn/fanOut/pageRank/isHub/isLeaf/instability and codegraph presets (architecturalHub/blastRadius/entryPoint) reference them, but: (1) custom rerank with {transitiveImpact: 1, fanIn: 1, fanOut: 1} yields score=0 for ALL results — derived signals do not extract from codegraph payload; (2) architecturalHub overlay outputs only git signals (commitCount, ageDays, bugFixRate) — codegraph keys missing from overlayMask. Why: codegraph payload (codegraph.chunk.fanIn/fanOut/pageRank, codegraph.file.transitiveImpact/instability/isHub/isLeaf) is computed at index time but reranker derived-signal classes never read it back at query time. How to apply: (a) add derived signal classes that read codegraph.*.* payload keys, (b) extend codegraph preset overlayMasks to surface those signals in rankingOverlay, (c) wire them into composition.
close_reason: Fixed: codegraph derived signals now read from real nested Qdrant payload shape (codegraph.symbols.file/chunk) via new codegraph-specific accessor helpers (mirror of git helpers). All 10 signals migrated. Commit 34f886aedf7f3582f5dc91bcc70023889d18a04e.

title: codegraph signals not exposed in filter schema (fanIn/fanOut/transitiveImpact/pageRank)
description: Filters tea-rags://schema/filters lists only git.file.* and git.chunk.* signals. codegraph.chunk.fanIn/fanOut/pageRank and codegraph.file.transitiveImpact/instability/isHub/isLeaf are computed at index time and visible in payload, but cannot be used in filter clauses. Why: discovered while looking for hub chunks across Go/Java/Rust — filter {key: 'codegraph.chunk.fanOut', range:{gte: 3}} returns 0 results despite payload visibly containing fanOut=3 for gin.go#Use. How to apply: extend filter schema doc + ensure Qdrant payload index covers codegraph paths so filter clauses work. Also surface them in get_index_metrics labelMaps so users can pick reasonable percentile thresholds.
close_reason: Closed via 973620c5 on fix/codegraph-nested-payload: codegraph trajectory now declares 8 typed filter descriptors (minFanIn/minFanOut/minPageRank/minInstability/minTransitiveImpact/minConnectionCount + isHub/isLeaf) that translate logical names to nested Qdrant paths codegraph.symbols.{file,chunk}.codegraph.{file,chunk}.X. Mirrors git's typed-filter pattern. 17 new tests, full suite 5841 passed.

title: codegraph tools accept project/collection params (get_callers, get_callees, find_cycles)
description: get_callers, get_callees, find_cycles currently accept only 'path' parameter. All other tea-rags tools accept project (recommended), collection, or path with resolution priority collection > project > path. This inconsistency forces callers to know absolute paths and breaks the 'project alias survives path moves' contract. Why: discovered while testing find_cycles/get_callers across other-language projects — passing project=tea-rags-worktree fails with InputValidationError 'expected string, received undefined'. Fix: add project + collection optional params to all three tool schemas + use the same Registry.resolveCollection() pattern as find_symbol/hybrid_search. How to apply: change mcp/tools/codegraph/*.ts (or wherever the three tools are wired), keep path as backward-compatible fallback.
close_reason: Closed via fcb02617 on fix/codegraph-nested-payload: get_callers/get_callees/find_cycles теперь принимают {collection?,project?,path?} с приоритетом collection > project > path. resolveCollection() переиспользован из find_symbol-pipeline через injected CollectionRegistry. InputValidationError subclasses при отсутствии триады. 5 новых тестов + 7 обновлённых; полный suite 5847 passed.

title: Regression: form.search_params/page/per edges drop after slice 3 walker changes
description: After commit 89ac22aa (send/&block/delegate/habtm/define_method + AR chain guard), Ruby walker still emits CallRefs for chained-arg sub-calls like form.search_params / form.page / form.per inside a chained AR-relation expression (verified via standalone walker test). However the corresponding edges DON'T appear in cg_symbols_edges_method, leaving get_callers(Product::IndexForm#search_params) and get_callers(PaginatableForm#page) returning []. Where they vanish is unclear — resolver path looks correct (receiver='form' is lowercase, no AR-chain match, short_name lookup is unique). Hypothesis: chunk-line attribution drift moves the line containing the chained expression OUTSIDE the def's chunk range, so calls get attributed to the class scope (which DOES contain the call_expression text but NOT as a separate edge). Repro: index taxdome-marketplace, query get_callees(ProductsController#index) — should show form.* edges, returns only index_meta. Investigation steps: (1) extract chunk line-ranges for ProductsController#index from chunker; (2) verify whether walker CallRef startLine falls inside chunk; (3) check if duplicate-edge dedup at provider level is too aggressive.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ruby: block arg &:method_name (Symbol#to_proc) edge extraction
description: users.each(&:save) — &:save desugars to { |u| u.save }. Walker currently misses this call. Walk argument_list for block_argument nodes whose child is a simple_symbol; emit an extra CallRef as if it were 'item.<symbol>' invoked once. Resolver path unchanged. Common Ruby idiom — significant edge loss without this. Effort: small (15 lines). Acceptance: 'users.each(&:save)' yields a CallRef with member='save' and no receiver (let resolver fall through to type tracking on iterator-element-type — out of scope for first pass).
close_reason: Closed

title: Ruby: delegate :method, to: :other macro
description: class User; delegate :email, :name, to: :profile; end — emits forwarder methods #email and #name on User that delegate to @profile.email/@profile.name. Currently delegate macro is ignored — User#email isn't indexed, callers using user.email mis-resolve via global short-name. Implementation: add 'delegate' to RUBY_DSL_MACROS BUT it differs from attr_*: the symbols are forwarders only — no real method body. We just need them in cg_symbols so callers find SOMETHING; resolver target points at User#email with no method-level body chunk (file-only edge OK). Bonus: optionally trace through to :other_class via second-arg parsing to get the actual receiver type. Effort: small (5 lines + first-pass support). Acceptance: User#email exists in cg_symbols when user.rb declares 'delegate :email, to: :profile'.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ruby: send(:method_name) + public_send + __send__ literal-arg parsing
description: user.send(:save) and similar dynamic dispatch with LITERAL symbol/string argument are statically resolvable. Walker should treat call.method='send'/'public_send'/'__send__' with first arg being simple_symbol or string literal as if it were a direct method call on the receiver (member = literal text). Resolver path unchanged. Skip when arg is variable/expression (truly dynamic). Effort: small (10 lines in collectRubyCalls). Acceptance: 'user.send(:save)' produces an edge to User#save (or equivalent) just like 'user.save' would.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ruby resolver: walk inheritance chain for ProductPolicy → AbstractPolicy
description: ProductPolicy.authorize!(...) calls the inherited class method from AbstractPolicy.authorize! defined in class << self block. Resolver looks up authorize! restricted to ProductPolicy's file, finds nothing, falls back to global short-name lookup which is ambiguous (AbstractPolicy.authorize! + AbstractPolicy#authorize!) → returns null. Need to walk the superclass chain from ProductPolicy's class declaration to find inherited class/instance methods. Tree-sitter-ruby exposes class header with optional 'superclass' field already extracted as zeitwerk: import. Implementation: when resolveByLocalType / class-method-on-constant lookup fails, fetch class hierarchy from symbol table (each class's fileScope already carries its inheritance chain via the superclass-as-import path) and retry the lookup against each ancestor. Acceptance: get_callers AbstractPolicy.authorize! returns non-empty for taxdome-marketplace (called transitively by ProductPolicy).
close_reason: Closed

title: Ruby AR Relation-chain receiver typing
description: Chained AR-relation calls like Model.where(...).first / Model.ransack(form).result(distinct: true) still get false-positive resolves because the local-bindings tracker only handles var = X.new(...) and direct AR finder shortcuts. The Relation builder methods (where/order/joins/select/group/having/includes/eager_load/preload/limit/offset/distinct/ransack) return ActiveRecord::Relation, and chained calls on Relation that produce instances (.first/.last/.find/.take) need awareness that the chain root is still the original Model. Implementation outline: (1) extend Ruby walker's collectLocalBindingsForChunk to recognize chained call expressions whose tail is an instance-returning finder AND whose root is a class constant; bind to root class. (2) In the resolver, when receiver is a chain-text containing AR Relation builder, skip ambiguous global short-name candidates that aren't methods on the chain root class — drop the edge rather than guess. Acceptance: Product.ransack(form).result(distinct:true) no longer FP-resolves to AbstractPolicy#result.
close_reason: Closed

title: Cross-language receiver type tracking: Ruby/Java/Go/Rust
description: Phase 1+2 done for Python. Evaluate need per language: TypeScript already has class-field-types resolver (works). Ruby has Zeitwerk autoload (separate concern). Java/Go/Rust are strongly typed — should be less ambiguity inherent. Plan: index gin/commons-lang/ripgrep/taxdome-marketplace with current resolver, measure false-positive rate per language via spot-check sample edges, decide which need type tracking. Could be e.g. Ruby only or none. Follow-up to Python Phase 1+2.
close_reason: Eval complete + Rust fixed. Go(6g9c)/Java(cvv9)/Python(rjuc,yrs0)/TS(x6ta,2v16) receiver-tracking done via dedicated beads. Ruby ALREADY adequate (walker+resolver: .new/AR-finders/YARD, ancestor-walk/MRO, drop-guards). Rust GAP fixed (743171fa): walker now emits localBindings+classFieldTypes, resolver self.field branch added — integration-verified on ripgrep (Parser#find_short→FlagMap#find via self.map.find). Remaining Rust let-ctor reference-return edge filed as follow-up.

title: Python receiver type tracking: self.field cross-method (Phase 3)
description: After Phase 1 (local var = ClassName() assignment) and Phase 2 (PEP 526 + arg type hints) land, the next class of Python receiver resolution involves cross-method state. Pattern: __init__ binds self.service = SomeService(); then handle() calls self.service.process(). Resolver must track instance fields as class-level state and use them when resolving method calls on self.X. Requires 2-pass walker: pass-1 builds Map<className, Map<fieldName, typeName>> from __init__ AsignmentStatements; pass-2 resolves method calls. Cross-method state, more complex than local-scope tracking. Impact estimate: +10% precision for Django/DRF view classes with stateful fields. Follow-up to current Phase 1+2 iteration.
close_reason: Fixed + integration-verified on flask. self.field from __init__ constructor tracked as class-level classFieldTypes (commit 959725e7); external best-effort target for known stdlib field types (commit 608e28ed). Live flask: FlaskClient#open callees now include ExitStack#close + ExitStack#enter_context via self._context_stack=ExitStack(). Unit 5946 green, unknown-type field still drops (no FP).

title: Python MRO / inheritance walk in resolver (Phase 4)
description: Resolver should walk class hierarchy when method not found on direct class: A.method() where A extends B (resolved import), B extends C, etc. — search method up the chain. Cap'd by venv exclusion (won't descend into DRF/Django framework classes which are excluded). Currently if class declares method via inheritance from external base, resolver fails (correctly via Phase 1+2 strict path, but loses legit edges for in-project inheritance hierarchies). Impact: +5-10% for projects with deep inheritance (Django CBVs, service base classes). Follow-up to Phase 1+2.
close_reason: Fixed + integration-verified on flask. Resolver walks classExtends chain (self.method() + typed-receiver) when method not on direct class, in-project only, cycle-guarded (commit df2e19b3). Live flask: App#_find_error_handler → Scaffold._get_exc_class_and_code (App extends Scaffold, inherited staticmethod). Unit 5951 green; external base + cycle drop (no FP).

title: Streaming Tarjan SCC + PageRank for >50k edges
description: Current implementation: collectAdjacency materializes full Map<string,string[]> in JS heap from streamAdjacency cursor, then tarjanScc runs recursive DFS on it. For projects with >50k method edges (e.g. monorepos like taxdome with 84k chunks) this becomes a JS heap bottleneck (~10MB for 25k edges, scales linearly). Algorithms inherently need full adjacency (Tarjan: DFS order; PageRank: iterative convergence over node vector). Options: (a) two-phase Tarjan with disk-backed intermediate state (Path-based SCC algorithm); (b) PageRank can run with streaming row-based updates per iteration if we persist value vector on disk between iterations; (c) push computation into DuckDB recursive CTE — DuckDB supports recursive WITH for SCC discovery. Option (c) likely cleanest — DuckDB handles memory pressure natively. Trigger threshold: profile real projects, switch implementation when adjacency exceeds N nodes/edges. Follow-up to OOM streaming fix (tea-rags-mcp-wajk).
close_reason: 30GB allocation eliminated; streaming Tarjan + cross-process lock fix landed in main (1d597534 + xa34 chain). Streaming-enrichment branch validates allocation bounded under taxdome scale. Confirmed by user 2026-05-29.

title: [epic] Codegraph Slice 4: static complexity track + blocked D5 composites
description:

⚠️ МОЖЕТ БЫТЬ ОСОЗНАННО ПРОПУЩЕН

Before opening a worktree, confirm with the owner: (1) are the 3 blocked D5 composites (changeRisk, safeToRefactor, couplingComplexity) actually valuable enough to ship the complexity track now, or can we live without them; (2) is the McCabe + Cognitive split (one visitor pass per language across 8 languages) the right level of investment given Yatish 2020 says complexity AUC is 54% vs process metrics 95%; (3) any user-visible feature path that depends on this (Slice 6 path tracing lists complexity as enrichment dependency — confirm path tracing actually NEEDS it vs. nice-to-have). Lives in src/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/chunker/complexity/. Decision-point node sets overlap entirely between McCabe and cognitive; cognitive adds only +3 concepts (nesting penalty, boolean sequence collapse, elsif flat-switch guard). Single migration cycle.

2. **C-STATS** (tea-rags-mcp-jjr8) — file aggregates (file.cyclomaticMax/Sum, file.cognitiveMax/Avg) + fileComplexity derived. Lives in src/core/domains/trajectory/static/stats/complexity-aggregates.ts. Different layer (stats accumulator), file-level payload, NOT a D5 blocker. Ships independently AFTER C-AST.

3. **D5(remaining)** (tea-rags-mcp-d8fk) — changeRisk + safeToRefactor + couplingComplexity composite presets. Lives in src/core/domains/trajectory/composite/presets/. Blocked by C-AST only (uses chunk-level signals, not file-level).

DAG:
  C-AST ───→ C-STATS  (parallel with D5-rest)
  C-AST ───→ D5(remaining)

Architecture: complexity is per-chunk scalar from tree-sitter AST walk (SAME pass as chunking, NEW visitor in chunker/complexity/). Lives in domains/trajectory/static/, NOT codegraph — independent from graph structure. Signals: payload.chunk.cyclomatic, payload.chunk.cognitive (C-AST); payload.file.cyclomaticMax/Sum, payload.file.cognitiveMax/Avg (C-STATS).

Ruby specifics covered in C-AST tests: postfix modifiers (if_modifier, unless_modifier, while_modifier, until_modifier — distinct tree-sitter node types), case/in pattern matching (Ruby 3.0+, opportunistic), block iterators (each/map/tap) DO NOT contribute nesting per SonarQube convention.

Hooks claim chunked methods, visitor runs on each method node, results written to BodyChunkResult.metadata.{cyclomatic,cognitive}, piped through chunker → file-processor → StaticPayloadBuilder → payload.chunk.{cyclomatic,cognitive}. Adds 2 raw payload signals (chunk.cyclomatic, chunk.cognitive with stats labels) + 2 derived signals (complexity bound=10, cognitiveLoad bound=15 per SonarQube standard).

Rationale for merging C1+C2 (decided 2026-05-21): single visitor pass, single migration cycle, identical decision-point node sets between cyclomatic and cognitive (overlap huge). Cognitive adds only +3 concepts on top of cyclomatic-only visitor (nesting depth, boolean sequence collapse, elsif flat-switch) — splitting into two phases would force two reindex cycles for no inkremental D5 unblock value.

Ruby specifics (covered in tests): postfix modifiers (if_modifier/unless_modifier/while_modifier/until_modifier), case/in pattern matching (Ruby 3.0+, opportunistic), block iterators (each/map/tap) DO NOT contribute nesting per SonarQube. title: Codegraph: refactor extraEnrichmentProviders shim → use TrajectoryRegistry directly in IngestFacade
description:
Slice 1 quick-fix in IngestFacade.buildIngestPipeline:

- Added 'extraEnrichmentProviders?: EnrichmentProvider[]' to IngestFacadeDeps.
- Bootstrap passes registry.getAllEnrichmentProviders().filter(p => p.key !== 'git').
- IngestFacade still hardcodes 'new GitEnrichmentProvider(config)' separately because git has its own trajectoryConfig flow.

Why this is invasive костыль:
1. IngestFacade has two paths for enrichment providers: hardcoded git + injected 'extra' list. Future providers (codegraph.symbols, slice 5 temporal, etc.) all flow via 'extra' while git is special-cased.
2. Bootstrap has to manually filter 'p.key !== git' to avoid double-instantiation.
3. The registry already aggregates EnrichmentProvider list via getAllEnrichmentProviders(); IngestFacade should consume that source of truth.

Slice 2 task: move git provider wiring out of IngestFacade into a trajectory factory (GitTrajectory creates provider given config), register that trajectory with proper config in composition, then IngestFacade consumes registry.getAllEnrichmentProviders() directly. Removes 'extraEnrichmentProviders' field entirely.

Constraints:
- GitEnrichmentProvider constructor needs trajectoryConfig.trajectoryGit + squashOpts — must thread through composition or trajectory-init path.
- Don't break existing tests that mock IngestFacadeDeps shape.

Parent: tea-rags-mcp-l26
close_reason: Landed in commit 61b20eed — IngestFacade now reads enrichment providers from TrajectoryRegistry via deps.enrichmentProviders. GitEnrichmentProvider construction moved to GitTrajectory (composition time). Bootstrap filters by enableGitMetadata at one site. 4443 tests pass.

title: Codegraph: graceful DuckDB lock conflict handling (multi-instance MCP)
description:
Bug discovered during Slice 1 MCP integration testing on tea-rags.

Symptom: when multiple tea-rags MCP processes coexist (e.g. Claude Code respawn or multiple IDE sessions), only the first one can open ~/.tea-rags/codegraph.duckdb — subsequent processes crash on wireCodegraph with 'IO Error: Could not set lock on file ... Conflicting lock is held'.

Root cause: DuckDB is single-writer per file. wireCodegraph eagerly opens it at bootstrap; if another tea-rags process is already holding the lock, the new one fails to start entirely.

Slice 2 options:
1. Catch lock error in wireCodegraph -> log warning -> return undefined (codegraph gracefully disabled for that instance; first-comer gets it).
2. Lazy DB open on first query/sink call instead of bootstrap-time eager open.
3. Per-collection DBs (current is per-App) — but App can serve multiple collections so this is per-request scope.
4. DuckDB shared connection daemon (mirror onnx-daemon pattern).
5. Move to SQLite or other multi-process-safe backend for slice 1 — overkill.

Recommended: option (1) for slice 1 compatibility, plan option (4) for v2 architecture if multi-tenant MCP becomes common.

Parent: tea-rags-mcp-l26
close_reason: Graceful lock handling (option 1) already implemented in src/bootstrap/factory.ts:210-227 (wireCodegraph): try { graphDb.init() + runMigrations } catch (err) { stderr.write('[tea-rags] codegraph disabled: failed to open ... Set CODEGRAPH_ENABLED=false to silence, or kill the other tea-rags process holding the lock'); return undefined }. First-comer keeps codegraph; later spawns serve search/index without it. Walker functions ARE NOT INVOKED when codegraph is disabled (composition gate at composition.ts:54-55 — codegraph trajectory not registered → coordinator does not get the provider → processFiles never runs → walkers never execute). Option 4 (shared DuckDB daemon, mirror of ONNX one) deferred to Slice 4+ follow-on if/when multi-tenant MCP becomes common.

title: [epic] Integration tests: golden coverage gaps
description:
Add integration tests for new API surface that landed during SOLID refactor (ExploreFacade, infra modules) — currently 0 coverage at integration level. Goal: catch wiring regressions before they ship.

Sub-tasks (file separately as children of this sub-epic):

ExploreFacade API:
- A1. findSymbol (most-used MCP search tool — symbol lookup + relativePath outline)
- A2. hybridSearch (BM25 + dense, validates sparse vector path)
- A3. findSimilar (recommend sub-query, positive/negative inputs)
- A4. rankChunks (scroll-based rank without vector)
- A5. getIndexMetrics (signal metrics endpoint)

New infrastructure:
- B1. CollectionRegistry — register_project / list_projects / unregister_project alias workflow
- B2. EmbeddingModelGuard — model mismatch detection
- B3. SchemaDriftMonitor — payload version tracking + drift warning attachment
- B4. Migrator end-to-end — driven via IngestFacade (replaces the skip'd standalone tests)
- B5. StatsRecomputeService — lazy percentile backfill triggered at rerank time

Quality extensions to existing:
- C1. Rerank presets (codeReview, techDebt, hotspots, ownership) — verify overlay fields attached
- C2. level=file vs level=chunk semantics — git time filters
- C3. Sparse vector / BM25 path when enableHybridSearch=true
- C4. TypeScript/Python deep AST chunking quality (not just presence)
- C5. Real git history fidelity — bugFixRate / churnVolatility / blame on a multi-author repo
- C6. Multi-collection isolation — two registered projects, isolated queries

title: Integration tests: restore skip'd SnapshotMigrator + SchemaManager scenarios
description:
Two test blocks were skip()'d during the post-SOLID mechanical rewrite because their underlying APIs changed signature:

1. tests/integration/suites/14-parallel-sync.mjs (lines ~139-194):
   Old: new SnapshotMigrator(snapshotDir, collectionName, codebasePath, shards) + migrator.needsMigration() + migrator.migrate()
   New: SnapshotMigrator(store) where store is SnapshotStoreAdapter(snapshotDir, collectionName); orchestrated by Migrator class with schema/sparse stores too
   Scenario value: v2 → v3 snapshot format upgrade with backup creation

2. tests/integration/suites/16-schema-delete-optimization.mjs (lines ~38-72):
   Old: schemaManager.getSchemaVersion(collection) + schemaManager.ensureCurrentSchema(collection) standalone
   New: SchemaManager(qdrant, schemaVersion, sparseVersion) — migration driven by Migrator class via IngestFacade
   Scenario value: 0→latest schema migration with payload index creation

Restore both by:
- Constructing the full Migrator graph (SnapshotStoreAdapter + IndexStoreAdapter + SparseStoreAdapter + Migrator)
- OR via IngestFacade end-to-end (cleaner — exercises real wiring)

Path of least surprise: drive via IngestFacade.indexCodebase on a fresh collection, assert migration markers in payload + Qdrant indexes were created.

- Has 0 coverage of new ExploreFacade API (findSymbol, hybridSearch, findSimilar, rankChunks, getIndexMetrics) and new infra (CollectionRegistry, EmbeddingModelGuard, SchemaDriftMonitor, Migrator end-to-end, StatsRecomputeService)

Child issues track each phase. Order matters: #1 must land first (otherwise nothing runs anywhere); #2 validates #1; #3 cleans technical debt; #4 expands coverage.

title: M2.D.1: investigate addUpsert legacy bug history
description: Plan Task 19 (M2.D.1). INVESTIGATION ONLY — no code changes. Document findings before deciding M2.D.2.
notes:
Investigation: pipeline-manager.ts addUpsert (lines 115-127, 13 lines incl JSDoc)

Code reality (worktree HEAD): addUpsert is a 3-line delegator:
  1. Check isRunning -> throw PipelineNotStartedError
  2. Wrap point into UpsertItem {type, id, point}
  3. Delegate to upsertAccumulator.add(item)

Bug history (full --follow log):
- ad376cf7 fix(pipeline): propagate batch errors instead of silently swallowing them
  → bug class: error propagation. WorkerPool.processWithRetry resolved with success=false,
    swallowing OllamaUnavailableError. Fix in worker-pool.ts + flush() of pipeline-manager.ts.
    NOT in addUpsert.
- 7fe355d8 fix(ingest): gate modified-file upsert on delete success per file
  → bug class: orphan duplication race. Delete failures + parallel upsert double-populated
    Qdrant. Fix in ReindexCoordinator + file-processor (sync/), NOT in addUpsert.
- 5896815a feat(ingest): ReindexCoordinator gates upsert
  → same root cause as 7fe355d8, upstream of addUpsert.
- 90127681 feat(pipeline): streaming chunk enrichment per-batch
  → bug class: serialization of git blame. Fix in EnrichmentCoordinator, NOT addUpsert.
- aaa2211e refactor(ingest): extract helpers (touched addUpsert site cosmetically only)
- a5d96fca feat: optimize delete operations (original introduction of addUpsert)
