Orchestrator slimmed to 45L (owns pause/resume try/finally). Phase B exceeds 60L target due to inherent two-wave parallel-await machinery + required pipelineLog timing; further split would fragment shared state (deletionOutcome, coordinator, addPromise). Extracted logDeleteSettled helper. RED tests from 9b9d6609 + 39 reindexing tests + 4308 full suite all green. type-check clean. GREEN commit 32a00ac9.

title: M2.E.1: investigate reindexing block 322-509 history
description: Plan Task 21 (M2.E.1). Identify bug class + decomposition phases.
notes:
Investigation: reindexing.ts::executeParallelPipelines (lines 195-352, 158 lines incl JSDoc)

Phase decomposition:
- Phase A (setup): 195-238 (44 lines)
  derive changedPaths/filesToDelete/addedFiles/modifiedFiles,
  initProcessing (ctx, chunker pool, pipeline), initChunkMap,
  build processOpts, logParallelStart, pauseOptimizer.
- Phase B (parallel L1+L2 execution): 239-321 (83 lines)
  L1: deletePromise || addPromise in parallel, await deletePromise,
  log partial-failure forensics, instantiate ReindexCoordinator + applyDeletionOutcome,
  L2: modifiedPromise gated on coordinator, await Promise.all([addPromise, modifiedPromise]),
  log ADD_AND_MODIFIED_COMPLETE.
- Phase C (partial-outcome assessment + stats + return): 322-342 (21 lines)
  read coordinator.hasBlockedPaths() / skippedFiles(),
  emit REINDEX_PARTIAL_COMPLETE if any, logPipelineStats, build result object.
- finally block (343-351): resumeOptimizer with catch-and-log (8 lines).

Bug class history on reindexing.ts (touching this region):
- 7fe355d8 fix(ingest): gate modified-file upsert on delete success per file
  → bug: orphan duplication race. Modified-file processing ran in parallel with
    L1 delete, but per-path delete failure left old chunks in collection while
    upsert added new ones — double-population. Fix: ReindexCoordinator instantiated
    AFTER deletePromise resolves, applyDeletionOutcome, modifiedOpts.coordinator
    gates upsert. Surfaces filesSkippedDueToDeleteFailure → stats.status="partial".
    This is PHASE B and PHASE C of the block.
- 6220f863 perf(qdrant): scroll+delete-by-IDs and optimizer pause
  → bug: WAL contention. Filter-based delete blocked concurrent upserts for minutes.
    Fix: pauseOptimizer/resumeOptimizer wrap try/finally around the whole parallel
    region. This is PHASE A (pause) and the finally block (resume).
- 6ff33cfa fix(ingest): delete chunks for newly ignored files during incremental reindex
  → bug class: filesToDelete construction was missing newlyIgnored. Fix in PHASE A
    (line 219 — filesToDelete includes newlyIgnored now).
- 6b5da2dd fix(ingest): scope enrichment to changed files and skip for deletion-only reindex
  → fixed upstream (executeDeletionOnly fast path, lines 90-98). Not in block.
- 1c271252 fix(ingest): update completedAt marker on incremental reindex
  → fix in finalizeReindex, NOT in block.

Bug class identified for T22 test: PARTIAL-OUTCOME REPORTING in Phase C.
If coordinator.hasBlockedPaths() / skippedFiles().length wiring drifts (off-by-one,
missed boolean, status not downgraded to "partial"), caller is silently lied to
about a successful reindex when files were skipped. This is the exact failure
class 7fe355d8 closed and is the most fragile contract in the block.

Recommendation: decompose into 3 private methods matching phases A/B/C.
Names: setupParallelExecution, runDeleteThenAddModified, assessParallelOutcome.
Each <60 lines. T22 RED test pins the partial-outcome contract (Phase C):
when ReindexCoordinator reports blocked paths, the returned object MUST carry
filesSkippedDueToDeleteFailure>0 so caller can downgrade stats.status to "partial".
close_reason: 3 phases identified (setup A:195-238, parallel execution B:239-321, partial-outcome assessment C:322-342) + finally(343-351). Bug class for T22: partial-outcome reporting drift (7fe355d8 contract). Decomp targets: setupParallelExecution, runDeleteThenAddModified, assessParallelOutcome.

title: Complete adaptive label clamp: collect referenced percentiles + scope-aware lookup
description:
Two gaps discovered during MCP-side testing of commit 654b8385 (adaptive label thresholds):

GAP 1 — Referenced percentiles not collected at index time:
The confidence.label.rules can reference any percentile via 'pN' strings
(e.g. 'p10'). Collection stats compute only percentiles declared in the
SUPPORT signal's stats.labels (currently p25/p50/p75/p95 for commitCount).
A rule with 'p10' falls back silently to its fallback number — adaptive
never fires for that rule.

FIX: extend collection-stats computation to union all percentile references
across ALL descriptors' confidence.label.rules.whenSupportBelow + 
confidence.score.adaptivePercentile, and ensure those percentiles are
computed for every support signal's stats.percentiles map.

GAP 2 — Scope mismatch (global vs language/scope):
preResolveConfidenceClamp in reranker.ts reads from collectionStats.perSignal
(global, no scope split). The label resolver itself reads from
collectionStats.perLanguage[language].get(key).{source|test} (scope-aware).
For test files the adaptive threshold should use test-scope percentile, not
global. Currently uses wrong stats source.

FIX: pass language + scope through to preResolveConfidenceClamp; look up
collectionStats.perLanguage[lang][git.{level}.{support}].{source|test}.percentiles[pct]
mirroring label-resolver scope detection.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. confidence.label.rule with 'pN' where N is not in support's labelMap
   still resolves adaptively (percentile collected during indexing).
2. Adaptive threshold for test files differs from source files when
   collection distribution differs by scope.
3. Regression test: query trigger file in taxdome ruby — adaptive
   threshold matches ruby.test.git.file.commitCount.percentiles[p25].

CROSS-REFS:
- Parent: tea-rags-mcp-vpn6
- Predecessor: 654b8385 (adaptive infrastructure landed)
- Discovery: MCP-side testing this session
close_reason: shipped: 80490ce6 + 01bb6981 — validateSignalDependencies, percentilesToCompute, scope-aware label-resolver in src/; signal-confidence rule operative

title: Adaptive label-clamp thresholds (whenSupportBelow accepts percentile strings)
description:
Score-side dampening was made adaptive in tea-rags-mcp-2h45 (commit 88a3534b) — reranker derives k from confidence.support p25 of collection stats. Label-side clamp rules still use STATIC whenSupportBelow numbers (e.g. 5, 10 hardcoded in bugFixRate descriptor).

Asymmetry pointed out by user during legacy migration testing.

OPTIONS:

A) Extend whenSupportBelow to accept percentile strings:
   rules: [
     { whenSupportBelow: 'p10', ceiling: 'healthy'    },
     { whenSupportBelow: 'p25', ceiling: 'concerning' },
   ]
   Reranker resolves to actual value from collection stats at apply time.
   Backwards compatible: number stays static, string is adaptive.

B) Derive label thresholds from score.threshold (no extra config):
   score: { threshold: 10 } → implicit rules <5 → first ceiling, <10 → second
   Less flexible but ZERO redundancy.

C) Document static-by-design — labels are categorical UX (severity tiers),
   not statistical fine-tuning. Keep as-is.

ACCEPTANCE (if Option A chosen):
- ConfidenceClampRule.whenSupportBelow: number | string
- Reranker resolves 'pN' strings via descriptor.stats.confidence.support
  percentile lookup before passing to label resolver
- Backwards compat: existing static numbers continue to work
- Test: adaptive label clamp with different collection-stats percentile distributions
- Update .claude/rules/signal-confidence.md threshold-picking guidance

CROSS-REFS:
- Score-side equivalent: tea-rags-mcp-2h45 (closed at 88a3534b)
- Discovery: user feedback during legacy migration in session
close_reason: Absorbed into ongoing legacy migration session — adaptive labels implemented inline alongside 6-signal migration in commit-to-be.

title: Restore adaptive confidence dampening for BugFixSignal (and unify with confidence.support)
description:
REGRESSION from epic tea-rags-mcp-vpn6 / Task 5 (commit 4ea7d279).

BACKGROUND:
Old BugFixSignal.extract:
  k = ctx?.dampeningThreshold ?? FALLBACK_THRESHOLD(10)
where ctx.dampeningThreshold = adaptive p25 of commitCount (from collectionStats via descriptor.dampeningSource = GIT_FILE_DAMPENING).
Static 10 was the FLOOR/FALLBACK when no collectionStats — not the primary value.

Task 5 refactor removed dampeningSource AND inverted precedence:
  k = ctx?.confidence?.score?.threshold ?? ctx?.dampeningThreshold ?? FALLBACK_K
Result: static threshold=10 from descriptor ALWAYS wins; ctx.dampeningThreshold never reached for bugFixRate; also dampeningSource removal means reranker resolveDampeningThreshold returns undefined for BugFixSignal.

IMPACT:
- For small codebases (tea-rags, p25 commitCount ≈ 1): OLD adaptive=1 → no dampening on 3 commits. NEW static=10 → (3/10)^2=9% weight. Ranking shift.
- For mature codebases (taxdome ruby, p25 commitCount ≈ 2): OLD adaptive=2 vs NEW static=10. Same direction, smaller magnitude.
- 'Numerical equivalence' acceptance criterion claimed in Task 5 commit/plan holds only when adaptive coincidentally equals 10.

DESIRED FIX (Variant B from analysis):
1. resolveDampeningThreshold reads adaptive from descriptor.stats.confidence.support (instead of removed dampeningSource). Default percentile = 25 (matches old GIT_FILE_DAMPENING).
2. BugFixSignal.extract precedence: adaptive (ctx.dampeningThreshold) > descriptor floor (ctx.confidence.score.threshold) > FALLBACK_K.
3. confidence.support becomes ONE source of truth: drives sibling lookup for label clamp AND adaptive threshold computation for score path.
4. Regression test: numerical equivalence vs pre-Task-5 code on (bugFixRate, commitCount) fixture WITH collectionStats present.
5. New test: in absence of collectionStats, static threshold=10 used as floor.

ALTERNATIVE (Variant C — hybrid):
k = max(adaptive ?? 0, descriptor_floor). Strictest available wins. Avoids regression in small codebases while preserving adaptive for mature ones. Adds a 3rd semantic slot.

OUT OF SCOPE:
- Adaptive thresholds for label clamp rules (whenSupportBelow). Currently static 5/10 — separate decision.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. BugFixSignal.extract reaches ctx.dampeningThreshold when collectionStats is present.
2. Falls back to descriptor confidence.score.threshold when collectionStats absent.
3. Numerical equivalence verified vs pre-Task-5 code path.
4. Test added covering both adaptive and floor branches.
5. Update Task 5 commit message claim / rule doc to reflect actual precedence.

LINKS:
- Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-14-hotspot-signal-interpretation-impl.md (Task 5 - D8)
- Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-14-bugfixrate-label-confidence-design.md (D8 score-side migration)
- Rule: .claude/rules/signal-confidence.md (threshold-value guidance — needs update for floor semantics)
- Discovery: user feedback this session ('Это же floor? Мы же пытались делать его адаптивным для confidence damping')
close_reason: Adaptive precedence restored at 88a3534b. resolveDampeningThreshold derives adaptive from descriptor.stats.confidence.support (p25 of git.file.{support}). BugFixSignal.extract: adaptive > floor > FALLBACK_K. 2 new tests cover precedence.

title: register_project enrichment reads teaRagsVersion + indexedAt from indexing-marker
description: tryEnrichFromQdrant in ProjectRegistryOps reads embeddingModel from the indexing-marker payload, but NOT teaRagsVersion or indexedAt — they only land in the registry when index_codebase calls recordRegistryEntry. After register_project on an already-indexed collection (zkaz scenario), tea-rags projects info shows teaRagsVersion='(unknown)' and stale indexedAt. Fix: IndexingMarker writes teaRagsVersion + indexedAt into its payload; tryEnrichFromQdrant reads both from the same marker scroll.
close_reason: Landed in commits f2d94011 (marker writes teaRagsVersion+indexedAt, enrichment reads them) + b622ba7e (fish completion auto-install via postinstall).

title: register_project: rename-only fast path skips Qdrant enrichment for populated entries
description: Current register_project always calls tryEnrichFromQdrant + registry.record() — even when re-registering an already-populated entry (just to rename). That hits Qdrant unnecessarily and risks overwriting live data with stale fallbacks. Fix: when existing.chunksCount > 0, skip enrichment and call setName() only. Enrichment stays for !existing (first-time) and existing.chunksCount===0 (stub from recoverFromQdrant or register-before-index) — preserves zkaz fix. Uniqueness check moves through setName (preserves y2ke no-orphan).
close_reason: Landed in commit 76c5d38f — rename-only fast path implemented + 3 new tests pin the contract.

title: Codegraph Slice 6 — enriched path tracing + cluster detection
description:
⚠️ ДОЛЖНО БЫТЬ ОГОВОРЕНО ПЕРЕД РЕАЛИЗАЦИЕЙ
⚠️ МОЖЕТ БЫТЬ ОСОЗНАННО ПРОПУЩЕН

Before opening a worktree, confirm with the owner: (1) path tracing is the killer-feature thesis — validate by collecting at least 2 concrete user scenarios where 'show me dangerous execution paths from A to B' beats existing get_callers / get_callees / find_cycles workflows; (2) recursive CTE + enrichment join at MCP-tool latency is feasible (bench against tea-rags' own ~3k method nodes before committing to API surface); (3) does cluster detection have demand independent of path tracing, or should it be deferred. Explicit skip is valid: the value-add is conceptual until users actually ask 'where do my dangerous paths run'.

---

## Concept

Trace execution paths from entry points through the call graph and enrich each path
with git/static signals along the trace. Rank paths by aggregated risk so agents can
prioritize where to look during debugging, code review, and pre-release QA.

This is the **unique tea-rags angle** that no other tool combines: GitNexus has
process flows but no enrichment integration; tea-rags has enrichments but no path
concept. After this slice, search and tools can answer "show me the most dangerous
execution paths from HTTP entry to DB" — not just "show me dangerous nodes".

## Depends on

- Slice 1 (`cg_symbols_edges_method` populated)
- Slice 2 (Tier 2 recursive queries via DuckDB CTE)
- Existing git enrichment (`bugFixRate`, `churn`, `ageDays`, `dominantAuthorPct`)
- Existing static enrichment (`complexity.cyclomatic`, `complexity.cognitive`)

## Pipeline

1. **Entry-point detection** — rule-based hooks per language:
   - TS: HTTP route decorators, exported `main`, test entries (`*.test.ts`),
     CLI command registrations.
   - Fallback: zero-indegree symbols in `cg_symbols_edges_method`.
2. **Path enumeration** — DFS from each entry through `cg_symbols_edges_method`,
   bounded by `maxHops` and cycle-cut policy.
3. **Path enrichment** — for each path, look up per-chunk signals from Qdrant payload
   (already populated by git/static providers).
4. **Path scoring** — aggregate node-level signals into a path risk score.
5. **Persistence** — materialize top-K risky paths per entry point in
   `cg_symbols_paths` (avoid re-running recursive CTE on every search).

## New schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE cg_symbols_paths (
  path_id           VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,  -- hash of node sequence
  entry_symbol_id   VARCHAR NOT NULL,
  entry_rel_path    VARCHAR NOT NULL,
  hop_count         INTEGER NOT NULL,
  path_nodes        VARCHAR[] NOT NULL,   -- ordered symbolIds along the path
  risk_score        DOUBLE NOT NULL,
  computed_at       TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX idx_cg_symbols_paths_risk ON cg_symbols_paths (risk_score DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_cg_symbols_paths_entry ON cg_symbols_paths (entry_symbol_id);
```

## New MCP tools

- `find_risky_paths(entryPoint?, limit?)` — top-K paths sorted by aggregated risk.
- `get_paths_through(symbolId)` — all paths traversing a node (for PR review:
  "diff touches Foo.bar — what processes are affected").
- `get_paths_to(symbolId)` — all paths terminating at a node (for bug diagnosis:
  "test failed at UserController.create — show 5 riskiest traces leading here").

## New rerank preset

`dangerousPath` — projects path risk back to chunks: a chunk's `pathDangerScore`
is the max risk score among paths it participates in. Used to rerank
`semantic_search` / `hybrid_search` results when the user is hunting bugs.

## New derived signal

`pathDangerScore` — derived from `cg_symbols_paths` aggregation, attached to
each chunk's overlay.

## Open questions (defer to plan-writing)

- **Entry-point detection**: rule-based per language vs pure zero-indegree
  heuristic vs hybrid. Trade-off: precision vs language-coverage.
- **Aggregation function**: `Σ` (additive) vs `max` (worst-link) vs weighted
  geometric (product of normalized signals). Different functions favor
  different intuitions ("any one bad node taints path" vs "many small risks
  compound").
- **Cycle handling**: cut at first re-visit, or allow N-bounded loops?
- **Top-K storage size**: K=10 per entry point or K=100? Affects DuckDB size
  and `find_risky_paths` recall.
- **Recompute trigger**: nightly cron, on-demand, or after every reindex?
- **Multi-language paths**: Slice 1 graph is per-language. Cross-language
  paths (TS → Python via subprocess) are out of scope until cross-language
  edges are introduced (Slice 5+).

## Use cases

1. **Bug diagnosis**: "test failed at X — top 5 riskiest paths leading to X"
2. **Pre-release QA**: "top 10 dangerous paths in auth subsystem"
3. **PR review**: "diff touches Y — paths through Y ranked by risk"
4. **Onboarding**: "highest-complexity paths in the codebase" — where new
   engineers will lose the most time

## Slice positioning

This is **Slice 6** in the codegraph roadmap. It depends on:
- Slice 1 (graph extraction, `cg_symbols_edges_method`)
- Slice 2 (recursive CTE, Tier 2-3 metrics)
- Optionally Slice 3 (multi-language) for cross-language traces — but
  TS-only paths are usable from Slice 6 day one.

## Out of scope

- Static path analysis with control-flow graph (CFG) — paths here are call-graph
  paths, not branch-level traces.
- Cross-repo paths (microservices) — that's a multi-repo concern, separate epic.
notes: Design approved 2026-05-25 for trace_path portion: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-trace-path-design.md. title: Re-evaluate backfill 60s timeout for large missed sets
description: Backfill calls provider.buildFileSignals(root, { paths: missedPaths }) which runs git log over the union of all missed paths. With 488 paths (or 7263 in force) the pathspec construction + history walk hits the 60s timeout in taxdome — backfill consistently returns backfilledFiles=0 despite missing files actually existing in git. Options: (a) batch the paths into smaller chunks, (b) raise/remove the timeout for backfill specifically, (c) drop pathspec when missedPaths exceeds N and fall back to per-file lookup. File: src/core/domains/trajectory/git/provider.ts (and underlying git client/reader).
close_reason: Resolved by option (a) — batching. file-reader.ts:60 buildFileSignalsForPaths uses BATCH=500 with per-batch 30s timeout, introduced in b8154d4b (2026-02-12, pre-dates this ticket). Combined with 89pc-family fixes (FIFO serialize 5943a872, RunState b8bb1673), the original 'backfill returns backfilledFiles=0 on 488/7263 missed paths' symptom no longer reproduces — pathspec union never assembled, each batch is independent.

title: Refire stats refresh after CHUNK_BACKFILL_COMPLETE
description: onChunkEnrichmentComplete fires when streaming + initial chunk enrichment finishes. Backfill (BACKFILL_COMPLETE + CHUNK_BACKFILL_COMPLETE in CompletionRunner) writes 7000+ file-level overlays after that, but no second stats refresh is triggered. StatsCache stays frozen on pre-backfill snapshot — user sees filesWithGitData=5 even though Qdrant already has 22459+7262 enriched files. Fix: trigger statsCache.save() after CompletionRunner.run() finishes, OR fire onChunkEnrichmentComplete a second time at end of completion. Files: src/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/enrichment/completion-runner.ts, src/core/api/internal/facades/ingest-facade.ts.
close_reason: Task 3 complete: commit 8e40ed8c. Integration sanity check on tea-rags-mcp passed (filesWithGitData=510=totalFiles, healthy)

title: stats-cache: recompute after forceReindex enrichment finishes
description: After mcp__tea-rags__index_codebase with forceReindex=true, get_index_metrics returns partial stats: topAuthors=[], git.file.* signals missing, git.chunk.* count=5 (vs 1881 expected), enrichmentTimeRange field absent. Enrichment markers correctly transition to healthy (matchedFiles=533, missedFiles=0, durationMs=39333) and chunk payloads contain git data (verified via semantic_search). The issue: stats are computed AT end of forceReindex BEFORE async file-enrichment completes. A subsequent index_codebase call (no-op, no changes) refreshes stats correctly — confirming the data is in Qdrant, only the cache is stale. Fix: trigger stats recompute when CompletionRunner finalizes (markFileFinal + markChunkFinal both emit 'healthy'), OR have get_index_metrics check enrichment.completedAt vs stats-cache timestamp and recompute if stale. Pre-existing issue unrelated to the EnrichmentCoordinator split refactor (epic tea-rags-mcp-b8qw).
close_reason: Resolved via sr33 (commit 8e40ed8c re-fire onChunkEnrichmentComplete after backfill). StatsCache now refreshes after CompletionRunner finalization.

title: enrichment: drive backfiller.test.ts seed via real applyFileSignals (not _missedFileChunks reflection)
description: tests/core/domains/ingest/pipeline/enrichment/backfiller.test.ts:15 reaches into private (applier as any)._missedFileChunks to seed missed paths. Defeats the encapsulation introduced in Task 2 (b0bfabd6) where _missedFileChunks was made private behind getMissedFileChunks(). A future rename will silently break test (no compile error due to as any cast, line 'internal.set(...)' just no-ops). Fix: drive seed by calling real applier.applyFileSignals with a fileMetadata Map that doesn't contain the path — that's the production path that fills _missedFileChunks. Per .claude/rules/test-patterns.md prefer behavior over reflection-style testing.
close_reason: Done in commit e97c2e3a. Reflection seed via (applier as any)._missedFileChunks replaced with applier.applyFileSignals + empty fileMetadata — production-path that itself fills _missedFileChunks and increments missedFiles. Tests 2/2 pass, type-check clean, src/ untouched.

title: dinopowers:systematic-debugging: emit cause + solution + prevention; use search-cascade decision tree with rerank presets
description:
Two enhancements for .claude-plugin/dinopowers/skills/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md (or its handoff template into superpowers:systematic-debugging):

## 1. Final report shape

Today the skill produces a fix only. Want an explicit 3-section presentation when the root cause is found, regardless of which phase ended:

- **Cause:** the single root-cause sentence (what is happening at which file:line, why it's wrong)
- **Fix:** what to change, file:line + diff intent
- **Prevention:** how to avoid recurrence — invariant to enforce, test to add (regression), architectural rule, lint rule, or assertion. Concrete and actionable, not "be careful".

This makes debugging output reusable as a postmortem note, not just an ad-hoc patch.

## 2. Tool selection via decision tree (not ad-hoc rerank choices)

Current bug-hunt step uses rerank="bugHunt" generically. Want the skill to consult the search-cascade decision tree (.claude-plugin/tea-rags/rules/search-cascade.md "Decision Tree" section) and pick the right cascade entry (find_symbol / hybrid_search / semantic_search / find_similar / rank_chunks) based on what is being looked up at that step (symptom matching vs definition lookup vs usage tracing vs structural exploration).

Rerank presets should also be picked from a small lookup table inside the skill or a referenced doc, not hardcoded. Suggested mapping (illustrative — adjust during implementation):
- symptom→suspect (bug origin) → rerank "bugHunt"
- regression area scan → rerank "hotspots"
- ownership / who knows this code → rerank "ownership"
- legacy/risky touch → rerank "techDebt"
- recent introduction (newly broken) → rerank "recent"

Skill should phrase the choice explicitly in its output ("using rerank=bugHunt because we are matching a symptom to a likely origin").

## Context that triggered this request

Found while debugging tea-rags own forceReindex stats-stale bug today. Initial bug-hunt PRESENT block was good, but downstream verification used direct semantic_search calls without going back through the decision tree. Also the final root-cause writeup had fix but no prevention/invariant — easy to reintroduce.

## Acceptance

- New section template in skill output enforced via skill markdown
- Decision-tree consultation step added before each search call (one-line note "consulting decision tree → using <tool> with rerank=<preset> because <reason>")
- Tested by running on a fresh symptom and confirming the 3-section output and explicit tool-choice rationales appear
notes:
ADDITION (clarification from user): the enrichment overlay signals on bug-hunt results MUST drive both Cause and Prevention sections, not just inform suspect ranking. Examples:

- file/chunk in "deep-silo" or "silo" ownership label → Prevention should call out knowledge-transfer / code-review pairing (not just a code patch)
- chunk churnVolatility "erratic" → Prevention should propose freezing the surface or extracting an interface to stop fanout
- file bugFixRate "critical" → Prevention should propose a regression test that locks the now-fixed invariant explicitly
- high imports (fan-in / coupling) → Prevention should propose defensive validation at the coupling point + adding an integration test through the call chain
- recently changed (ageDays "recent") + this fix → Prevention should call out reviewing the recent commit for the introduction point

Skill template should require the Cause/Prevention sections to cite which overlay signal informed each line ("because chunk shows bugFixRate=critical we propose X", "because file shows ownership=deep-silo we propose pairing on this area").

Also: the lookup table mapping symptom-class → rerank preset should be explicit in the skill markdown (or a referenced doc), not in the skill author's head. Decision-tree consultation should be a documented step ("consult search-cascade.md Decision Tree → tool=<X> because <reason from tree>").

title: Split file/chunk marker writes in awaitCompletion
description:
awaitCompletion currently waits for ALL inFlightWork (file applyFileSignals + chunk streaming) before writing file marker. Chunk marker is written earlier via post-flush short-circuit. Result: file marker shows completed after chunk marker, even though file work finishes first per-batch.

Plan: split state.inFlightWork into fileWork[] and chunkWork[]. awaitCompletion sequence:
1. await prefetchPromises
2. await fileWork
3. backfillMissedFiles
4. count(file) → write file marker
5. await chunkWork
6. count(chunk) → write chunk marker
close_reason: Implemented split file/chunk marker writes in awaitCompletion (commit aeb992d0)

title: Re-tune QDRANT_DELETE_BATCH_SIZE/CONCURRENCY defaults after filter-delete bench fix
description:
Once tea-rags-mcp-e3sn lands (bench measures filter-based delete-by-relativePath via deletePointsByPathsBatched), re-run benchmarks/tune.mjs against embedded Qdrant on a representative corpus (>= 3000 files) and update defaults in QdrantTuneConfig + bootstrap embedded-tuning.

Current values were calibrated against ID-delete profile and are wrong for filter-delete:
- QDRANT_DELETE_BATCH_SIZE=1000 — too aggressive: each filter-should with 1000 conditions degenerates to scan-per-condition under embedded WAL pressure
- QDRANT_DELETE_CONCURRENCY=4 — saturates upsert channel, observed 186s stall on 3455-file delete-storm

Acceptance:
- New defaults documented in src/bootstrap/config/embedded-tuning.ts and PERFORMANCE_TUNING.md
- Verified on embedded Qdrant: delete-storm of 3000+ files does not block upsert channel longer than X seconds (define X from bench)
- BREAKING CHANGE footer in commit if env-var defaults shift
close_reason: user request: closed without implementation

title: tune benchmark measures delete-by-IDs instead of delete-by-filter
description:
benchmarks/lib/benchmarks.mjs:421,479 use qdrant.client.delete({points: batch}) — production deletePointsByPathsBatched (src/core/adapters/qdrant/client.ts:737) uses {filter:{should: paths-by-relativePath}} with pipelined concurrency and indexed relativePath (schema v4/v5: keyword + text). Different WAL cost profiles. Current tune defaults (QDRANT_DELETE_BATCH_SIZE=1000, QDRANT_DELETE_CONCURRENCY=4) are optimal for ID-delete but cause 186s upsert-channel stall on 3455-file delete-storm with filter-delete on embedded Qdrant.

Fix in benchmarks/lib/benchmarks.mjs:
1. seedPoints / benchmark seed must populate payload.relativePath with synthetic-but-unique values
2. createCollection step must call schema-manager equivalent: createPayloadIndex(name, 'relativePath', 'keyword') + 'text' before measuring delete (matches schema v4/v5)
3. Replace inlined client.delete loop in benchmarkDeleteBatchSize/benchmarkDeleteConcurrency with direct call to QdrantManager.deletePointsByPathsBatched({batchSize, concurrency}) — single source of truth for pipelining, retry, filter shape; any future changes in client auto-reflect in bench
4. Acceptance: deleting N points via path-batch=B,concurrency=C produces same {durationMs,batchCount} shape as production hot path
close_reason: user request: closed without implementation

title: Reindex stats downgrade for pure-delete L2 failures (no modified-upsert collision)
description:
Phase 3.2 closes the orphan-duplication race for the modified-files path. But edge case remains: if ONLY a DELETED file's L2 fails (no corresponding modified file), DeletionOutcome.failed is non-empty, BUT coordinator.skippedFiles().length === 0 (no modified file triggers canUpsertForFile). Result: filesSkippedDueToDeleteFailure = 0, stats.status stays 'completed', yet old chunks for a supposedly-deleted file remain.

Fix: in reindexing.ts, consider downgrading stats.status = 'partial' also when deletionOutcome && !deletionOutcome.isFullSuccess() (not only on upsert-skips). Alternatively, extend filesSkippedDueToDeleteFailure to include delete-only failures.

Discovered during code review of commit faf7bbe (Phase 3.2).

title: Path-based overlay + helper refactor for arbitrary trajectory key depth
description: Current RankingOverlay shape is {preset, file?, chunk?} — flat buckets by level. Signal keys carry full namespaced paths (git.file.commitCount, cg_symbol.<anything>.<field>, cg_temp_coupling.<anything>.<field>), but overlay drops the namespace and intermediate segments, forcing consumers to hardcode where to place signals (buildGitFromOverlay → payload.git). Also applyEssentialSignalsToOverlay parses essentialKeys as namespace.level.field (3 segments) — breaks on deeper keys. Fix: (1) RankingOverlay.signals: Record<string /* full payload path */, unknown>. (2) Reranker.buildOverlay writes full paths, not flat {file, chunk}. (3) applyEssentialSignalsToOverlay walks payload tree by dotted path, no segment-count assumption. (4) filterMetaOnly.buildGitFromOverlay removed — consumers use path walk. Breaking change for reranker output shape, filterMetaOnly, API response shape, tests. Prerequisite for cg_symbol and cg_temp_coupling trajectories.
close_reason: Premature. Current 3-segment key structure (namespace.level.field) is stable and will hold for cg_symbol and cg_temp_coupling. Path-based rewrite not needed. Minimal namespace-discriminator fix can be reconsidered when a second trajectory with derived signals actually lands.

title: Fix find_symbol payload contract inconsistency with semantic/hybrid
description: find_symbol returns raw git payload including internal fields (dominantAuthorEmail, lastCommitHash, lastModifiedAt, firstCreatedAt, enrichedAt, linesAdded, linesDeleted, fileChurnCount) regardless of rerank parameter. This violates the by-design contract where BY rerank = essential-only, WITH rerank = overlay mask + labels. Root cause: SymbolSearchStrategy.postProcess overrides base without calling applyMetaOnly, and resolveSymbols only deletes payload.content on metaOnly. Fix: symmetrize find_symbol with semantic/hybrid — filter git by essentialKeys without rerank, apply overlay mask with rerank. Also need to decide if metaOnly=false should get full payload (matching semantic) or essential-filtered git (more restrictive).
close_reason: Fixed find_symbol payload contract. SymbolSearchStrategy.postProcess now applies applyEssentialGitToResult on metaOnly=true — filters git.file and git.chunk to essential keys + overlay signals when reranked. Preserves outline-specific payload fields (chunkCount, mergedChunkIds). Exposed filterGitByEssential logic as applyEssentialGitToResult helper in post-process.ts. base.ts essentialKeys moved from private to protected for subclass access. 3 new tests cover: metaOnly=true strips internal fields, metaOnly=false preserves full payload, outline fields survive filtering. All 3645 tests pass.

title: dinopowers:receiving-code-review — wrapper с imports overlay для impact анализа ревью
description: Wrapper над superpowers:receiving-code-review. При получении review-комментария, затрагивающего функцию/файл, запрашивает imports overlay через tea-rags, чтобы оценить blast radius изменения перед согласием с ревьюером. Помогает избежать 'performative agreement' когда фикс из ревью ломает много dependents. Через /optimize-skill + eval (comment 'rename X' → показать imports.count перед согласием). 100% pass rate.
close_reason: 12/12 with-rule, 4/12 baseline, +67pp.

title: dinopowers:verification-before-completion — wrapper с git-signals для collateral damage check
description: Wrapper над superpowers:verification-before-completion. Перед claim 'done' собирает git-signals (recency + imports) по изменённым файлам и выделяет high-impact/high-churn файлы для дополнительной проверки. Цель — не пропустить collateral damage в зонах с большим blast radius. Реализация через /optimize-skill + eval (сценарий: изменён файл с imports>10 → должен потребовать verification по dependents). 100% pass rate.
close_reason: 15/15 with-rule, 2/15 baseline, +87pp delta. Collateral-damage scan post-edit with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW-BLAST verdict informs verification scope.

title: dinopowers:test-driven-development — wrapper с find_symbol/find_similar для поиска паттернов тестов
description: Wrapper над superpowers:test-driven-development. Перед написанием failing-теста ищет существующие тесты по паттерну через tea-rags:find_similar + find_symbol, чтобы агент следовал локальным conventions (vi.mock patterns, helper reuse). Реализация через /optimize-skill с eval-кейсами (новый тест для X → должен найти похожие тесты в соседних файлах). 100% pass rate eval.
close_reason: 15/15 with-rule, 3/15 baseline, +80pp. semantic_search+testFile='only'+rerank='proven'+metaOnly=false for test pattern discovery before RED phase.

title: dinopowers:systematic-debugging — wrapper с bug-hunt по исторически багованной зоне
description: Wrapper над superpowers:systematic-debugging. Перед debugging-циклом запускает tea-rags:bug-hunt по симптому, чтобы сузить поиск к исторически багованному коду (высокий bugFixRate + recency). Реализация: через /optimize-skill с eval-кейсами (симптом X → должен найти bug-hunt перед systematic-debugging). Покрытие — 100% pass rate eval.
close_reason: 15/15 with-rule, 4/15 baseline, +73pp delta. First composition-style wrapper: delegates to Skill(tea-rags:bug-hunt) instead of calling semantic_search directly, then feeds suspect list to superpowers:systematic-debugging.

title: dinopowers: search-cascade injection для subagent prompts
description: Механизм, который автоматически добавляет .claude/rules/search-cascade правила в каждый subagent prompt при dispatching. Сейчас search-cascade доступен только в main-session через CLAUDE.md, а subagent'ы работают без него и падают на built-in Grep вместо tea-rags. Реализация: либо hook на Agent tool call, либо wrapper-скилл над superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents / subagent-driven-development, который предваряет user-prompt блоком правил.
notes: Если injection реализуется как wrapper-скилл (а не hook) — применяется /optimize-skill + eval-кейсы, 100% pass rate.
close_reason: PreToolUse hook на Agent: инжектит wrapper-routing таблицу в subagent prompts.

title: dinopowers:executing-plans — wrapper с pre-touch modification_guard через git signals
description: Wrapper над superpowers:executing-plans. Перед каждым Task, который модифицирует файл, автоматически запрашивает git-сигналы (dominantAuthor, commitCount, ageDays, imports) для этого файла и показывает агенту пред-touch сводку (аналог tree-sitter modification_guard). Цель — предотвратить blind edits в hotspot/high-impact зонах. Интерфейс: тот же что у superpowers:executing-plans + pre-step сигналов перед каждой модификацией.
notes: Acceptance: реализовать через /optimize-skill + eval-кейсы. 100% pass rate обязательно перед close.
close_reason: 15/15 with-rule, 8/15 baseline, +47pp delta (below +50pp target — verdict+control cases are natural, skill closes protocol-enforcement gap). Per-Task guard with SAFE/CAUTION/UNSAFE ladder.

title: dinopowers:writing-plans — wrapper над superpowers:writing-plans с data-driven-generation сигналами
description: Wrapper, который при написании плана собирает git-сигналы (ownership, churn, bugFixRate, age) для файлов, которые план затрагивает, через tea-rags:data-driven-generation и включает их в секцию 'Affected files' плана. Цель — чтобы решения о декомпозиции и приоритетах опирались на реальные данные о коде, а не только на структуру. Интерфейс: тот же что у superpowers:writing-plans + авто-обогащение Affected files.
notes: Acceptance: реализовать через /optimize-skill + eval-кейсы. 100% pass rate обязательно перед close.
close_reason: 14/14 with-rule, 4/14 baseline, +71pp delta. Custom impact rerank {imports:0.5, churn:0.3, ownership:0.2} + brace expansion + aggregation by relativePath.

title: dinopowers:brainstorming — wrapper над superpowers:brainstorming с risk-assessment инъекцией
description: Wrapper-скилл, который при вызове сначала запускает tea-rags:risk-assessment для области brainstorming'а (если она известна из запроса), затем передаёт результат как контекст в superpowers:brainstorming. Цель — чтобы brainstorming оперировал реальными сигналами (hotspots, tech-debt, ownership) по затронутой зоне вместо общего gut-feeling. Интерфейс: тот же что у superpowers:brainstorming + пре-шаг с risk-assessment.
notes: Acceptance: реализовать через /optimize-skill + eval-кейсы. 100% pass rate обязательно перед close.
close_reason: 13/13 with-rule, 3/13 baseline, +77pp delta. Bootstrap via dinopowers:writing-skills worked — draft hit 100% first iteration.

title: Sub-collections (Cold Memory): directory-scoped collections with per-collection embedding
description: Allow declaring project subdirectories as separate collections via .tea-rags config or MCP tool params. Simplified pipeline (chunking + embedding + Merkle sync, no git enrichment). Per-collection embedding model. excludeFromProject flag. Incremental reindex. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-sub-collections-design.md. Blocked by collection registry (tea-rags-mcp-gr4o) — needs registry metadata to track sub-collection state.

title: DDG: add DEFENSIVE hard rule for dangerous overlay labels
description: When risk-assessment ran with dangerous preset and labels show bugFix concerning+ AND knowledgeSilo high+ → trigger DEFENSIVE strategy in data-driven-generation skill. Currently DDG uses custom weights, not named presets. This adds a new hard rule row to Step 1 strategy selection table.
close_reason: Updated DDG SKILL.md: replaced inline custom weights with rerank:proven, added DEFENSIVE for critical bugFixRate (any ageDays), added CONSERVATIVE when no overlay labels present

title: File-level grouping: aggregate members in explore tools (semantic_search, rank_chunks)
description: Currently level=file in all explore strategies is dedup only (keep highest-scored chunk per file). No members[] or file structure is returned. All data is available in payload (symbolId, parentName, headingPath). Need to aggregate members on file-level results — at minimum list symbolIds per file, for markdown also generate TOC from headingPath. Reuses outlineDoc() from symbol-resolve.ts. Affects: vector.ts (queryGroups groupSize), hybrid/scroll-rank/similar (groupByFile), reranker (groupBy). Separate from find_symbol file-level which already has full grouping via resolveSymbols.

title: Navigation end-to-end integration test
description: Integration test: MarkdownChunker → FileProcessor → StaticPayloadBuilder → verify navigation + doc symbolId + headingPath in Qdrant payload
close_reason: Closed

title: Update search-cascade and add drift vs migration rule
description: Add chunk navigation guidance to search-cascade. Create rule documenting schemaDrift for breaking changes vs migrations for partial updates
close_reason: search-cascade updated: af875be9 (chunk navigation), a368c1dd (documentation rerank auto-activation), e4ea7b50 (chunk grouping), 7e1f84a7 (mid-session reindex). schema-drift-vs-migration.md created in .claude/rules/.local/.

title: Add navigation field to CodeChunk type
description: Add optional navigation: { prevSymbolId?: string; nextSymbolId?: string } to CodeChunk.metadata in types.ts
close_reason: Closed

title: Verify stripInternalFields does NOT strip navigation
description: Add test confirming navigation passes through stripInternalFields unchanged while headingPath is still stripped
close_reason: Closed

title: Auto-detect Ollama model info (contextLength, dimensions) from /api/show
description: Query /api/show at index time. Store in collection marker. Cap chunkSize by contextLength*3. Use dimensions for collection creation. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-01-ollama-model-info-design.md
close_reason: All 5 tasks implemented: resolveModelInfo, marker codec, chunkSize cap, dimensions auto-detect, marker-first resolution

title: Operation failures docs
description: docs/website/operation-failures.md article on Ollama failure behavior
close_reason: Closed

title: Crystallization: emergent best practices and anti-patterns from bullet statistics
description:
## Problem
Tier 1 (CLAUDE.md anti-patterns, best practices) пишется вручную. При этом Tier 2 (living memory) уже содержит статистику: какие знания помогают (helpful), какие вредят (harmful), какие не используются (not retrieved). Эта статистика может автоматически порождать governance rules.

## Core insight
Bullets кристаллизуются в governance через статистику:
- Cluster of helpful bullets → emergent best practice
- Cluster of harmful bullets → emergent anti-pattern  
- Unused bullets → decay and delete
- Orphaned domain → deprecation signal

Tier 1 перестаёт быть написанным — он ВЫРАЩЕН из кода. Конституция как emergent property кодовой базы.

## Crystallization rules

### Best practice emergence
```
Trigger: 3+ bullets in same domain with helpful > N (adaptive threshold)
Action:
  1. Cluster bullets by semantic similarity (cosine > 0.7 within domain)
  2. If cluster size >= 3 → candidate for best practice
  3. LLM (Llama 14B): summarize cluster into one rule
     Input: [bullet_1.content, bullet_2.content, bullet_3.content]
     Prompt: "These patterns consistently help in {domain}. Write ONE rule."
     Output: "Always set FirmContext before calling scoped workers in communication/"
  4. Generate rule file: .claude/rules/generated/{domain}-best-practice.md
  5. Or: append to CLAUDE.md generated section
```

### Anti-pattern emergence
```
Trigger: 2+ bullets in same domain with harmful > helpful * 3 AND total interactions >= 10
Action:
  1. Cluster harmful bullets by similarity
  2. LLM: summarize into anti-pattern
     Input: harmful bullets + context of failures
     Prompt: "These patterns consistently cause problems in {domain}. Write ONE prohibition."
     Output: "NEVER call TwilioClient without timeout — Twilio silently drops after 25s"
  3. Add to anti-patterns table in CLAUDE.md (generated section)
  4. Original bullets remain in memory (they're the evidence)
```

### Decay (not retrieved)
```
Trigger: bullet.last_retrieved is NULL or > 30 days ago
Action by source:
  - auto: delete (knowledge not needed)
  - code_comment: keep (synced with code, deletion = code change)
  - human: flag for review, don't auto-delete
```

### Domain deprecation signal
```
Trigger: ALL bullets in domain have empty resolved_symbols
Meaning: code for this domain was deleted/moved
Action: warn user "domain {X} appears deprecated — N bullets have no code references"
```

## Generated vs hand-written governance
Critical distinction: generated rules live in a SEPARATE section/directory from hand-written:
- `.claude/rules/generated/` — auto-generated, can be overwritten by crystallization
- `.claude/rules/` — hand-written, never auto-modified
- CLAUDE.md: `## Generated Anti-patterns` section (clearly marked)

Hand-written rules ALWAYS override generated. If conflict detected (generated rule contradicts hand-written) → discard generated, log conflict.

## Feedback loop
Generated rules affect future bullet lifecycle:
1. Best practice crystallized → future bullets matching this pattern get helpful++ boost at birth
2. Anti-pattern crystallized → future bullets matching pattern get harmful++ boost
3. This accelerates convergence: confirmed patterns strengthen faster

## Crystallization is NOT:
- Replacing hand-written rules (those are governance, override everything)
- Running after every session (periodic: weekly or after N new bullets)
- Modifying bullet content (bullets are evidence, crystallized rules are conclusions)
- Deleting source bullets (keep as audit trail / evidence for the generated rule)

## Metrics
After each crystallization run:
- New best practices generated (count, content preview)
- New anti-patterns generated (count, content preview)
- Bullets decayed (count, domains affected)
- Domains flagged as deprecated (count)
- Conflicts with hand-written rules (count, details)

## Related
- ACE7 epic (tea-rags-mcp-e98j)
- Depends on: kds5 (retrieval tracking — needs counter data), 3iv6 (curator — crystallization is a curator extension)
- Feeds into: Tier 1 governance (CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/)
- LLM: Llama 14B for summarization (same infra as reflector)

title: Human bullet ingestion: manual knowledge input with protected lifecycle
description:
## Problem
Не все знания выводимы из кода или комментариев. Архитектурные решения, юридические ограничения, бизнес-контекст, inter-team agreements — только человек может добавить.

## Solution
MCP tool или skill для добавления human bullets с защищённым lifecycle.

## Interface
```
add_bullet(
  content: "Legal requires double opt-in for SMS marketing",
  domain: "communication/sms",
  type: "decision"
)
```
Auto-populated: source=human, helpful=0, harmful=0, created=now.
resolved_symbols: computed via vector proximity (auto-synonym resolution).

## Protected lifecycle rules (CRITICAL)
Human bullets are NEVER auto-pruned or auto-merged:

| Operation | Auto/Code_comment bullets | Human bullets |
|-----------|--------------------------|---------------|
| Pruning by counters | harmful > helpful*2 → delete | NEVER. Flag for review only |
| Semantic dedup merge | cosine > 0.95 → auto-merge | Warn human: "similar bullet exists: [content]. Merge?" |
| Orphan pruning | resolved_symbols empty → delete | Warn: "no code references found. Still relevant?" |
| Content update | Auto (caption regenerated) | Only human can edit |
| Deletion | Curator auto-deletes | Only human can delete |

Rationale: human knowledge often has LOW cosine with code (it's about WHY, not WHAT). Auto-pruning would kill the most valuable bullets — the ones that can't be derived from code.

## harmful tracking for human bullets
Although helpful++ is skipped (human decided it matters), harmful++ IS tracked:
- If agent retrieves human bullet AND produces wrong code → harmful++
- High harmful count on human bullet → flag for review: "this bullet may be outdated or misleading"
- Human decides: update content, or delete, or keep as-is

## Input sources
1. **Direct tool call**: `add_bullet(content, domain, type)` via MCP
2. **Conversation extraction**: agent asks "should I remember this?" → user confirms → add_bullet
3. **Batch import**: parse existing .claude/context/*.md → bullets (part of bootstrap, rpzq)

## Dedup at ingestion
Before adding, check:
1. cosine(new_bullet, existing_bullets) > 0.90 → warn: "similar exists"
2. If user confirms merge → keep human source, combine content
3. If user says "add anyway" → add as separate bullet (may have different nuance)
Threshold 0.90 (not 0.95) for human bullets — more conservative, prefer to warn.

## Related
- ACE7 epic (tea-rags-mcp-e98j)
- Depends on: e0tf (typed collections), 6315 (auto-synonym for resolved_symbols)
- Feeds into: kds5 (retrieval tracking — special counter rules for human bullets)
- Feeds into: 3iv6 (curator — special pruning rules for human bullets)

title: Auto-synonym resolution: bridge registry concepts to code symbols via vector proximity
description:
## Problem
Memory collection хранит доменные концепты ("SMS sending subsystem"), код содержит реализации (SmsSender, DeliveryService). Агент должен перейти от концепта к коду без code-level facts в bullets (которые дрифтят).

## Insight
Embedding vectors уже содержат семантическую близость. Qdrant может найти связь без LLM — чистая векторная математика.

## Solution
При reindex для каждого bullet:
1. Embed content → вектор
2. Cross-collection nearest neighbors: query code collection с этим вектором
3. Извлечь top-K символов (class/method names) из результатов
4. Сохранить как `resolved_symbols: string[]` в payload bullet

### Exception: code_comment bullets
Code comment bullets уже имеют resolved_symbols из AST parent (прямая привязка). Vector proximity НЕ нужен — это было бы менее точно чем AST link.

Порядок resolved_symbols по source:
1. `code_comment` → AST parent (точная привязка, не пересчитывается через vectors)
2. `human` / `auto` → vector proximity (пересчитывается при reindex)

## Resolution algorithm
```
For each bullet where source != "code_comment":
  1. embed(bullet.content) → query vector
  2. search code_collection with query vector
     - filter: chunkType in ["function", "class", "interface"] (не markdown, не comments)
     - limit: 20 candidates
  3. deduplicate by symbol name (один класс может иметь несколько chunks)
  4. filter by similarity threshold (adaptive, see below)
  5. extract symbol names → resolved_symbols
  6. upsert bullet with updated resolved_symbols
```

## Adaptive threshold for symbol resolution
- Не hardcoded 0.3 — разные проекты, модели, домены
- Подход: для каждого bullet собрать все candidate scores → threshold = mean + 0.5*std
  (выше среднего — реально связанные символы, ниже — шум)
- Cold start: default 0.35, адаптируется после 50+ bullets

## Incremental update при reindex
- Не пересчитывать все bullets при каждом reindex
- Только bullets, чьи resolved_symbols содержат символы из изменённых файлов
- Плюс bullets, для которых в изменённых файлах появились НОВЫЕ символы с высоким cosine
  (новый класс SmsRetryService может стать resolved_symbol для bullet "SMS retry logic")

## Stale symbol detection
- resolved_symbol ссылается на символ, который удалён из кода
- При reindex: find_symbol(name) → не найден → убрать из resolved_symbols
- Если resolved_symbols стал пустым → bullet orphaned (для auto/code_comment → prune, для human → warning)

## Related
- ACE7 epic (tea-rags-mcp-e98j)
- Depends on: e0tf (typed collections), rpzq (bootstrap creates bullets to resolve)
- Multi-collection search (mc87) — per-collection models affect cross-collection query
- Existing find_symbol tool

title: Code comment extraction: AST-linked bullet source from inline comments
description:
## Problem
Код содержит ценные знания в комментариях: HACK, WARN, WHY, NOTE, @deprecated. Эти знания привязаны к конкретным символам через AST и дрифтуют вместе с кодом — самый надёжный источник bullets.

## Insight
Tea-rags chunker уже знает о комментариях: comment-capture hook помечает doc comments и связывает с function/class chunks через excludedRows. Нужно расширить: кроме doc comments, извлекать inline knowledge markers.

## Comment patterns → bullet types

| Pattern | Regex (JS) | Bullet type | Priority |
|---------|-----------|-------------|----------|
| HACK | \`\/\/\s*HACK:?\s*(.+)\` | failure_mode | high — known workaround |
| FIXME | \`\/\/\s*FIXME:?\s*(.+)\` | failure_mode | high — known bug |
| WARN/WARNING | \`\/\/\s*WARN(?:ING)?:?\s*(.+)\` | failure_mode | high — trap |
| WHY/REASON | \`\/\/\s*(?:WHY\|REASON):?\s*(.+)\` | decision | medium — architectural context |
| NOTE/IMPORTANT | \`\/\/\s*(?:NOTE\|IMPORTANT):?\s*(.+)\` | anti_pattern | medium — constraint |
| @deprecated | \`@deprecated\s+(.+)\` | migration | high — API change |
| TODO with context | \`\/\/\s*TODO:?\s*(.+)\` | domain_map | low — improvement area |

Multi-line comments: if next line starts with `//` and no pattern prefix → continuation of previous comment. Concat into single bullet content.

Language-specific variants:
- Ruby: `# HACK:`, `# WHY:`, `# NOTE:`
- Python: `# HACK:`, `# WHY:`, `# NOTE:`
- JS/TS: `// HACK:`, `// WHY:`, `// NOTE:`, `/* HACK: */`

## AST-linked resolved_symbols
Code comment bullets get resolved_symbols from AST parent — NO vector proximity needed:
1. Comment is inside a function chunk → resolved_symbol = function name
2. Comment is inside a class chunk → resolved_symbol = class name
3. Comment is at file top (outside any symbol) → resolved_symbol = file path
4. Comment-capture hook already tracks which chunk owns the comment → reuse this mapping

This is MORE precise than vector proximity (exact AST link vs semantic guess).

## Lifecycle: drifts WITH code
- Comment changed → bullet content updated (match by chunk ID + pattern type)
- Comment deleted → bullet marked orphaned → curator prunes
- Function renamed → chunk ID changes → old bullet orphaned, new bullet created from new chunk
- File deleted → all bullets from file orphaned

## Bullet identity
How to match existing bullet to updated comment (avoid duplicates on reindex):
- Key: `{file_path}:{chunk_id}:{pattern_type}` 
- On reindex: find bullet by key → update content if changed, or mark orphaned if comment gone
- New comment (no matching key) → new bullet

## Integration with chunker pipeline
Two options:

**Option A: Extract during chunking** (in comment-capture hook)
- Pro: single pass, no extra file reads, AST context already available
- Con: couples bullet extraction with chunking, chunker becomes more complex

**Option B: Post-indexing extraction pass** (separate step after chunks indexed)
- Pro: decoupled, chunker stays simple, can be run independently
- Con: extra pass over all chunks, needs to re-read AST parent info

**Recommended: Option B** — decoupled, can be toggled on/off, doesn't complicate chunker.
Run as enrichment-like step: after chunks indexed → scan chunks for patterns → extract bullets.

## Edge cases
- Comment in dead code (commented out function): skip — if the code block is commented out, don't extract
- Comment in test file: extract with domain suffix "/test" — test-specific knowledge is still valuable
- Same comment in multiple files (copy-paste): dedup by content cosine > 0.95
- Comment with only pattern prefix and no content ("// TODO" with nothing after): skip

## Related
- ACE7 epic (tea-rags-mcp-e98j)
- Depends on: e0tf (typed collections for bullet schema)
- Existing comment-capture hook in chunker (chunker-hooks)
- Feeds into: 3iv6 (curator manages orphaned bullets)
- Feeds into: kds5 (retrieval tracking — code_comment bullets get full counter lifecycle)

title: Multi-collection search: single query across code + registry
description:
## Problem
Поиск по code_xxx и registry требует двух отдельных вызовов. Агент должен знать, в какой коллекции искать. Кроме того, разные коллекции могут требовать разных embedding моделей — code_xxx оптимален с jina-code, а registry артефактов (natural language descriptions, frontmatter) лучше работает с nomic-embed или другой text-oriented моделью.

## Solution
1. **Multi-collection search**: новый параметр или tool для поиска по нескольким коллекциям одним запросом
2. **Per-collection embedding model**: при create_collection можно указать embedding модель, отличную от дефолтной

## Scope

### Multi-collection search
- Новый параметр `collections: string[]` в semantic_search/hybrid_search (опциональный, default = текущая)
- Или отдельный tool `cross_collection_search`
- Merge результатов с source collection в метаданных
- Limit распределяется пропорционально или задаётся per-collection

### Per-collection embedding model
- Новый параметр `embeddingModel` в create_collection (optional, default = global config)
- При add_documents и search использовать модель коллекции, а не глобальную
- Кандидаты: nomic-embed-text для registry/artifact коллекций (лучше для natural language), jina-embeddings-v2-base-code для code коллекций
- EmbeddingProvider поддерживает несколько моделей параллельно (300-500MB per model — несущественно при 64GB+ RAM)

## Design Questions
- Как ранжировать результаты из коллекций с разными embedding spaces? (scores не сравнимы напрямую)
- Нужен ли normalization layer для cross-collection scoring?
- Параметр в существующих tools vs отдельный tool?

## Related
- Context7 epic (tea-rags-mcp-e98j)
- Existing semantic_search / hybrid_search
- EmbeddingProvider interface в contracts/

title: Artifact verification: validate context doc claims against code index
description:
## Problem
Context docs и агенты содержат claims о коде: имена классов, методов, сигнатуры, file paths. Код меняется, claims устаревают. Stale doc хуже отсутствия дока — агент уверенно использует несуществующий API.

## Insight
Tea-rags уже знает актуальное состояние кода (code collection). Проблема не в обнаружении drift, а в том что никто не верифицирует claims артефактов против индекса.

## Solution
Verification pass на уровне плагина (оркестрационный слой, не enrichment):

1. Registry doc содержит key_files и (новое) referenced_symbols
2. При reindex_changes: если файлы из key_files изменились → trigger verification
3. Verification: find_symbol по каждому referenced_symbol из дока
   - Символ не найден → "stale: doc says TwilioSender.send, symbol not found"
   - Сигнатура изменилась → "stale: doc says send(phone, message), code has deliver(recipient, payload)"
   - Файл удалён/перемещён → "stale: doc references app/services/twilio.rb, file not found"
4. Результат: список конкретных расхождений, не абстрактный "driftScore"

## Scope
- Парсинг context docs: извлечение referenced symbols (code blocks, backtick references)
- Verification skill: find_symbol + path checks против code index
- Trigger: hook на reindex_changes когда key_files затронуты
- Output: конкретный diff "doc claims X, code has Y"

## What this is NOT
- Не derived signal в Qdrant
- Не enrichment provider
- Не CI lint скрипт
- Это оркестрационный skill, использующий существующие tea-rags tools

## Related
- Context7 epic (tea-rags-mcp-e98j)
- find_symbol tool (уже существует)
- reindex_changes pipeline (trigger point)
- Registry auto-sync (tea-rags-mcp-rpzq) — prerequisite
close_reason: Merged into 3iv6 (constitution linter). If context docs store only non-code knowledge, drift detection is a linting concern (detect code-level claims that should be live tea-rags queries), not a separate enrichment/verification feature.

title: Bullet bootstrap: seed memory from .claude/ artifacts and code comments
description:
## Problem
При первом подключении ACE7 к проекту memory collection пуста. Нужен bootstrap — первичная генерация bullets из существующих источников знаний.

## Three bootstrap sources

### 1. Existing .claude/ artifacts → bullets
Парсинг существующих context docs, agents, rules:
- .claude/context/*.md — каждая секция Known Failure Modes → bullets type=failure_mode
- .claude/agents/*/AGENT.md — Knowledge Embedding секции → bullets type=decision/anti_pattern
- .claude/rules/*.md — каждое ограничение → bullet type=anti_pattern
- Source: `human` (написаны людьми/factory-агентами)
- helpful/harmful: начинают с 0/0, accumulate через usage

### 2. Code comments → bullets (ongoing, not just bootstrap)
AST-linked extraction при каждом index/reindex:
- Chunker уже знает doc comments (comment-capture hook помечает excludedRows)
- Pattern matching на комментариях внутри chunks:

| Pattern | Regex | Bullet type |
|---------|-------|-------------|
| HACK/FIXME | `//\s*(HACK\|FIXME):?\s*(.+)` | failure_mode |
| WARN/WARNING | `//\s*(WARN\|WARNING):?\s*(.+)` | failure_mode |
| WHY/REASON | `//\s*(WHY\|REASON):?\s*(.+)` | decision |
| NOTE/IMPORTANT | `//\s*(NOTE\|IMPORTANT):?\s*(.+)` | anti_pattern |
| @deprecated | `@deprecated\s+(.+)` | migration |
| TODO with context | `//\s*TODO:?\s*(.+)` | domain_map (potential improvement area) |

- Source: `code_comment`
- resolved_symbols: parent function/class name из AST (уже известен — это chunk owner)
- **Не нужен vector proximity** — прямая привязка через AST parent
- При reindex: chunk перепроцессирован → bullet обновлён если комментарий изменился, удалён если комментарий удалён

### 3. Auto-discovery of knowledge gaps (ongoing, not bootstrap)
→ Отдельная задача (reflector)

## Bootstrap workflow
1. create_collection с bullet schema (зависит от e0tf)
2. Scan .claude/ → parse → generate bullets → dedup check → add_documents
3. Scan code collection → extract comment-pattern bullets → dedup check → add_documents
4. Для каждого bullet: embed description → nearest neighbors в code collection → resolved_symbols
   (кроме code_comment bullets — у них resolved_symbols уже из AST)

## Nuances

### Code comment lifecycle
- Комментарий изменён → bullet content обновлён (не новый bullet, update existing)
- Комментарий удалён → bullet помечен orphaned → pruned при следующем curator pass
- Файл удалён → все bullets из этого файла orphaned
- Matching: bullet привязан к chunk ID, не к строке кода. Chunk ID стабилен при minor edits.

### Dedup at bootstrap
- Между .claude/ bullets и code comment bullets могут быть дубли
  (context doc описывает failure mode, код содержит `// HACK:` о том же)
- Semantic dedup: cosine > 0.95 → merge, сохранить source=human (приоритет)

### Incremental after bootstrap
- Новый .claude/ файл → parse → new bullets (hook на file change в .claude/)
- Новый code comment → extract при reindex_changes → new bullet
- Удалённый .claude/ файл → orphan bullets (но не auto-delete: source=human)

## Related
- ACE7 epic (tea-rags-mcp-e98j)
- Depends on: e0tf (typed collections — bullet schema)
- Existing comment-capture hook in chunker
- Existing reindex_changes pipeline

title: Typed collections: schema validation for add_documents
description:
## Problem
Memory collection хранит bullets с определённой структурой: id, domain, type, source, content, helpful, harmful, resolved_symbols, created, last_confirmed. Без schema validation ничто не гарантирует эту структуру при add_documents.

## Solution
Опциональная JSON Schema при create_collection. При add_documents — валидация против схемы.

## Bullet schema (ACE7)
```json
{
  "id": "string (unique, auto-generated if not provided)",
  "domain": "string (subsystem path: communication/sms, crm/contacts)",
  "type": "enum: failure_mode|decision|anti_pattern|migration|domain_map",
  "source": "enum: auto|human|code_comment",
  "content": "string (one sentence, atomic knowledge unit)",
  "helpful": "number (default 0)",
  "harmful": "number (default 0)",
  "resolved_symbols": "string[] (auto-populated via vector proximity)",
  "created": "ISO date",
  "last_confirmed": "ISO date (last session where bullet was retrieved and confirmed helpful)"
}
```

## Scope
- Новый параметр `schema` в create_collection (optional JSON Schema object)
- Валидация в add_documents при наличии схемы — typed error с описанием нарушения
- Default values: helpful=0, harmful=0, resolved_symbols=[], created=now
- Schema хранится в collection metadata в Qdrant
- Без schema — поведение как сейчас (произвольный JSON)

## Implementation notes
- Использовать Zod (уже в проекте) для runtime validation, не JSON Schema напрямую
- Schema → Zod compile при create_collection, кешировать
- Валидация при add_documents: batch — все документы валидны или весь batch rejected
- Error: конкретное поле + ожидаемый тип + полученное значение

## Related
- ACE7 epic (tea-rags-mcp-e98j)
- Existing create_collection / add_documents в api/public/app.ts

title: find_similar ignores pathPattern negation (!**/dir/**)
description:
## Problem

find_similar does not filter results when pathPattern uses negation syntax (`!**/ingest/**`). Results include paths that should be excluded. Confirmed during risk-assessment skill testing: Pass 1 (no pathPattern) and Pass 2 (`!**/ingest/**`) return identical results with identical scores.

## Context

rank_chunks correctly handles pathPattern negation (picomatch client-side filter). find_similar likely skips the picomatch filter or applies it differently.

## Where to look

- `src/core/domains/explore/strategies/similar.ts` — SimilarStrategy, check if pathPattern filter is applied to results
- `src/core/api/internal/facades/explore-facade.ts` — ExploreFacade.findSimilar(), check if pathPattern is passed through
- Compare with how rank_chunks applies pathPattern in its pipeline

## Expected behavior

`find_similar({ positiveIds: [...], pathPattern: '!**/ingest/**' })` should exclude all results with relativePath matching `**/ingest/**`.

## Impact

risk-assessment skill Phase 3 EXPAND cannot do cross-domain antipattern search via pathPattern exclusion. Currently relies on negativeIds as workaround.
close_reason: Verified: pathPattern negation now works in find_similar. Results correctly exclude matching paths.

title: ACORN filtered search quality (2-hop HNSW traversal)
description: Enable Qdrant 1.16 ACORN for filtered queries. When filters reject >90% of candidates (e.g. language=ruby in mixed repo), standard HNSW degrades to 53% accuracy. ACORN examines neighbors-of-neighbors, restoring accuracy to 97%. tea-rags uses heavy filtering (language, fileExtension, pathPattern, chunkType, git signals). Pass acorn:true in query params — no index changes needed. Consider making it default or auto-enabled when filter selectivity is low. Requires Qdrant ≥1.16.

title: MMR diversity reranking via Qdrant 1.15 server-side MMR
description: Add diversity parameter to search_code/semantic_search using Qdrant MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance). Solves result clustering: currently searches return 5+ chunks from same file instead of diverse codebase coverage. Qdrant MMR iteratively selects results balancing similarity-to-query with dissimilarity-to-already-selected. Parameters: diversity (0.0-1.0), candidates_limit. Especially valuable for onboarding, refactoring presets, and exploratory search. Requires Qdrant ≥1.15.

title: Upgrade Zod 3.x → 4.x
description:
Risk: HIGH. Zod is used throughout for MCP tool schemas (SchemaBuilder), DTOs, and validation.

Breaking changes:
- z.uuid() now strict RFC 4122 — chunk IDs are UUIDs, must verify compatibility
- .strict()/.passthrough() → z.strictObject()/z.looseObject() — grep all usages
- .default() inside .optional() behavior changed — audit all optional+default combos
- String format methods moved to top-level functions — e.g. z.string().email() → z.email()
- Error customization API unified — check custom error messages
- zodToJsonSchema may need updates for MCP schema generation

Approach:
1. Run codemod first: npx @zod/codemod --transform v3-to-v4 --dry-run ./src — assess scope
2. Apply codemod, review changes
3. Manual fixes: UUID validation (z.uuid() → z.guid() if RFC strict breaks), .strict()/.passthrough()
4. Verify SchemaBuilder.generateSchema() produces valid MCP schemas
5. Run build + full test suite
6. Integration test: verify all MCP tools register correctly with updated schemas

Dependencies: consider upgrading openai@6 first (adds Zod v4 compat)
close_reason: committed in d7b0d89

title: ONNX daemon: connect handshake timeout on model reload
description: ONNX fp16 model fails to load: onnxruntime 1.24.3 hangs in loadPipeline after 'Exception during initialization: GetIndexFromName... name does not exist: InsertedPrecisionFreeCast_'. Worker never sends 'ready', client times out. Affects all Node versions (22.14, 24.5). Root cause: onnxruntime graph optimization crash on fp16 model, loadPipeline promise never resolves. This commit also fixes: (1) connect timeout 10s→120s with log keepalive, (2) daemon socket path mismatch (onnx-daemon.sock vs onnx.sock), (3) worker log forwarding to pre-handshake clients.

title: Lazy collection stats recomputation
description:
When a derived signal requests a specific percentile from collectionStats that hasn't been computed yet (field missing from perSignal map), trigger lazy recomputation for that field only.

Current problem: adding new percentiles to PayloadSignalDescriptor.stats requires full reindex (67s) just to recompute stats from already-stored payload data.

Solution: 
1. In ExtractContext or collectionStats accessor, detect missing stats for a declared field
2. Scroll only the needed payload field values from Qdrant
3. Compute requested percentiles
4. Cache in collectionStats.perSignal
5. Return to the requesting signal

This should take 2-3s instead of 67s full reindex.
close_reason: shipped: StatsRecomputeService (lazy-at-rerank), merges 3ee72b44 + d9dfb13e; signal-confidence rule documents it operative/live

title: Grouping API and MMR usage per search level
description:
## Decision

Grouping API is used only at file level (group_size: 1). MMR is a preset property, enabled only for exploration presets (relevance, onboarding).

## Rationale

### Grouping API
- **level: file** → `group_by: "relativePath"`, `group_size: 1` — exactly 1 best chunk per file. This IS the file-level aggregation mechanism. Server-side, faster and more accurate than client-side dedup.
- **level: chunk** → NOT used. Agent/user wants ALL relevant chunks, even multiple from same file. Limiting chunks per file hides real problems.

### MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance)
- MMR is a **preset property** (`mmr: boolean`, default `false`).
- Analytical/rerank presets want ALL results ranked by score — diversity hides real problems (e.g., hiding 3rd hotspot from same file for "diversity").
- Only exploration presets enable MMR:
  - `relevance` → `mmr: true` (semantic exploration, avoid cluster duplication)
  - `onboarding` → `mmr: true` (diverse modules for learning)
  - ALL other presets (techDebt, hotspots, codeReview, refactoring, ownership, securityAudit, recent, stable, decomposition) → `mmr: false`
- Custom weights: `mmr: false` by default, user can pass `mmr: true` as API parameter.

### Key insight
Rerank presets already filter by importance (churn, bugFix, age, etc.) — diversity interferes with analytical ranking. For `relevance` preset, pure semantic similarity causes clustering (10 retry blocks) — MMR spreads results across different areas.

## Alternatives Considered

- **MMR always on**: rejected — destroys analytical preset rankings by hiding real problems for fake diversity
- **MMR per-query parameter only**: rejected — preset knows best whether diversity helps its use case; per-query override still available for custom weights
- **Grouping API at chunk level with high group_size**: rejected — any limit is arbitrary and hides valid results

## Affects

- tea-rags-mcp-35e (Grouping API)
- tea-rags-mcp-93d (MMR)
close_reason: Decision recorded and implemented — grouping at file level done in 35e, MMR closed as won't-do in 93d
